  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the hierarchy record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the hierarchy record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the hierarchy record.']

  Note: 'Defines parent-child corporate hierarchy relationships between customer accounts, enabling roll-up of revenue, design-wins, and engagement metrics across global enterprise accounts. Captures parent account, child account, hierarchy level, relationship type (subsidiary, division, affiliate, distributor branch), effective date, and end date. Critical for global OEM and distributor account management.'
}

Table customer.segment {
  segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the customer segment record. _canonical_skip_reason: Inferred role REFERENCE_LOOKUP; no mandatory minimum categories required.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Market segments (automotive, mobile, IoT) align with profit center reporting structures for P&L analysis, strategic resource allocation, and segment profitability reporting in semiconductor business p']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the segment record was first created.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Default discount percentage offered to the segment.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the segment definition becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the segment definition expires or is retired (null if open‑ended).']
  market_share_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired market share percentage for the segment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes or comments about the segment.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'Pricing model applied to the segment.']
  region varchar [note: 'Primary geographic region associated with the segment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|CHN|JPN|KOR|DEU|FRA|GBR|IND|BRA|AUS — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  revenue_target_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual revenue target for the segment expressed in US dollars.']
  sales_motion varchar [note: 'Primary sales approach used for the segment.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the market segment within the organization.']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the segment purpose and characteristics.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the market segment (e.g., Hyperscaler, Automotive Tier‑1).']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment.']
  strategic_priority_tier varchar [note: 'Internal priority ranking used for resource allocation and go‑to‑market planning.']
  sub_vertical varchar [note: 'More granular market sub‑category within the vertical (e.g., AI Chipmakers, Power Management). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ai_chipmakers|power_management|networking|mobile_devices|automotive_sensors|industrial_automation|defense_systems|smart_home|wearables|edge_computing] ']
  tam_band varchar [note: 'Size band of the total addressable market for the segment.']
  target_customer_type varchar [note: 'Primary type of customers targeted by the segment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the segment record.']
  vertical_market varchar [note: 'High‑level industry vertical to which the segment belongs (e.g., Semiconductor, Automotive, Consumer Electronics). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: semiconductor|automotive|consumer_electronics|industrial_iot|government_defense|healthcare|telecom|energy|finance|retail|education|media] ']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the segment record.']

  Note: 'Reference data defining market segmentation classifications for customer accounts. Each segment represents a strategic grouping used for go-to-market planning, pricing strategy, and resource allocation. Attributes include segment code, segment name (e.g., Hyperscaler, Automotive Tier-1, Consumer Electronics OEM, Industrial IoT, Government/Defense), vertical market, sub-vertical, strategic priority tier, TAM band, and assigned sales motion. Managed by sales strategy and marketing teams as controlled vocabulary.'
}

Table customer.address {
  address_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the address record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Addresses are associated with customer accounts (ship-to, bill-to, HQ). This FK is required for order fulfillment, logistics, and export control screening.']
  address_code varchar [note: 'Internal code used to reference the address within ERP and PLM systems.']
  address_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the address record.']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Category of the address indicating its business purpose.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description for the most recent address change.']
  change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the most recent address change was recorded.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the address.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Classification indicating whether the address belongs to internal organization, external customer, or partner.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact at the address.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person at the address.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact at the address.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 3166‑1 country code.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the address record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the address becomes valid for business use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the address ceases to be valid (null if open‑ended).']
  export_control_screened boolean [note: 'True if the address has been screened against export control lists.']
  export_control_status varchar [note: 'Result of export‑control screening for the address.']
  geolocation_accuracy varchar [note: 'Indicates the precision level of the latitude/longitude values.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this address is the primary location for the associated account.']
  label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable label identifying the address (e.g., “HQ San Jose”).']
  last_verified date [note: 'Date when the address information was last validated.']
  latitude double [note: 'Latitude coordinate in decimal degrees.']
  lineage_code varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this address to its predecessor in case of address merges or splits.']
  longitude double [note: 'Longitude coordinate in decimal degrees.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks about the address.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the address.']
  purpose varchar [note: 'Business purpose for which the address is used.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region (e.g., APAC, EMEA, AMER) used for reporting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system where the address originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Salesforce CRM).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the address.']
  street_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the street address.']
  street_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the street address (optional).']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction applicable to the address.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time‑zone identifier for the address location.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the address record.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of address verification.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that created the record.']

  Note: 'Physical and mailing addresses associated with customer accounts and contacts, including headquarters, ship-to, bill-to, and design center locations. Captures address type, street lines, city, state/province, postal code, country (ISO 3166), region, time zone, and geolocation coordinates. Supports multi-site global accounts with multiple address records per account. Used for logistics, tax jurisdiction, and export control screening.'
}

Table customer.customer_design_win {
  customer_design_win_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the customer design win record.']
  channel_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distributor channel partner, if the win originated via a distributor.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.contact.contact_id — Design-wins reference the FAE owner and customer engineering contact. Contact FK enables relationship tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Design wins incur engineering support costs (FAE time, design tools, validation) tracked to specific cost centers for project accounting, resource planning, and design win ROI analysis.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Every design-win is attributed to a specific customer account. This FK is critical for revenue forecasting and account-level reporting.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Each design win part must have an ECCN classification for export control; used in export license generation and customs documentation.']
  flow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_flow. Business justification: A design win is committed to a specific process flow for yield forecasting, capacity planning, and NRE scoping. The existing \'process_node\' text field is a denormalization of process_flow data. Semico']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Design‑win forecasts and revenue reports must reference the exact catalog entry; part_number is a denormalized duplicate and is removed.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Design wins specify exact materials/components (wafers, substrates, packaging materials) for production. Links to material master for BOM planning, cost estimation, supply chain readiness, and product']
  primary_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer organization that owns the design win.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Design win financial tracking attributes revenue to a profit center for margin analysis; appears in Design Win Profitability report.']
  reach_svhc_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.reach_svhc_declaration. Business justification: Design win parts require REACH SVHC declarations for EU chemical compliance; needed for regulatory filing and product safety reporting.']
  sourcing_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.sourcing_contract. Business justification: Design wins often trigger or reference specific sourcing contracts for long-term component supply. Links to sourcing contract for supply assurance, contract compliance tracking, revenue-to-supply alig']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Design win must identify the supplier manufacturing the part, essential for supply chain planning and production ramp scheduling.']
  target_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: Design wins specify target fabrication equipment for production planning. Critical for design-to-manufacturing handoff, capacity allocation, tool qualification requirements, and production ramp schedu']
  technology_control_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.technology_control_plan. Business justification: Design wins involving controlled technology (advanced process nodes, cryptographic IP, military-grade components) must reference applicable technology control plans. Sales and compliance teams verify ']
  competitive_displacement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the win displaces a competitor\'s product (true) or not (false).']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the design win was officially confirmed (won).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  design_project_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal design project associated with the win.']
  design_win_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the design win record.']
  design_win_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the design win (internal development, channel‑sourced, or partner‑sourced).']
  estimated_annual_revenue_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected annual revenue from the design win, expressed in US dollars.']
  estimated_annual_unit_volume varchar [note: 'Projected number of units the customer will purchase per year.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the FAE or sales team.']
  nre_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated NRE cost associated with the design win, expressed in US dollars.']
  nre_required_flag boolean [note: 'True if the project includes a non‑recurring engineering (NRE) component.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Package format chosen for the part (e.g., BGA, WLCSP).']
  platform_generation varchar [note: 'Generation of the platform family for which the design win is targeted.']
  production_ramp_date date [note: 'Target date when volume production is expected to ramp.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this record.']
  registration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the design win was first registered in the system.']
  stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the design‑win lifecycle.']
  tapeout_target_date date [note: 'Planned date for tape‑out of the design.']
  target_application varchar [note: 'Intended end‑product or market application (e.g., smartphone SoC, ADAS ECU, AI accelerator).']

  Note: 'Unified record tracking the full design-in lifecycle from initial registration through confirmed design-win for semiconductor products selected by customers. SSOT for the complete design-in pipeline from early registration through revenue forecasting — distinct from sales-domain opportunity records which track revenue probability and sales stage. Captures design project name, target end product/application (e.g., smartphone SoC, ADAS ECU, AI accelerator), platform generation, process node, package type, device/part number selected, design-win stage (registered, evaluating, sampling, won, lost, on-hold), registration date, design-win confirmation date, tapeout target date, production ramp date, estimated annual unit volume (AUV), estimated annual revenue, competitive displacement flag, associated distributor (if channel-sourced), FAE owner, and NRE requirements. Integrates with Salesforce CRM Opportunity.'
}

Table customer.customer_design_registration {
  customer_design_registration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each customer design registration record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer owning the design project.']
  channel_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distributor (if any) associated with the registration.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: A design registration is initiated and owned by a specific design engineer or technical contact at the customer account. The customer_design_win table already carries contact_id, and the same pattern ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Design registrations consume engineering resources (FAE support, EDA tools, reference designs) charged to cost centers for activity-based costing and engineering capacity planning.']
  customer_design_win_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_design_win. Business justification: In the semiconductor design-in lifecycle, a design registration is the formal precursor to a confirmed design win. Linking customer_design_registration to customer_design_win enables full traceability']
  flow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_flow. Business justification: Design registration specifies which process flow the customer\'s design will be fabricated on — essential for tapeout scheduling, DFM rule application, and NRE budgeting. The existing \'process_node_nm\'']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Design registration validation uses the catalog to verify process node, package, and qualification; part_number is redundant and removed.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Design registrations specify target materials/packages during early design phase. Links to material master for early supply chain engagement, material qualification planning, and lead-time assessment.']
  technology_control_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.technology_control_plan. Business justification: Design registrations for advanced process nodes (sub-14nm) or controlled IP blocks require technology control plan coverage per EAR/ITAR. Export compliance officers must verify TCP applicability befor']
  validation_test_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.test_program. Business justification: Design registrations specify validation test programs for engineering samples and characterization. Design-in process requires linking customer projects to test programs for silicon validation, yield ']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the registration record was first created.']
  design_complexity varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of design difficulty.']
  design_project_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the design objectives, features, and scope.']
  design_project_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the customer’s design project.']
  design_start_date date [note: 'Date when the customer officially started the design effort.']
  expected_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected percentage of good dies per wafer for the design.']
  nre_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Non‑Recurring Engineering budget allocated for the design project.']
  nre_budget_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the NRE budget amount.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Physical package style for the final silicon die.']
  platform_generation varchar [note: 'Generation of the semiconductor platform (e.g., next‑gen, legacy) targeted by the design.']
  production_target_date date [note: 'Target date for first silicon production run.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date when the qualification status was determined.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Result of the customer design qualification review.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the design registration, used for tracking and external communication.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the design registration.']
  tapeout_target_date date [note: 'Planned date for the final mask set release (tape‑out).']
  target_application varchar [note: 'Primary market or end‑use application for which the design is intended.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the registration record.']

  Note: 'Formal registration of a customer design project that uses a specific semiconductor product, capturing the design project name, target application, platform generation, process node, package type, design start date, tapeout target date, production target date, design registration status, and associated distributor (if applicable). Enables early pipeline visibility and NRE planning. Precedes and feeds into design_win records.'
}

  required_tool_qualification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.tool_qualification. Table customer.nda_agreement {
  nda_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the NDA agreement record.']

  renewal_notice_date date [note: 'Date on which a renewal notice must be sent to the customer before expiry.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the technology, roadmap, pricing, or other information covered by the NDA.']
  signatory_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the signatory used for correspondence and notifications.']

  signatory_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number of the signatory.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for early termination of the NDA, if applicable.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the NDA record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the NDA record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the NDA when amendments are made.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the NDA record.']

  Note: 'Non-Disclosure Agreement records between the semiconductor company and customer accounts, capturing NDA type (mutual, one-way), execution date, expiry date, covered scope (product roadmap, process technology, pricing), signatory contacts, legal entity names, NDA status (active, expired, under renewal), and document reference ID. Required before sharing roadmap, PDK, or pre-production device information with customers.'
}

Table customer.distributor_agreement {
  distributor_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the distributor agreement record.']
  channel_partner_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the distributor party associated with this agreement.']

  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Distributor agreements define channel economics (discount rates, MDF, price protection) that impact profit center P&L, margin analysis, and channel profitability reporting by business unit.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External contract number assigned by the company to identify the agreement.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the agreement such as distribution, exclusive, or co-marketing.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the agreement received final approval.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the agreement.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'List of regulatory or industry compliance obligations attached to the agreement.']
  contract_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored electronic copy of the signed agreement.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency used for all monetary amounts in the agreement.']
  discount_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard discount percentage applied to the distributor\'s purchases.']
  distributor_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the agreement.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the agreement expires or is terminated; null for open‑ended contracts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the agreement becomes legally binding.']
  exclusive_distribution boolean [note: 'True if the distributor has exclusive rights for the territory/product line.']
  mdf_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code for the MDF entitlement.']
  mdf_entitlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of Marketing Development Fund allocated to the distributor.']
  mdf_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the MDF entitlement expires if not used.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum number of units the distributor must order per transaction.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or remarks.']
  price_protection_terms varchar [note: 'Textual description of price protection clauses (e.g., minimum advertised price).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agreement record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the agreement record.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of required regulatory approvals for the agreement.']
  renewal_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the distributor must be notified before automatic renewal.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the agreement renews automatically, manually, or not at all.']
  stock_rotation_rights boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the distributor has rights to rotate stock to maintain freshness.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to give notice before terminating the agreement.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for agreement termination, if applicable.']
  territory_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the geographic territory covered.']
  tier varchar [note: 'Level of the distributor based on company‑defined criteria.']

  Note: 'Master records of distribution agreements between the semiconductor company and authorized distributor accounts, capturing distributor tier (authorized, preferred, elite), geographic territory coverage, product line authorizations, stock rotation rights, price protection terms, marketing development fund (MDF) entitlements, agreement effective date, expiry date, and agreement status. Distinct from direct customer accounts — distributors resell to end customers.'
}

Table customer.credit_profile {
  credit_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the credit profile record.']
  primary_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Credit profiles are per-account. Must reference the account for order-to-cash credit checks.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the credit profile is subject to any regulatory hold or review.']
  credit_analyst_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the credit analyst.']
  credit_hold boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer\'s credit is currently on hold.']
  credit_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for placing the customer\'s credit on hold.']
  credit_limit_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount by which the credit limit was adjusted.']
  credit_limit_adjustment_date date [note: 'Date when the credit limit adjustment took effect.']
  credit_limit_adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the most recent credit limit change.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount approved for the customer.']
  credit_limit_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the credit limit (ISO 4217 three‑letter code).']
  credit_limit_effective_from date [note: 'Date when the current credit limit became effective.']
  credit_limit_effective_until date [note: 'Date when the current credit limit expires (null if open‑ended).']
  credit_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the credit profile.']
  credit_profile_type varchar [note: 'Category of the credit profile based on internal risk segmentation.']
  credit_rating_external varchar [note: 'External credit rating from a third‑party agency.']
  credit_rating_internal varchar [note: 'Internal credit rating assigned by the company.']
  credit_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent credit review.']
  credit_review_notes varchar [note: 'Analyst comments from the latest credit review.']
  credit_review_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent credit review.']
  credit_score varchar [note: 'Numerical credit score from an external scoring model.']
  credit_score_date date [note: 'Date when the credit score was obtained.']
  credit_score_source varchar [note: 'Source of the credit score (e.g., Equifax, Experian).']
  credit_utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the credit limit currently utilized.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency used for all monetary fields (ISO 4217).']
  external_rating_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the external agency providing the credit rating.']
  is_preferred_customer boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is marked as a preferred partner.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the credit profile.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current unpaid balance owed by the customer.']
  overdue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount past due beyond the agreed payment terms.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the customer.']
  preferred_customer_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for preferred customer status.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact for credit communications.']
  profile_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the credit profile (e.g., "Standard Credit Profile").']
  rating_date date [note: 'Date when the external credit rating was issued.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credit profile record was first created.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification derived from credit metrics.']
  status_date date [note: 'Date when the credit profile status last changed.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the credit profile record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the credit profile record.']

  Note: 'Customer-facing credit and financial risk profile maintained by the customer domain as SSOT for customer creditworthiness assessment. Captures credit limit, credit rating (internal and external Dun & Bradstreet), payment terms (Net 30, Net 60, prepay), credit hold status, credit review date, outstanding balance, overdue amount, credit utilization percentage, currency, and credit analyst notes. Distinct from finance-domain AR aging and collections records — this product owns the customer\'s credit posture used for real-time order-to-cash credit checks. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA FI-AR (Accounts Receivable).'
}

Table customer.sample_request {
  sample_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sample request record.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.contact.contact_id — Sample requests are submitted by a specific contact (design engineer). FK enables follow-up and design-in tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Sample request costs are charged to an internal cost center; required for Sample Cost Allocation reporting.']
  customer_design_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_design_registration. Business justification: In semiconductor sales, engineering sample requests are almost always tied to a specific design project or design registration. A customer requests samples to evaluate a part for a registered design. ']
  die_bank_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.die_bank. Business justification: Sample requests are often fulfilled from die bank inventory for engineering samples before final packaging. Essential for tracking die consumption for customer evaluation and linking sample requests t']
  finished_good_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.finished_good. Business justification: Sample requests must track which finished goods inventory was allocated and shipped to fulfill the request. Critical for sample inventory management, cost tracking, and reconciling sample consumption ']
  fulfillment_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: Engineering sample requests specify which fab tool/line to use for prototype production. Critical for sample fulfillment planning, tool availability checking, process capability matching, and ensuring']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Sample requests must specify which IC product is being requested for evaluation. Fulfillment process requires linking request to IC catalog entry to validate availability, export controls, and sample ']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Sample requests generate inspection lots for quality validation before shipment to customers. Links sample fulfillment to quality inspection results for traceability, customer complaint investigation,']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Sample fulfillment may be executed via an internal order to capture labor/material spend; needed for Internal Order Cost Tracking.']
  lot_process_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.lot_process_run. Business justification: A customer sample request is fulfilled by executing a specific lot process run. Semiconductor customer service and fab operations teams track which lot run produced the samples shipped to a customer —']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Sample requests often specify raw materials or components (wafers, substrates, packaging materials) needed for prototyping and customer evaluation. Links to material master for sample fulfillment, inv']
  primary_sample_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Sample requests originate from a customer account. Required for funnel tracking and account-level engagement metrics.']
  shipping_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Normalize shipping address details into address table, reducing redundancy.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Samples are fulfilled at SKU level (specific orderable part number with package/speed/voltage variant), not just IC catalog. Real-world sample requests specify exact MPN; fulfillment systems pick inve']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Sample request is sent to a specific supplier; linking enables tracking of sample fulfillment and supplier performance.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Sample programs tracked as projects with WBS elements for cost recovery, program management, and evaluation of sample-to-production conversion rates in semiconductor customer acquisition.']
  application_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the customer’s intended application for the sample.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the request was approved.']
  cost_adjustment decimal(12,2) [note: 'Any additional charge or discount applied to the gross cost (e.g., tax, handling fee).']
  cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base cost of the sample before any adjustments.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the sample cost.']
  cost_net decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final cost after adjustments; amount to be billed to the customer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the request record was first created in the system.']
  customer_sample_request_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the sample request.']
  delivery_requested_date date [note: 'Date by which the customer expects the sample to be delivered.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of sample fulfillment and delivery.']
  fulfillment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the fulfillment status last changed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the requestor or processing team.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units requested for the sample.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable request number assigned by the system.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the request was submitted by the customer.']
  sample_purpose varchar [note: 'Intended use of the sample (e.g., evaluation, design‑in, qualification, testing).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the request record.']

  Note: 'Customer requests for engineering samples, evaluation kits, and pre-production devices prior to volume purchase. Captures requesting contact, account, part number requested, quantity, application description, sample purpose (evaluation, design-in, qualification), requested delivery date, shipping address, approval status, approving FAE, sample cost (if charged), and fulfillment status. Tracks the early-stage design-in funnel from sample to design registration to design-win.'
}

Table customer.price_agreement {
  price_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the price agreement record.']
  distributor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.distributor_agreement. Business justification: Price agreements (SPAs — Special Price Authorizations) in the semiconductor industry are frequently negotiated under the umbrella of a master distributor agreement. Linking price_agreement to distribu']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue posting for each price agreement requires a GL account; used in the Revenue Recognition report.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Price agreements are contractually bound to a catalog item to ensure correct pricing and compliance; part_number is a denormalized duplicate.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Price agreements are legal contracts executed by specific legal entities; required for contract management, intercompany pricing compliance, and transfer pricing documentation in global semiconductor ']
  price_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Customer-specific pricing is negotiated per account. FK to account is mandatory for pricing lookups during order entry.']
  primary_price_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer to which the price agreement applies.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Price agreements impact profit center revenue and margin; essential for pricing analytics, variance analysis, and profitability reporting by business unit in semiconductor pricing management.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote. Business justification: Price agreements in semiconductor sales often reference the originating quote that established negotiated terms. Required for pricing audit trails, compliance verification, and tracking quote-to-agree']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Pricing is negotiated and tracked at SKU level (specific orderable MPN with package/speed/voltage variants), not just IC catalog. Real-world price agreements specify exact manufacturer part numbers. C']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the price agreement, used in contracts and communications.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the price agreement indicating its purpose or pricing model.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the price agreement was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the internal approval workflow for the price agreement.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the person who approved the agreement.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Special Price Authorization or contract linked to this agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price agreement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the agreed price.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied on top of the base unit price.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the price agreement becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the price agreement expires; null for open‑ended agreements.']
  is_price_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the price agreement is locked from further changes.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier that performed the latest update.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered to qualify for the agreed price.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or internal comments related to the agreement.']
  part_revision varchar [note: 'Specific revision or mask set of the part covered by the agreement.']
  price_agreement_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose, scope, or special conditions of the agreement.']
  price_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the price agreement.']
  price_change_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the approval workflow instance for a price change.']
  price_change_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which a price amendment becomes effective.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative reason for a price amendment or re‑negotiation.']
  price_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the price data (e.g., SAP SD pricing condition, manual entry, external feed).']
  price_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the price (e.g., per die, per wafer, per lot).']
  pricing_channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel through which the price is offered.']
  pricing_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region to which the pricing terms apply.']
  tax_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether taxes are already included in the unit price.']
  tier_end_quantity varchar [note: 'Inclusive upper bound of quantity for this volume tier; null if no upper limit.']
  tier_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed unit price applicable for the defined volume tier.']
  tier_start_quantity varchar [note: 'Inclusive lower bound of quantity for this volume tier.']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed price per unit of the part, expressed in the specified currency.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the price agreement record.']
  volume_tier varchar [note: 'Label for the volume‑based pricing tier.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier that created the price agreement record.']

  Note: 'Customer-specific pricing agreements and special price authorizations (SPAs) negotiated for specific part numbers. SSOT for customer-negotiated prices distinct from sales-domain standard list pricing and discount schedules. Captures agreed unit price, currency, minimum order quantity (MOQ), volume tiers, effective date, expiry date, approval chain, SPA reference number, and associated design-win or contract. These are customer-committed prices that flow into order entry — not sales pipeline pricing assumptions. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA SD pricing conditions.'
}

Table sales.opportunity {
  opportunity_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the sales opportunity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer account linked to the opportunity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing or design‑win campaign linked to the opportunity.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Opportunity Management process requires a primary contact person; linking to customer.contact enables accurate follow‑up and reporting.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Opportunities need ECCN classification to identify export restrictions, assess destination country eligibility, and incorporate compliance costs/risks into pricing models and probability scoring in se']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Opportunities for export-controlled semiconductors must reference applicable export licenses to qualify deal feasibility, assess license capacity, and incorporate compliance lead times into expected c']
  fab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fab_facility. Business justification: Opportunities specify target fab for capacity/capability matching and export compliance (ITAR, EAR). Sales qualification checks facility availability, technology readiness, and regulatory clearance. E']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: Foundry and custom silicon opportunities require qualification against specific fab tool capabilities (process node compatibility, capacity). Sales engineers must verify tool availability during oppor']
  fabrication_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_technology_node. Business justification: Opportunities target specific process nodes (7nm, 28nm, etc.) for customer designs. Sales qualification process requires checking node availability, capacity, and export compliance. Essential for oppo']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_family. Business justification: Opportunity pipeline dashboards aggregate by product family to forecast revenue.']
  flow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_flow. Business justification: Opportunity cost estimation uses a specific process flow; linking enables accurate BOM, yield, and cycle‑time forecasts.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Opportunities target specific IC catalog items for technical qualification and pricing. Technical sales teams track exact part numbers being designed into customer products for accurate forecasting an']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Opportunity tracking for a specific chip design project is required for pipeline reporting and resource planning.']
  quality_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_spec. Business justification: Opportunities for advanced/custom chips reference quality specifications (reliability grades, process tolerances, automotive/aerospace standards) during technical qualification and customer negotiatio']
  sourcing_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.sourcing_contract. Business justification: High-volume opportunities reference existing supply agreements for cost modeling, margin analysis, and deal structuring. Essential for validating pricing and delivery commitments against contracted te']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Opportunities for hub/consignment programs identify target storage locations for customer-managed inventory. Semiconductor sales teams negotiate VMI (vendor-managed inventory) arrangements where stora']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Semiconductor opportunities specify preferred/qualified suppliers (foundry, OSAT) for custom silicon or packaging. Critical for RFQ routing, supplier selection during design-in phase, and feasibility ']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Opportunities are owned within a sales territory; FK replaces free‑text field for data integrity.']
  competitive_landscape varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of key competitors and market positioning for the opportunity.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Planned end or expiration date of the contract; nullable for open‑ended agreements.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the contract if the opportunity is won.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contract.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the contractual agreement associated with the opportunity.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code of the primary customer location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the opportunity record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary amounts (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  end_market varchar [note: 'Primary market segment the opportunity addresses.']
  expected_close_date date [note: 'Projected date when the opportunity is expected to be closed (won or lost).']
  expected_discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated discount or rebate amount applied to the gross revenue.']
  expected_gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected gross revenue before discounts, taxes, or adjustments.']
  expected_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected net revenue after discounts and adjustments.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the opportunity record.']
  nre_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Potential NRE revenue associated with custom design or engineering services.']
  opportunity_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the opportunity, often reflecting the target product or project.']
  opportunity_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier assigned to the opportunity (e.g., OPP‑2024‑00123).']
  price_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Unit price used for revenue calculations, expressed in the selected currency.']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'Pricing structure applied to the opportunity.']
  probability_percent varchar [note: 'Estimated probability (0‑100) that the opportunity will be won, based on stage and historical data.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region for the opportunity.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the sale is pursued.']
  stage varchar [note: 'Current pipeline stage of the opportunity.']
  stage_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the opportunity last moved to its current stage.']
  target_application varchar [note: 'Intended end‑use or application for the product (e.g., automotive ADAS, AI accelerator).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity (e.g., units, chips).']
  win_loss_date date [note: 'Date on which the opportunity outcome (win or loss) was recorded.']
  win_loss_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative reason why the opportunity was won or lost.']

  Note: 'Master record: Core sales opportunity tracking design-win campaigns, pipeline stages (Prospecting→Design-In→Design-Win→Production Ramp→Won/Lost), probability, and expected revenue for semiconductor IC, SoC, ASIC, and FPGA engagements. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Opportunity object. Captures end market (automotive, mobile, AI, IoT, computing), product family, target application, competitive landscape, NRE potential, design-win status, campaign grouping (campaign type, target segment, effectiveness metrics), and stage transition history for pipeline velocity analysis. SSOT for all sales pipeline activity including campaign-level aggregation for design-in programs, product launches, and competitive displacement initiatives.'
}

Table sales.quote {
  quote_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the quote record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer or prospect receiving the quote.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Quote generation must record the exact customer contact authorizing the quote; this FK supports compliance and audit trails.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Quotes require ECCN classification to determine export restrictions, calculate compliance handling fees, and include regulatory disclaimers for controlled semiconductor products in customer-facing pri']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Quotes for export-controlled semiconductors must reference valid export licenses to provide accurate lead times, delivery terms, and compliance disclaimers, ensuring quoted terms are legally executabl']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: Foundry service quotes specify which fab tool will execute the work for capacity reservation and lead time calculation. Operations planning requires knowing tool allocation at quote stage to commit de']
  fabrication_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_technology_node. Business justification: Quotes specify process node for pricing, lead-time, and feasibility assessment. Quote approval workflow requires node capacity check and yield assumption validation. Critical for quote-to-order conver']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_family. Business justification: Quote header must be tied to a product family for contract and pricing governance.']
  flow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_flow. Business justification: Quotes are generated based on a defined process flow; the link supports pricing, lead‑time, and qualification status.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Quotes reference specific IC catalog items for precise pricing, lead times, compliance documentation, and technical specifications. Quote generation requires exact part-level detail beyond family for ']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Quotes are generated for design projects; linking enables financial forecasting of design spend.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.opportunity.opportunity_id — Every quote is generated in context of an opportunity — this is the core pipeline-to-commercial conversion link. Engineers need this to trace quote→opportunity for pipeline reporting.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.price_list.price_list_id — Quotes reference the applicable price list for base pricing. Essential for pricing compliance and margin analysis.']
  quality_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_spec. Business justification: Quotes for semiconductor products must reference applicable quality specifications (JEDEC reliability, automotive IATF-16949, KGD certification requirements) to price correctly, set lead times, and es']
  sourcing_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.sourcing_contract. Business justification: Quotes leverage existing supply contracts for pricing accuracy and terms compliance, especially for materials and OSAT services. Critical for margin calculation and contract adherence in semiconductor']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Quotes for packaged/tested semiconductors reference the OSAT or material supplier for accurate lead time and cost. Required for quote generation, supply chain feasibility checks, and customer delivery']
  conversion_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quote was converted to an order, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quote record was first created in the system.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the quote amounts.']
  delivery_terms varchar [note: 'Additional delivery conditions beyond Incoterms, such as FOB destination or DAP warehouse.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to the gross amount.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms defining delivery responsibilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|FOB|CFR|CIF|DAP|DDP — promote to reference product]']
  is_converted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quote has been converted to a sales order.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Estimated number of calendar days from order receipt to delivery.']
  net_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final amount payable after discount and tax.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition attached to the quote.']
  quote_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quote was issued to the customer.']
  quote_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the scope, special conditions, or notes for the quote.']
  quote_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier for the quotation, often used in communications and tracking.']
  quote_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the quote.']
  reason_lost varchar [note: 'Textual reason provided when a quote is marked as lost.']
  sales_region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code representing the primary sales region for the quote.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Tax calculated on the gross amount after discount.']
  total_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quoted amount before discounts, taxes, or adjustments.']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per individual unit for the quoted product at the selected volume tier.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the quote record.']
  valid_until date [note: 'Date after which the quote is no longer valid.']
  volume_tier varchar [note: 'Quantity bracket that determines unit pricing.']
  win_loss_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the quote after the decision period.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date from which the quoted terms become effective.']

  Note: 'Transaction record: Commercial price quotation issued to a customer or prospect for semiconductor products including ICs, packaged devices, wafer-level products, and IP licensing. Captures quoted unit price, volume tiers, lead time, validity period, incoterms, currency, and SAP SD quotation reference. Tracks quote-to-order conversion and win/loss outcomes.'
}

Table sales.quote_line {
  quote_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quote line record.']
  design_ip_core_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ip_core. Business justification: Quote lines often represent IP core licensing; linking tracks IP revenue and compliance.']
  fabrication_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_technology_node. Business justification: Quote lines specify technology node for accurate pricing, lead-time calculation, and yield assumptions. Quote generation system pulls node-specific cost structures and capacity constraints. Critical f']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_family. Business justification: Enables quote‑level reporting and margin analysis by product family.']
  finished_good_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.finished_good. Business justification: Quote line pricing and availability require linking to the specific finished good inventory record for SKU, enabling real-time stock checks during quoting.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Quote lines itemize specific IC catalog products with exact pricing, packaging options, and delivery terms. Line-item quoting requires catalog-level detail including part number, revision, and qualifi']
  price_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.price_agreement. Business justification: Quote lines must reference negotiated price agreements for specific parts and volume tiers. Semiconductor pricing involves complex tiered agreements that quote lines must honor for compliance, margin ']
  primary_quote_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent sales quote to which this line belongs.']
  raw_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.raw_material. Business justification: Quotes for wafer starts or foundry services may specify customer-supplied materials (e.g., epitaxial wafers, SOI substrates, specialty materials). Tracking customer-furnished raw materials in quote li']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Required for accurate pricing, inventory and compliance tracking in quote lines.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Quote lines specify the supplier (foundry, OSAT) for each SKU to ensure accurate lead time, cost, and capacity availability. Essential for line-level supply chain validation and customer commitments.']
  tertiary_quote_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.quote.quote_id — Header-detail relationship: quote_line cannot exist without its parent quote. Critical for quote value calculation and line-level pricing analysis.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center to which the line\'s revenue is allocated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quote line was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the quote (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  customer_requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the customer expects delivery of the quoted items.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the unit price.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the quoted terms become effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the quoted terms are no longer valid.']
  internal_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of internal sales or finance approval for the line.']
  is_custom boolean [note: 'True if the quoted SKU is a custom or engineered product.']
  is_price_locked boolean [note: 'True if the quoted price is locked and cannot be changed without re‑quote.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Estimated number of calendar days from order to shipment.']
  line_comment varchar [note: 'Free‑form comment entered by the sales rep for this line.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the quote for ordering.']
  net_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Unit price after discount, before tax.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Physical packaging of the die (e.g., BGA, QFN, WLCSP).']
  pricing_tier varchar [note: 'Pricing tier classification that determines discount levels.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of the product requested in this line.']
  quote_line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quote line.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the sale is pursued.']
  sales_region varchar [note: 'Geographic sales region associated with the quote.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the quote line (e.g., SAP, Salesforce).']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Any customer‑specific handling, packaging, or shipping notes.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary tax calculated for this line.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a percentage.']
  total_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Gross amount for the line (unit_price * quantity) before tax.']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Base price per unit before discounts, in the quote currency.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for quantity, typically \'pcs\' for pieces.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the quote line.']
  warranty_years varchar [note: 'Number of years of warranty offered for the product.']

  Note: 'Detail record: Individual line item within a sales quotation specifying a single semiconductor SKU, die, or IP core with its quoted quantity, unit price, discount, package type, lead time, and applicable pricing tier. Supports multi-line quotes covering mixed product families and end-market configurations.'
}
