  fabrication_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_technology_node. Business justification: Design wins lock in specific process node for production. Essential for capacity planning, revenue forecasting, and fab loading models. S&OP process requires node-level design win visibility for multi']
  finished_good_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.finished_good. Business justification: Design win commits to manufacturing a specific finished good; linking design win to the finished‑good record ties sales commitment to inventory production planning.']
  flow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_flow. Business justification: Design win records the process flow used for the winning design, required for revenue attribution and capacity planning.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Design wins track which specific IC catalog item won the socket at customer. Revenue forecasting, competitive displacement tracking, and production planning require exact part number identification be']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Design wins track which specific IC design project won the customer engagement. Critical for revenue attribution, project ROI analysis, and design reuse decisions. Semiconductor sales teams must link ']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Design wins represent future revenue commitments that must be tracked by legal entity for transfer pricing planning, tax optimization, and statutory revenue forecasting.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.opportunity.opportunity_id — A design win is the successful outcome of an opportunity. This link is critical for pipeline conversion metrics and is the most important FK in semiconductor sales.']
  primary_sales_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who selected the semiconductor device.']
  primary_sales_sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the semiconductor device that won the design.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Design wins drive future revenue streams that must be allocated to profit centers for business unit forecasting, capacity planning, and performance tracking against design win pipeline targets.']
  qualification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_qualification. Business justification: A semiconductor design win is commercially valid only when the process is qualified. Revenue ramp forecasting and design win reporting require linking to the process_qualification record. Sales ops an']
  quality_qualification_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_qualification_program. Business justification: Design wins for automotive, aerospace, or industrial applications trigger or reference qualification programs (IATF-16949, AEC-Q100, MIL-STD) as contractual deliverables. Real process: design win clos']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales quote associated with the design win.']
  sales_nre_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Non-Recurring Engineering agreement linked to the design win.']
  sourcing_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.sourcing_contract. Business justification: Design wins reference the supply contract governing production terms, pricing, and delivery. Critical for margin tracking, revenue forecasting, and ensuring supply chain commitments align with custome']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Design wins track the qualified supplier (fab, OSAT) fulfilling production for supply chain planning, capacity reservation, and revenue forecasting. Core to semiconductor design-in and production ramp']
  competitor_displacement_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0-100) representing the impact of displacing the competitor.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework applicable to the design win. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ITAR|EAR|RoHS|REACH|ISO9001|ISO14001|ISO45001 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the design win record.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Contract end date.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Contract start date.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Length of the contract in months.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the design win record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for revenue amounts.']
  design_win_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the design win record.']
  displaced_competitor_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the competitor whose device was displaced.']
  displaced_device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the competitor\'s device that was displaced.']
  displacement_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the competitor\'s device was displaced.']
  estimated_annual_revenue_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected gross revenue per year from the design win before discounts or adjustments.']
  estimated_annual_revenue_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected net revenue after adjustments.']
  estimated_annual_unit_volume varchar [note: 'Projected number of units the customer will purchase per year.']
  export_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates if the device is subject to export control regulations.']
  forecast_accuracy decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage indicating forecast accuracy of the revenue estimate.']
  forecasted_ramp_rate_per_month decimal(10,2) [note: 'Projected increase in unit volume per month during the ramp period.']
  is_key_account boolean [note: 'Indicates if the customer is a strategic key account.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the design win was last reviewed for compliance or forecast updates.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Industry segment of the end product.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes about the design win.']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'Pricing approach applied to the design win.']
  region varchar [note: 'Global region of the customer.']
  revenue_adjustment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any discount, rebate, or adjustment applied to the gross revenue.']
  revenue_ramp_end_date date [note: 'End date of the revenue ramp period.']
  revenue_ramp_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the revenue ramp period.']
  sales_design_win_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the design win.']
  sales_territory varchar [note: 'Geographic or market territory responsible for the design win.']
  target_application varchar [note: 'End product or application where the winning device will be used (e.g., smartphone, automotive ECU).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the design win record.']
  win_source varchar [note: 'System or source where the design win was recorded.']
  win_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the design win was officially confirmed.']

  Note: 'Transaction record: Confirmed design-win record capturing the moment a customer selects a semiconductor device for integration into their end product (PCB, module, or SoC platform). Records the winning device, customer design reference, target application, estimated annual unit volume (AUV), revenue ramp timeline, and competitive displacement details (displaced competitor, displaced device, displacement reason). Critical KPI for semiconductor commercial teams. Represents the key conversion event from opportunity to committed design.'
}

Table sales.sales_nre_agreement {
  sales_nre_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the NRE agreement record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer party for whom the NRE work is performed.']
  chips_act_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.chips_act_obligation. Business justification: NRE agreements may be subject to CHIPS Act obligations; linking records the specific obligation tied to each agreement.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: NRE engineering projects must be allocated to cost centers for tracking R&D expenses, project accounting, and cost recovery against customer payments. Essential for project P&L and resource allocation']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: NRE agreements involving technology transfer, design IP, or controlled process nodes require export license references to ensure ITAR/EAR compliance for deemed exports and international customer engag']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_family. Business justification: NRE agreements are allocated to product families for cost recovery and reporting.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: NRE agreements fund development of specific IC catalog products (custom ASICs, process variants). Milestone tracking and deliverable acceptance are tied to specific product development programs, not j']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: NRE agreements fund specific IC design projects. Essential for cost recovery tracking, milestone billing validation, and project budget reconciliation. Finance and program management require this link']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.opportunity.opportunity_id — NRE agreements are commercial vehicles arising from ASIC/custom IC opportunities. Required for NRE pipeline tracking.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: NRE revenue and associated costs must be allocated to profit centers for business unit P&L reporting, technology node profitability analysis, and management reporting by product line.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.test_program. Business justification: NRE agreements frequently include custom test program development as contractual deliverable. Milestone payments tied to test program completion, validation, and handoff. Critical for revenue recognit']
  quality_qualification_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_qualification_program. Business justification: NRE agreements fund and reference specific qualification programs as milestones (e.g., automotive qualification, reliability testing). Real process: NRE milestone payments and revenue recognition tied']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: NRE agreements involve supplier-specific tooling, mask sets, or process qualifications (foundry, OSAT). Essential for NRE cost allocation, supplier coordination, and milestone tracking in custom silic']
  actual_revenue_recognized decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue recognized to date according to accounting standards.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the NRE agreement, used in contracts and invoicing.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the NRE agreement type.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the milestone deliverable.']
  change_order_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if a change order has been issued for the agreement.']
  change_order_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the change order associated with the agreement.']
  completed_milestones varchar [note: 'Number of milestones that have been completed.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the agreement content.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the related SAP SD contract number.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agreement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the NRE amount.']
  deliverable_type varchar [note: 'Type of deliverable associated with the milestone.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the NRE agreement ends or expires; null for open‑ended agreements.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the NRE agreement becomes binding.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'True if the agreement grants exclusive rights to the customer for the developed IP.']
  forecasted_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected revenue from the NRE agreement for forecasting purposes.']
  invoice_trigger_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the milestone triggers an invoice generation.']
  ip_ownership_clause varchar [note: 'Specifies IP ownership rights granted by the agreement.']
  last_milestone_completed_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent milestone that has been completed.']
  milestone_actual_date date [note: 'Actual completion date recorded for the milestone.']
  milestone_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee associated with the milestone, invoiced upon completion.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Name of a specific NRE milestone (e.g., RTL Freeze, Tapeout).']
  milestone_planned_date date [note: 'Planned completion date for the milestone.']
  milestone_sequence varchar [note: 'Order of the milestone within the agreement schedule.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments regarding the agreement.']
  nre_total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total contracted NRE fee for the agreement.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms governing invoicing of NRE fees.']
  risk_assessment_score varchar [note: 'Internal risk rating (1‑5) for the NRE project.']
  sales_nre_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the NRE agreement.']
  sales_region varchar [note: 'Geographic sales region associated with the agreement.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement was terminated.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided if the agreement is terminated before completion.']
  total_milestones varchar [note: 'Total number of milestones defined in the agreement.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the agreement record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the agreement record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the agreement record.']

  Note: 'Master record: Non-Recurring Engineering (NRE) agreement governing custom IC or ASIC development engagements. Captures NRE fee structure, milestone payment schedule with individual billing/delivery milestones (RTL freeze, tapeout, first silicon, qualification), deliverables (RTL, GDS, PDK, tapeout), IP ownership terms, exclusivity clauses, and SAP SD contract reference. Contains milestone detail records tracking milestone name, planned/actual completion dates, milestone amounts, invoice trigger flags, approval status, and completion workflow. Enables NRE revenue recognition scheduling and project tracking. SSOT for all NRE commercial terms and milestone execution.'
}

Table sales.price_list {
  price_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the price list record.']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_family. Business justification: Price lists are defined per product family to enforce consistent pricing policies.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Price lists in semiconductor industry are catalog-specific with volume tiers and customer segments. Pricing teams maintain part-number-level price lists for distributors, OEMs, and direct customers wi']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: Price lists are defined for specific market segments; FK to customer_segment supports pricing strategy and segment‑based pricing reports.']
  superseded_by_price_list_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a newer price list that supersedes this one.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'Monetary amount of the price adjustment.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or justification for the price adjustment.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Type of price adjustment applied to the base price.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price list was approved.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'Append-only log of changes to the price list record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price list record was created.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the price.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the price becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the price expires or is superseded.']
  end_market varchar [note: 'Target market segment (e.g., automotive, mobile, data center).']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if this price list is the default for its scope.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes or comments about the price list.']
  price_list_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the price list purpose and scope.']
  price_list_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the price list.']
  price_list_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price list.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Category of pricing defined by the list (e.g., standard list, distributor, OEM, spot).']
  pricing_condition_code varchar [note: 'SAP SD pricing condition record identifier.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region for which the price list applies.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code applicable to the price.']
  unit_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'Base price per unit for the defined tier and product.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the price list record.']
  validity_end date [note: 'End date of the price list\'s validity window.']
  validity_start date [note: 'Start date of the price list\'s validity window.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the price list.']
  volume_tier_max varchar [note: 'Maximum quantity for the volume pricing tier.']
  volume_tier_min varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity for the volume pricing tier.']

  Note: 'Master record: Master pricing repository defining standard and customer-specific pricing for semiconductor products by product family, package type, volume tier, and end market. Supports list price, distributor price, OEM price, and spot price variants. Contains price adjustment records capturing negotiated deviations, special bids, volume rebate adjustments, design-win incentives, and distributor markdowns with adjustment type, approved deviation amount, approval authority, validity windows, and SAP SD pricing condition override references. Integrates with SAP SD condition records and Salesforce CRM pricing engine. Tracks effective dates, supersession history, and full adjustment audit trail. SSOT for all semiconductor product pricing and price deviations.'
}

  unit_price decimal(15,4) [note: 'Base price per unit of product or service covered by the contract.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contract record.']
  volume_commitment varchar [note: 'Total quantity of units the customer commits to purchase over the contract term.']

  Note: 'Master record: Long-term commercial contract with a semiconductor customer covering supply commitments, pricing agreements, volume guarantees, last-time-buy (LTB) provisions, product change notification (PCN) obligations, and end-of-life (EOL) terms. Distinct from NRE agreements — governs ongoing product supply rather than development services. Linked to SAP SD outline agreements.'
}

Table sales.territory {
  territory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the sales territory.']
  parent_territory_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent territory in the hierarchy, if any.']
  channel_tier varchar [note: 'Sales channel classification for the territory.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for the primary country of the territory. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|CHN|JPN|KOR|... — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the territory record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the territory definition expires or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the territory definition becomes effective for sales activities.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year to which the quota targets apply.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Delimited path representing the territory\'s position in the global→regional→country→district hierarchy (e.g., "Global>EMEA>Germany>Berlin").']
  is_overlay boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the territory is an overlay (true) used for specialist coverage in addition to primary territories.']
  multi_rep_coverage boolean [note: 'True if the territory is covered by multiple sales representatives or FAEs.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes or comments about the territory.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Internal code representing the broader region (e.g., EMEA, APAC, AMER) to which the territory belongs.']
  revenue_target_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fiscal period revenue quota assigned to the territory (in corporate currency).']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to uniquely reference the territory in external systems.']
  territory_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context about the territory.']
  territory_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the sales territory.']
  territory_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the territory.']
  territory_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the territory based on its purpose or focus.']
  unit_target_quantity varchar [note: 'Target number of units (e.g., die, wafers) to be sold from the territory for the fiscal period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the territory record.']

  Note: 'Master record: Sales territory definition and quota management assigning geographic regions, end markets, or named accounts to sales representatives and field application engineers (FAEs). Captures territory code, region hierarchy (global→regional→country→district), assigned sales rep, FAE coverage, revenue and unit targets by fiscal period (quarterly, half-year, annual), quota amounts, stretch targets, attainment tracking, and effective dates. Includes account-to-territory assignment details with assignment type (direct, distribution, rep firm), primary/secondary coverage flags, channel tier, and multi-rep coverage models. Supports territory realignment, overlay models (product specialists, strategic account managers), quota assignment workflows, and sales performance management common in semiconductor distribution channels. Used for compensation planning and quarterly business review (QBR) reporting.'
}

Table sales.sales_forecast {
  sales_forecast_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the forecast record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer for whom the forecast is created.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Sales forecasts must track ECCN classification mix to plan export compliance capacity, anticipate license application volumes, and assess regulatory risk exposure in semiconductor demand planning.']
  fab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fab_facility. Business justification: Sales forecasts are facility-specific for S&OP capacity planning. Forecast allocation to fabs drives loading models, capex decisions, and customer commit dates. Essential for monthly S&OP process and ']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: Capacity Planning compares forecasted demand with each fab tool\'s capacity; the Forecast‑vs‑Capacity report needs this FK.']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_family. Business justification: Forecasts are produced per product family for capacity planning and financial reporting.']
  flow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_flow. Business justification: Semiconductor demand planning requires forecasts at process-flow granularity to drive wafer start scheduling. The sales_forecast already links to process_technology_node but fab capacity planning requ']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Forecasts at IC catalog level enable fab loading, wafer starts, inventory planning. Demand planning requires part-number-level forecasts for production scheduling and capacity allocation across foundr']
  process_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_technology_node. Business justification: Demand forecasts are created per technology node; linking provides node‑specific capacity and yield assumptions.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Revenue forecasts must be allocated to profit centers for business unit planning, budget variance analysis, and executive reporting on product line performance against targets.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Forecasts at SKU level (packaging variants, speed grades) drive assembly/test capacity planning. OSAT planning and finished goods inventory management require SKU-level demand signals for accurate sup']
  sourcing_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.sourcing_contract. Business justification: Forecasts reference supply contracts to validate material availability and pricing assumptions. Critical for demand-supply matching, financial planning, and ensuring forecasted revenue is backed by su']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Forecasts are created for a specific sales territory; replace string with FK to territory for referential integrity.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the forecast was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who approved the forecast.']
  bias decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average signed deviation of forecast from actuals.']
  confidence_level varchar [note: 'Confidence rating assigned to the forecast.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the forecast record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  effective_end date [note: 'Last day of the period the forecast covers.']
  effective_start date [note: 'First day of the period the forecast covers.']
  end_market varchar [note: 'Target market segment of the forecast.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period identifier (e.g., FY2025Q1).']
  forecast_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the forecast (demand, supply, or capacity).']
  forecast_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the forecast (e.g., F2025Q1-001).']
  forecast_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the forecast.']
  forecast_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the forecast scenario (commit, upside, or best‑case).']
  geography varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the forecast geography.']
  horizon_months varchar [note: 'Length of the forecast horizon expressed in months.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the forecast is locked from further edits.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent review of the forecast.']
  last_reviewer varchar [note: 'User identifier who performed the last review.']
  mape decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical measure of forecast accuracy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or rationale for the forecast.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Projected unit volume for the forecast period.']
  revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected revenue amount for the forecast period.']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Named scenario associated with the forecast.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the forecast data.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the forecast was submitted for review.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for forecast_quantity.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the forecast.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the forecast record.']
  variance_to_actual decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance between forecast and actual results.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier for the forecast record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the forecast.']

  Note: 'Transaction record: Sales demand forecast capturing expected unit volume and revenue by product family, customer, end market, geography, and fiscal period. Supports rolling 12-month and 18-month forecast horizons used in semiconductor S&OP and wafer-start authorization processes. Tracks forecast version (commit, upside, best-case), submission date, confidence level, and variance against actuals. Integrates with SAP APO/IBP demand planning. Enables forecast accuracy KPI tracking (MAPE, bias) critical for fab capacity planning decisions.'
}

Table sales.booking {
  booking_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the booking record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who placed the booking.']
  address_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the destination location for shipment.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost accounting allocates each sales booking to a finance cost center to track expenses and calculate profitability.']
  customer_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the governing sales contract or NRE agreement.']
  die_bank_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.die_bank. Business justification: Bookings for KGD (Known Good Die) sales directly reference die bank inventory. Semiconductor customers often purchase bare die for multi-chip modules or hybrid assemblies. Die bank allocation at booki']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Bookings must capture ECCN classification at order entry to trigger export compliance screening, determine license requirements, and calculate compliance costs before order acceptance in semiconductor']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Required for export‑control compliance; each shipment (booking) must be tied to the export license covering the commodities and destination.']
  flow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_flow. Business justification: Bookings are fulfilled using a defined process flow; the link drives production scheduling and yield tracking.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Booking must reference the exact IC catalog entry to drive manufacturing and compliance checks.']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: Booking of customer orders triggers allocation of a wafer lot; linking booking to the wafer lot enables production scheduling and traceability.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Bookings must be assigned to legal entities for revenue recognition, statutory accounting, tax reporting, and intercompany reconciliation in multi-entity semiconductor operations.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.opportunity.opportunity_id — Bookings represent the commercial commitment arising from won opportunities. Essential for opportunity-to-revenue traceability.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit‑and‑loss reporting ties booking revenue to a profit center for accurate profit analysis.']
  qualification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_qualification. Business justification: Semiconductor bookings require a valid process qualification before delivery commitment. Revenue recognition and customer delivery SLAs depend on confirming the process_qualification is approved. Sale']
  restricted_party_screening_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.restricted_party_screening. Business justification: Every booking requires restricted party screening against denied parties lists (BIS, OFAC, etc.) before order acceptance to prevent illegal exports and ensure trade compliance in semiconductor sales.']
  sales_design_win_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.design_win.design_win_id — In semiconductor sales, bookings trace to the design-win that generated the demand. Critical for design-win revenue attribution.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Bookings capture orderable SKU with exact packaging, speed, temperature grade for revenue recognition and fulfillment. Order-to-cash flow requires SKU-level booking detail for shipment planning and in']
  sourcing_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.sourcing_contract. Business justification: Bookings reference the supply contract governing pricing, terms, and delivery for the ordered SKU. Required for revenue recognition, contract compliance, and ensuring booked orders align with supply c']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Bookings specify the supplier (OSAT, foundry) fulfilling the order for traceability, logistics coordination, and shipment tracking. Essential for order fulfillment and supply chain execution in semico']
  tapeout_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.tapeout. Business justification: Production bookings are linked to a tapeout to schedule wafer fabrication and track yield.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Bookings are tied to a sales territory; FK enables consistent territory hierarchy and reporting.']
  backlog_flag boolean [note: 'True if the booking is currently in backlog (unfulfilled).']
  booked_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units (dies) committed in the booking.']
  booked_revenue_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue amount before discounts and taxes, in the booking currency.']
  booked_revenue_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue after discounts and before tax.']
  booking_number varchar [note: 'External business number assigned to the booking, used in sales and finance processes.']
  booking_source varchar [note: 'Origin system that created the booking record.']
  booking_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the booking.']
  booking_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the booking was created in the source system.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by sales or operations.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the booking with internal and regulatory rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first captured in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the booking amounts.']
  delivery_mode varchar [note: 'Preferred logistics mode for shipment.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to the gross revenue.']
  external_order_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the original sales order in SAP SD.']
  forecast_flag boolean [note: 'True if the booking is included in the sales forecast.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the booking is deemed critical for product launch or revenue targets.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the booking (e.g., standard sale, NRE, design‑win).']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the customer.']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'Pricing approach applied to the booking.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the booking.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'High‑level product family (e.g., ASIC, FPGA, SoC) for the booked part.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if the booking must be reported under specific regulatory frameworks (e.g., CHIPS Act).']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the customer expects the shipment to be delivered.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date when the booked revenue is recognized for accounting.']
  sales_region varchar [note: 'Geographic sales region for the booking.']
  ship_to_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code of the ship‑to location.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the net revenue.']
  total_tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code used for calculating tax_amount.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the booking record.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Warranty duration provided to the customer, in months.']

  Note: 'Transaction record: Confirmed sales booking representing a firm customer purchase order commitment for semiconductor devices. Captures booking date, booked revenue, booked units, device part number, customer account, ship-to location, requested delivery date, backlog status, and SAP SD sales order reference. Represents the commercial booking event — distinct from shipment or invoice. SSOT for bookings-to-billings (B2B) ratio, book-to-ship analysis, and revenue waterfall reporting critical to semiconductor quarterly earnings.'
}

Table sales.lead {
  lead_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the lead record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer account associated with the lead.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign associated with the lead.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Leads originate from specific contacts at target accounts. Tracking the originating contact enables lead source analysis, contact scoring, campaign attribution, and conversion tracking. The contact_na']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Leads for controlled technology products need ECCN classification to assess export feasibility, qualify opportunities early, and route ITAR-flagged leads to authorized sales teams in semiconductor bus']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_family. Business justification: Lead qualification and routing depend on the product family of interest.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Leads express interest in specific IC catalog products for technical evaluation. Lead qualification tracks which specific parts are being evaluated for design-in, enabling targeted technical support a']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.opportunity.opportunity_id — Leads convert to opportunities upon qualification. This FK enables lead-to-opportunity conversion funnel analysis.']
  process_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_technology_node. Business justification: Leads are qualified against a target technology node; the link supports early feasibility analysis.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Leads for custom silicon or high-volume production capture preferred supplier information for feasibility assessment. Critical for lead qualification, technical feasibility evaluation, and early suppl']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Leads are assigned to a sales territory; FK provides proper linkage to territory hierarchy.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the lead\'s organization address.']
  company_industry varchar [note: 'Industry classification of the lead\'s organization.']
  company_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization associated with the lead.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the lead is subject to any regulatory compliance considerations (e.g., ITAR).']
  conversion_date date [note: 'Date when the lead was converted to an opportunity or closed.']
  conversion_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the lead conversion attempt.']
  country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the lead\'s organization.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the lead was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the estimated revenue.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level of the lead data as per corporate policy.']
  device_interest varchar [note: 'Specific type of semiconductor device the lead is interested in (e.g., ASIC, FPGA, SoC).']
  estimated_quantity varchar [note: 'Projected number of units the lead may purchase.']
  estimated_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected monetary value of the lead opportunity.']
  expected_close_date date [note: 'Projected date by which the lead is expected to be closed.']
  is_nre boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the lead is for a Non‑Recurring Engineering (NRE) project.']
  itar_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the lead involves technology subject to ITAR regulations.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the lead record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the lead record.']
  lead_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for the lead as used in sales systems.']
  lead_source varchar [note: 'Origin channel through which the lead was captured.']
  lead_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the lead within the sales pipeline.']
  lead_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lead based on sales process stage.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Target market segment for the lead (e.g., Consumer, Automotive, Data Center).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional comments or observations about the lead.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the lead for follow‑up.']
  rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative rating of the lead\'s potential.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region of the lead (e.g., APAC, EMEA, AMER).']
  score varchar [note: 'Numerical score representing lead quality based on qualification criteria.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record where the lead originated (e.g., Salesforce, web portal).']
  target_application varchar [note: 'Intended application area for the semiconductor product (e.g., mobile, automotive, AI).']
  technology_node varchar [note: 'Process technology node of interest (e.g., 7nm, 5nm).']
  zip_code varchar [note: 'Postal code of the lead\'s organization address.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the lead record.']

  Note: 'Transaction record: Early-stage sales lead representing an unqualified potential customer or design opportunity. Captures lead source, contact information, target application, device interest, estimated opportunity size, lead status, and conversion outcome. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Lead object. Tracks lead-to-opportunity conversion funnel for semiconductor design-in campaigns.'
}

Table sales.channel_partner {
  channel_partner_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the channel partner record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Partner‑related operational costs are tracked in a dedicated finance cost center for cost allocation.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Channel partners transact with specific legal entities for invoicing, payment processing, VAT/GST compliance, and intercompany settlement in multi-country distribution networks.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Distribution agreements require linking each channel partner to its upstream supplier(s) for inventory allocation and contractual compliance.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the partner\'s primary business address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the partner\'s primary business address (optional).']
  authorized_product_lines varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of product families the partner may sell.']
  channel_partner_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the partner relationship.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the partner\'s primary address.']
  contract_effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the partner contract becomes effective.']
  contract_effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the partner contract expires or is scheduled to terminate (null if open‑ended).']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the master agreement governing the partner relationship.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of the partner contract.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for the partner\'s primary location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the channel partner record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  external_partner_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used for the partner in external systems (e.g., third‑party distributor portal).']
  hub_consignment_level varchar [note: 'Level of consignment inventory maintained at partner hubs.']
  inventory_reporting_obligation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner must provide regular inventory visibility to Semiconductors.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the record was last reviewed for compliance or data quality.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for internal comments, observations, or special handling instructions.']
  partner_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trade name of the channel partner organization.']
  partner_type varchar [note: 'Category of the partner based on its business model and relationship to Semiconductors.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the partner\'s primary address.']
  price_protection_claims_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates if the partner can submit price‑protection claims under the agreement.']
  pricing_agreement_type varchar [note: 'Pricing model governing the partner\'s purchase terms.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address used for official communications with the partner.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the main business contact for the partner.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Telephone number for the primary partner contact.']
  sap_partner_function_code varchar [note: 'SAP SD code that classifies the partner\'s functional role (e.g., sold‑to, ship‑to, bill‑to).']
  sell_through_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of partner inventory sold to end customers over a defined period.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the partner\'s primary address.']
  stock_on_hand varchar [note: 'Current quantity of semiconductor units physically held by the partner.']
  territory_coverage varchar [note: 'Geographic or market territories the partner is authorized to sell within.']
  tier_classification varchar [note: 'Strategic tier assigned to the partner reflecting level of authorization and performance expectations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the channel partner record.']
  weeks_of_supply decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated number of weeks the current stock will satisfy forecasted demand.']

  Note: 'Master record: Authorized semiconductor sales channel partner including distributors (Arrow, Avnet, WPG), rep firms, value-added resellers (VARs), and system integrators. Captures partner type, authorized product lines, territory coverage, tier classification (authorized, preferred, elite), contract status, inventory reporting obligations (stock on hand, weeks of supply, sell-through, hub/consignment levels), and SAP SD partner function reference. SSOT for channel partner identity and distributor inventory visibility within the sales domain. Supports ship-from-stock-and-debit (SFSD) processing and price protection claim management.'
}

Business justification: Design registrations specify exact IC catalog part numbers being designed into end customer\'s product. Registration systems track specific part numbers for price protection, commission allocation, and']

  sales_rep_firm_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the sales rep firm.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the design registration was initially submitted.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to the registration record.']
  wafer_size_mm varchar [note: 'Diameter of the wafer used for the design, expressed in millimetres.']

  Note: 'Transaction record: Formal design registration submitted by a distributor or rep firm to claim sales credit and price protection for a specific customer design opportunity. Captures registered device, customer, end application, estimated annual volume, registration status (pending, approved, rejected, expired), approval date, and protected pricing terms. Prevents channel conflict and double-claiming in semiconductor distribution channels.'
}

Table sales.campaign {
  campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for campaign']

  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the campaign budget and plan.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget allocated for the campaign.']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'External or legacy code used to reference the campaign in marketing systems.']
  campaign_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the campaign objectives and scope.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the marketing campaign.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the campaign.']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Category of the campaign indicating its primary purpose.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Primary marketing channel used to deliver the campaign.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign complies with internal and external regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign record was first created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Planned or actual end date of the campaign.']
  external_campaign_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the campaign in an external marketing platform (e.g., Salesforce, Marketo).']
  kpi_actual varchar [note: 'Actual measured value for the primary KPI of the campaign.']
  kpi_target varchar [note: 'Target value for the primary KPI of the campaign (e.g., leads, pipeline value).']
  marketing_platform varchar [note: 'Marketing automation platform used to execute the campaign.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments captured by campaign managers.']
  objective varchar [note: 'High‑level business objective the campaign aims to achieve (e.g., lead generation, brand awareness).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the campaign.']
  region varchar [note: 'Primary geographic region for campaign execution.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk level for the campaign execution.']
  roi_estimate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected return on investment for the campaign, expressed as a percentage.']
  start_date date [note: 'Planned or actual start date of the campaign.']
  target_product_family varchar [note: 'Product family or portfolio the campaign promotes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the campaign record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for campaign. Referenced by campaign_id.'
}

Table order.order {
  order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for order']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who placed the sales order. Links to the customer master data product.']
  blanket_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.blanket_order. Business justification: An order may be covered by a blanket purchase agreement; adding FK from order to blanket_order captures this relationship without creating a cycle.']
  channel_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.channel_partner. Business justification: Orders may be placed via channel partners; the FK enables partner‑attributed revenue and commission reporting.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Order Management requires a primary sales contact for each order; the Order Confirmation Report pulls contact details, making this link essential.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Orders are charged to a cost center for expense tracking and budgeting in the finance system.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Export compliance reporting requires linking each order to its ECCN classification for customs documentation.']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Export License Management: each sales order must reference the approved export license, replacing the free‑text export_license_number.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Order creation originates from a closed‑won opportunity; linking enables opportunity‑to‑order conversion reporting.']
  price_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.price_agreement. Business justification: Pricing Governance applies a specific price agreement to an order; the Pricing Audit Report and revenue recognition rely on this relationship.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit‑center reporting requires each sales order to be linked to the responsible profit center.']
  quality_qualification_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_qualification_program. Business justification: Customer orders for automotive (IATF 16949) and aerospace applications require qualification program compliance tracking. Orders must reference the applicable qualification program to ensure all deliv']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote. Business justification: Orders are generated from accepted quotes; the link supports quote‑to‑order tracking and pricing audit.']
  restricted_party_screening_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.restricted_party_screening. Business justification: Trade compliance hold process records the screening result for the customer/account on the order.']
  sales_design_win_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Salesforce CRM design-win opportunity that originated this sales order. Links the order to the customer design-in engagement, enabling design-win to revenue conversion tracking.']
  shipping_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Shipping Execution process stores the exact customer address for each order; the Shipping Manifest and compliance checks depend on this link.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Direct orders placed with suppliers for foundry services, wafer procurement, or material purchases. Supplier linkage at order header enables supplier order tracking, delivery performance monitoring, a']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the ordered products were delivered to the customer or ship-to location. Used for on-time delivery (OTD) performance analysis and customer satisfaction reporting.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current inventory or production capacity allocation status of the order. Indicates whether sufficient die, wafer, or finished goods inventory has been reserved to fulfill the order. Critical for backlog management and supply-demand balancing.']
  backlog_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this order is currently included in the active order backlog. True when the order is open and not yet fully shipped or invoiced. Used for backlog valuation, revenue forecasting, and capacity planning.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason code or description for order cancellation. Populated only when order_status is CANCELLED. Used for lost revenue analysis, demand forecasting accuracy, and customer relationship management.']
  chips_act_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this order is associated with products or programs eligible for US CHIPS and Science Act incentives or reporting requirements. Used for government reporting and subsidy tracking.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'The delivery date confirmed by the semiconductor manufacturer based on production capacity, wafer fab scheduling, and logistics. May differ from the requested delivery date. Used for backlog management and customer commitment tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sales order record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA SD). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and order entry performance measurement. Corresponds to SAP VBAK.ERDAT + VBAK.ERZET.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the order transaction (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, TWD, KRW). Defines the currency in which order values, pricing, and invoicing are denominated. Corresponds to SAP VBAK.WAERK.']
  customer_po_number varchar [note: 'The purchase order number provided by the customer at the time of order placement. Used for cross-referencing with the customer\'s procurement system and required for invoice matching. Corresponds to SAP VBAK.BSTNK.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'The sales channel through which the order was placed. Determines pricing tiers, commission structures, and revenue reporting by channel. Corresponds to SAP VBAK.VTWEG.']
  end_market_segment varchar [note: 'The end-market application segment for which the ordered semiconductor products are destined. Used for market segmentation analysis, revenue reporting by vertical, and strategic planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: COMPUTING|MOBILE|AUTOMOTIVE|AI_ML|IOT|INDUSTRIAL|NETWORKING|CONSUMER|DEFENSE|MEDICAL — promote to reference product]']
  export_license_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an export license is required for this order based on the destination country, end-user, and product ECCN classification. True triggers export compliance review workflow before shipment authorization.']
  gross_order_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total gross value of the sales order before application of discounts, taxes, or surcharges, expressed in the order currency. Represents the sum of all line item gross values. Used for revenue forecasting and backlog valuation.']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description for placing the order on hold. Populated when order_status is ON_HOLD. Common reasons include credit hold, export compliance review, customer request, or supply shortage.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the responsibilities of buyer and seller for delivery, risk transfer, and cost allocation. Corresponds to SAP VBAK.INCO1. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — promote to reference product]']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'The named place or port associated with the Incoterms code, specifying where risk and cost transfer between buyer and seller (e.g., \'Shanghai Port\', \'Customer Dock\'). Corresponds to SAP VBAK.INCO2.']
  itar_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any products in this order are subject to ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations) controls. When True, additional export authorization and end-use certification requirements apply before shipment.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the sales order record in the source system. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing. Corresponds to SAP VBAK.AEDAT.']
  net_order_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net value of the sales order after application of all discounts and pricing conditions but before tax. Represents the contractually agreed revenue amount. Corresponds to SAP VBAK.NETWR. Used for revenue recognition and financial reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special instructions associated with the sales order, capturing customer-specific requirements, handling instructions, or internal processing notes. Corresponds to SAP order header text.']
  nre_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Non-Recurring Engineering (NRE) charges associated with this order, covering one-time design, mask set, and process development costs. Applicable for ASIC, custom IC, and MPW order types. Reported separately from unit product revenue.']
  order_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the customer placed the sales order. Represents the principal business event date for the order-to-cash lifecycle. Corresponds to SAP VBAK.AUDAT.']
  order_source varchar [note: 'The channel or system through which the customer order was received and entered. Used for order entry efficiency analysis and digital transformation tracking.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sales order within the order-to-cash process. Drives workflow routing, backlog reporting, and revenue recognition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DRAFT|OPEN|IN_FULFILLMENT|SHIPPED|DELIVERED|CLOSED|CANCELLED|ON_HOLD — promote to reference product]']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sales order by product or engagement type. Determines order processing rules, pricing, and fulfillment workflow. Values include: STANDARD_IC (standard integrated circuit), ASIC (Application-Specific Integrated Circuit), FPGA (Field-Programmable Gate Array), MPW (Multi-Project Wafer), NRE (Non-Recurring Engineering), DIE_BANK (die bank pull order), WAFER_START (wafer start authorization). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: STANDARD_IC|ASIC|FPGA|MPW|NRE|DIE_BANK|WAFER_START — promote to reference product]']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP payment terms key defining the payment due date, early payment discount conditions, and cash discount percentages applicable to this order. Corresponds to SAP VBAK.ZTERM (e.g., NT30, NT60, 2/10NET30).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the order, influencing production scheduling, wafer start queue position, and allocation decisions. Critical orders may trigger expedite fees or override standard allocation rules.']
  reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the products ordered comply with the EU REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals) Regulation. Required for EU market access and supply chain chemical safety reporting.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The date by which the customer has requested delivery of the ordered products. Used for delivery scheduling, wafer start planning, and on-time delivery (OTD) performance measurement. Corresponds to SAP VBAK.VDATU.']
  rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the products ordered comply with the EU RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances) Directive, restricting the use of specific hazardous materials in electronic equipment. Required for EU market access and customer compliance declarations.']
  ship_to_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the destination country for shipment. Critical for export compliance screening (EAR/ITAR/BIS), trade sanctions checks, and logistics routing. Corresponds to SAP ship-to party country.']
  so_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible sales order number as assigned by SAP S/4HANA SD module. Used in all customer-facing communications, shipping documents, and invoices. Corresponds to the VBELN field in SAP VBAK table.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total tax amount applicable to the sales order, including VAT, GST, or other applicable taxes, expressed in the order currency. Required for tax reporting and financial compliance.']
  wafer_start_authorization boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this order has triggered a wafer start authorization in the fabrication facility (FAB). When True, the MES (Manufacturing Execution System) has been instructed to initiate wafer lot processing for this order.']

  Note: 'Master record for all customer sales orders in the semiconductor order-to-cash lifecycle. Captures order header information including customer identity, order type (standard IC, ASIC, FPGA, MPW, NRE), order date, requested delivery date, priority, incoterms, payment terms, currency, total order value, and order status. SSOT for all customer-placed orders including wafer start authorizations, die bank orders, and production lot assignments. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD module.'
}

Table order.line {
  line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each order line item within the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for the order_line data product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer placing the order. Used for line-level customer attribution in multi-ship-to or multi-sold-to scenarios. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA SD KUNNR (Customer Number).']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Engineering sample orders, pre-production orders, and design-win tracking require catalog-level product reference before SKU finalization. Critical for NRE orders and prototype fulfillment where packa']
  primary_sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent sales order header to which this line item belongs. Establishes the header-to-line relationship in the order-to-cash lifecycle. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA SD VBELN (Sales Document Number).']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.test_program. Business justification: Required for Test Program Assignment per SKU during order fulfillment; ensures each order line selects the correct test program for quality validation.']
  quality_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_spec. Business justification: Order lines must reference applicable quality specifications for acceptance criteria. Each order line commits to specific quality parameters (yield, defect density, reliability) that are defined in qu']
  quote_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote_line. Business justification: Each order line is derived from a quote line; needed for line‑level margin and fulfillment analysis.']
  reach_svhc_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.reach_svhc_declaration. Business justification: REACH SVHC Declaration is required per SKU on an order line to certify hazardous substance compliance.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the semiconductor product (IC, SoC, ASIC, FPGA, or discrete device) being ordered on this line. Links to the product master for SKU-level configuration details including package type, speed grade, and temperature range.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Primary supplier for the SKU on each order line, used in procurement planning and supplier performance reporting.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'The date on which the semiconductor units for this order line were physically shipped from the warehouse or OSAT facility. Populated upon goods issue posting in SAP S/4HANA. Used for on-time delivery (OTD) performance measurement.']
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the supply allocation method applied to this order line. Standard = normal backlog allocation; Priority = customer-priority or design-win protected allocation; Strategic = long-term agreement allocation; Spot = spot market fulfillment; Buffer = safety stock draw.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for cancellation of this order line when line_status is \'cancelled\'. Supports backlog analysis, demand planning accuracy, and customer relationship management. Populated only for cancelled lines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer_request|supply_constraint|end_of_life|duplicate|pricing_dispute|export_hold|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of semiconductor units confirmed by supply chain for delivery on this order line, reflecting available inventory, wafer start authorizations, and die bank allocations. May differ from ordered quantity due to allocation constraints.']
  confirmed_ship_date date [note: 'The date confirmed by supply chain and manufacturing for shipment of this order line, based on available inventory, die bank status, and OSAT packaging capacity. May differ from requested ship date due to supply constraints.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the semiconductor product was manufactured (FAB location). Required for customs declarations, trade compliance, tariff classification, and CHIPS Act domestic content reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this order line record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking. Conforms to ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the transaction currency of this order line (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, KRW, TWD). Semiconductor industry commonly transacts in USD but supports multi-currency for global customers.']
  customer_part_number varchar [note: 'The customer\'s own internal part number or cross-reference number for the ordered semiconductor product. Critical for design-win tracking, customer portal integration, and order acknowledgment matching. Sourced from Salesforce CRM design-win records.']
  date_entered date [note: 'The business date on which this order line was entered into the SAP S/4HANA SD system. Represents the commercial event date for backlog aging calculations and order-to-cash cycle time measurement.']
  die_bank_order boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this order line is to be fulfilled from the die bank (inventory of known good dies (KGD) awaiting packaging) rather than from finished goods or new wafer starts. True = die bank fulfillment; False = standard finished goods or new production.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export Administration Regulations (EAR) Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) for the semiconductor product on this order line (e.g., 3A001 for advanced ICs). Required for export compliance screening, license determination, and ITAR/EAR regulatory reporting.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between the semiconductor company and the customer for this order line (e.g., FOB, DDP, EXW). Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA SD INCO1. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  item_category varchar [note: 'SAP SD item category classification for this order line, indicating the business nature of the line (e.g., standard product sale, consignment fill-up, customer return, engineering sample, NRE charge, MPW service, evaluation unit). Drives pricing, delivery, and billing behavior. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|consignment|returns|sample|nre|mpw|evaluation — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential position number of this line item within the parent sales order. Used for ordering, display, and reference in customer-facing documents. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA SD POSNR (Item Number of the SD Document).']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of this order line item within the order-to-cash lifecycle. Drives backlog management, allocation decisions, and revenue recognition. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA SD overall delivery status at item level.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturing lot number assigned to the production batch fulfilling this order line. Enables traceability from customer order back to wafer fabrication lot, die bank lot, and OSAT packaging lot. Sourced from Camstar MES lot tracking.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material number (MATNR) identifying the specific semiconductor product SKU ordered on this line. Includes full part number with package, speed grade, and temperature range encoded per internal part numbering convention.']
  mpw_order boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this order line is associated with a Multi-Project Wafer (MPW) run, where multiple customer designs share a single wafer to reduce NRE costs. True = MPW order; False = dedicated production lot order.']
  net_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total net value of this order line calculated as confirmed quantity multiplied by unit price in the transaction currency. Used for backlog reporting, revenue forecasting, and SOX-compliant revenue recognition. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA SD NETWR (Net Value of the Order Item).']
  ordered_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of semiconductor units (dies, packaged ICs, wafers, or reels) requested by the customer on this order line. Expressed in the sales unit of measure (e.g., pieces, wafers, reels). Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA SD KWMENG (Cumulative Order Quantity).']
  partial_shipment_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer permits partial shipments against this order line. True = partial shipments accepted; False = complete order line quantity must ship together. Drives delivery scheduling and backlog management decisions.']
  product_revision varchar [note: 'The silicon revision or stepping of the ordered semiconductor product (e.g., A0, B1, C2). Critical for tracking product change notifications (PCNs), errata management, and ensuring customers receive the correct silicon revision per their qualification.']
  reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the semiconductor product on this order line is compliant with the EU REACH Regulation (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals). Required for EU chemical safety compliance and customer material declarations.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The date by which the customer requires receipt of the semiconductor units at their facility. Distinct from requested ship date; accounts for transit time. Used for customer SLA compliance tracking.']
  requested_ship_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer has requested shipment of this order line. Drives production scheduling, wafer start authorization timelines, and OSAT packaging commitments. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA SD WUNSK (Customer\'s Requested Delivery Date at Item Level).']
  rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the semiconductor product on this order line is compliant with the EU Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) Directive, restricting use of lead, mercury, cadmium, and other hazardous materials. Required for EU market access.']
  sap_line_item_number varchar [note: 'The native SAP S/4HANA SD six-digit item number (POSNR) for this order line, used for traceability back to the system of record and for EDI/customer portal reconciliation.']
  ship_to_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the destination country of this order line shipment. Used for export control screening, customs documentation, tariff determination, and trade compliance reporting.']
  shipped_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cumulative quantity of semiconductor units actually shipped against this order line across all partial shipments. Used for backlog calculation and revenue recognition. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD delivery documents.']
  special_handling_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating special handling, storage, or shipping requirements for this order line (e.g., ESD-sensitive, moisture-sensitive level (MSL), controlled humidity, ITAR-controlled, hazmat). Drives warehouse and logistics instructions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ESD|MSL1|MSL2|MSL3|ITAR|HAZMAT|CONTROLLED_TEMP|FRAGILE — promote to reference product]']
  speed_grade varchar [note: 'The speed or performance grade of the ordered semiconductor product, indicating the maximum operating frequency or timing specification (e.g., -1, -2, -3 for FPGAs; 3200MHz, 4800MHz for memory). Differentiates SKUs within the same product family.']
  temperature_grade varchar [note: 'Operating temperature range classification for the ordered semiconductor product. Commercial (0°C to 70°C), Industrial (-40°C to 85°C), Automotive (-40°C to 125°C, AEC-Q100), Military (-55°C to 125°C). Determines qualification standard and pricing tier.']
  to_sales_order varchar [note: 'FK to order.sales_order.sales_order_id — Fundamental header-to-line relationship. Every order line must belong to exactly one sales order. This is the most critical FK in the domain — without it, order lines are orphaned.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Sales unit of measure for the ordered semiconductor product. EA = Each (individual packaged IC), WFR = Wafer, REEL = Tape-and-reel packaging, TRAY = JEDEC tray, TUBE = Tube packaging. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA SD VRKME (Sales Unit).']
  unit_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'Agreed net unit selling price for the semiconductor product on this order line, expressed in the transaction currency. Reflects negotiated pricing, volume discounts, and design-win pricing agreements. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA SD NETPR (Net Price).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to this order line record in the data platform. Supports change data capture (CDC), audit trail, and incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.']
  wafer_start_authorization boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a wafer start authorization (WSA) has been issued to the fabrication facility (FAB) to initiate production of wafers for fulfilling this order line. True = WSA issued; False = pending or not required (e.g., fulfilled from die bank or finished goods inventory).']

  Note: 'Individual line items within a customer sales order, each representing a distinct semiconductor product SKU, quantity, unit price, requested ship date, confirmed ship date, and line-level status. Captures product configuration details such as package type, speed grade, temperature range, and special handling requirements. Supports partial shipments and line-level allocation tracking. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD order line items.'
}

Table order.wafer_start_authorization {
  wafer_start_authorization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a wafer start authorization record. Primary key for this entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
