  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Export compliance verification requires each inspected lot to be linked to the export license authorizing its shipment.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_wafer_lot. Business justification: Inspection lot records are tied to a specific wafer lot for lot‑level quality inspection; linking enables lot‑level inspection reports and traceability.']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Inspection lots tracked at product family level for yield trending, family-level quality metrics, and portfolio quality management. Supports business unit quality reporting.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Inspection lot reports are generated per IC part; linking to ic_catalog enables traceability for compliance and lot‑by‑lot quality reports.']
  inspection_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: Inspection Lot Report requires linking each lot to the Fab Tool used for inspection to ensure traceability and compliance.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Inspection lot is generated per order line; linking enables traceability for warranty and inspection reporting.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Opportunity‑to‑Lot traceability report ties production lot quality outcomes back to the originating sales opportunity for revenue impact analysis.']
  physical_layout_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.physical_layout. Business justification: Inspection defect patterns are correlated with physical layout (GDS) to identify systematic DFM issues. Semiconductor DFM engineers require this link to map wafer inspection results back to specific l']
  process_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.process_node. Business justification: Inspection lots track process node for yield analysis, defect density correlation, and process capability monitoring. Essential for fab yield management and process qualification.']
  process_step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_process_step. Business justification: Inspection lots are created at specific fabrication process steps (in-line metrology, visual inspection). The existing process_step_id on inspection_lot points to process.process_step (cross-domain); ']
  process_tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.tool_chamber. Business justification: Inspection lots processed through specific chambers require chamber traceability for lot disposition decisions. Chamber hold/release decisions based on inspection results are standard semiconductor qu']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Inspection lots are performed within specific profit centers for P&L tracking, yield accountability by business unit, and fab line performance measurement in semiconductor operations.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.test_program. Business justification: Inspection lots execute against specific test programs for parametric and functional validation. Test program defines the measurement suite applied to the lot. Critical for traceability and test cover']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Inspection Lot‑PO traceability required for Supplier Performance Review reports linking inspection results to the originating purchase order.']
  quality_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_spec. Business justification: Inspection lots are evaluated against quality specifications. The inspection_lot has acceptance_criteria: INT and rejection_criteria: INT which are scalar representations of what should be defined in ']
  step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_step. Business justification: Inspection lot tracking ties each lot to the specific process step being inspected, required for lot‑by‑step quality dashboards.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Inspection lots are physically staged at storage locations during quality hold/release gates. Fab operations require tracking where quarantined or released lots reside for material flow control and co']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier providing the material.']
  tapeout_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.tapeout. Business justification: Inspection lots on fabricated wafers must trace to the originating tapeout submission for yield-by-tapeout reporting and DFM feedback loops. Semiconductor fabs require this link to correlate inspectio']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection lot record was created.']
  defect_count varchar [note: 'Total number of defects detected in the lot.']
  defect_density decimal(10,4) [note: 'Defects per unit area metric for the lot.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Final disposition assigned to the lot after inspection.']
  disposition_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the chosen disposition.']
  external_lot_code varchar [note: 'External reference code used by suppliers or customers.']
  iatf_16949_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with IATF 16949 automotive quality standards.']
  inspection_lot_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection lot.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the inspection.']
  inspection_stage varchar [note: 'Process stage at which the inspection occurs.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Category of inspection performed on the lot.']
  iso_9001_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with ISO 9001 quality management.']
  jedec_reliability_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with JEDEC reliability specifications.']
  kgd_certification_date date [note: 'Date when KGD certification was granted.']
  kgd_certified boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the lot contains KGD‑certified dies.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User name of the person who last modified the record.']
  lot_size varchar [note: 'Total number of units (wafers, dies, or packaged parts) in the lot.']
  lot_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lot based on its position in the workflow.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Type of material the lot consists of.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the primary measurement taken during inspection.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the measured value.']
  measurement_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the key measurement (e.g., critical dimension).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the inspection lot.']
  quality_engineer varchar [note: 'Name of the quality engineer responsible for the lot.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of units sampled from the lot.']
  sampling_plan_aql varchar [note: 'AQL value defined for the sampling plan.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit used to quantify the lot size.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  wafer_size_mm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Diameter of wafers in the lot, expressed in millimetres.']
  yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of good units produced from the lot.']

  Note: 'Master record for a quality inspection lot representing a batch of wafers, dies, or packaged units submitted for quality inspection at any stage. Covers all inspection types: incoming material (IQC for raw wafers, chemicals, photomasks, gases, OSAT subassemblies with supplier and PO reference), in-process (FEOL, BEOL, packaging), and final outgoing. Captures lot origin, supplier ID (for IQC), inspection type, inspection stage, lot size, sampling plan parameters (AQL, sample size, acceptance/rejection numbers), inspection results, measured parameters, and disposition decision (accept, reject, hold, rework). Integrates with SAP QM and Camstar MES for lot status management and goods receipt inspection. SSOT for all quality inspection activities across the fab-to-finish flow including incoming quality control.'
}

Table quality.defect_record {
  defect_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the defect record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Enables the Defect Ownership Tracking process, linking each defect to the responsible customer account for warranty claims and customer notifications.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Defect handling expenses are charged to a cost center for financial tracking of quality loss costs.']
  design_ip_core_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ip_core. Business justification: Needed for Defect Attribution Report to associate each defect with the specific IP core block used in the design, enabling targeted remediation.']
  detection_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: Defect Analysis Report ties each defect to the detecting Fab Tool for root‑cause analysis and corrective action planning.']
  detection_final_test_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.final_test_run. Business justification: Defects found at final test must link to the test run that detected them. Critical for test escape analysis, DPPM calculation, and final test program effectiveness evaluation.']
  detection_tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.tool_chamber. Business justification: Defects originate from specific chambers, not just tools. Chamber-level defect pareto analysis drives targeted maintenance and chamber matching. Essential for yield excursion response and chamber qual']
  detection_wafer_probe_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.wafer_probe_run. Business justification: Defects detected during wafer probe must link to the specific probe run that identified them. Essential for yield analysis, test escape tracking, and probe card qualification.']
  die_bank_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.die_bank. Business justification: Defects are tracked at die level in die banks for KGD certification. Inline inspection and final test defect data must link to die bank inventory for bin assignment, yield analysis, and customer quali']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Defect records must link to IC products for root cause analysis, product-specific yield learning, and quality trending. Critical for product engineering and reliability analysis.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.inspection_lot.inspection_lot_id — Every defect is detected within an inspection lot. This is the fundamental parent-child relationship for defect tracking. Without this FK, defects cannot be traced to their inspection context.']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: Defect Tracking & Recall Management uses the inventory lot ID to locate and quarantine affected stock across the supply chain.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Defect records must be tied to the originating order line for root‑cause analysis, warranty claims, and corrective actions.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Defects in incoming materials (substrates, chemicals, photoresists) require linkage to material master for root cause analysis, supplier feedback (SCAR), and material qualification tracking. Standard ']
  physical_layout_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.physical_layout. Business justification: Individual defect records are traced to physical layout to identify layout patterns (antenna violations, density issues) causing systematic defects. DFM analysis and post-silicon debug in semiconducto']
  primary_wafer_map_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.wafer_map.wafer_map_id — Defects with X/Y coordinates must link to the wafer map for spatial analysis. This is critical for systematic defect pattern detection.']
  process_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.process_node. Business justification: Defect records correlate to process node for yield learning, defect density tracking, and process improvement. Critical for fab yield management and technology maturity assessment.']
  process_recipe_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.recipe. Business justification: Defect analysis reports need the recipe used during the step to correlate process parameters with defect types.']
  process_step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_process_step. Business justification: Defects are detected at specific fabrication process steps. The existing process_step_id on defect_record points to process.process_step; a direct FK to fabrication_process_step enables fab-domain tra']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Defect records directly impact profit center yield metrics and cost-of-quality reporting. Semiconductor fabs track defect density and yield loss by business unit for P&L accountability.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Defect Record linked to purchase order for Supplier Defect Rate reports used in corrective action planning.']
  quality_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_spec. Business justification: Defect records identify deviations from quality specifications. Linking defect_record to quality_spec identifies which specification was violated by the detected defect, enabling specification-level d']
  step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_step. Business justification: Root cause analysis requires linking each defect record to the exact process step where the defect originated, used in RCA reports.']
  tapeout_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.tapeout. Business justification: Defect records must reference the tapeout that produced the wafer for post-silicon debug and design-quality traceability. Semiconductor quality engineers use tapeout-level defect aggregation to drive ']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer on which the defect was observed.']
  bin_assignment varchar [note: 'Pass/fail bin code assigned to the die (e.g., P, F1, F2).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes from the inspector or analyst.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Planned or executed action to remediate the defect.']
  defect_area_um2 double [note: 'Calculated area of the defect in square micrometers.']
  defect_classification varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the defect (e.g., particle, patterning, etch).']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the defect type (e.g., D001, D002).']
  defect_density_per_zone double [note: 'Number of defects per unit area within the zone.']
  defect_layer varchar [note: 'Process layer where the defect was found.']
  defect_severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating indicating impact on yield.']
  defect_size_nm double [note: 'Measured size of the defect in nanometers.']
  defect_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the defect record.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to detect the defect.']
  die_x varchar [note: 'Column index of the die containing the defect on the wafer grid.']
  die_y varchar [note: 'Row index of the die containing the defect on the wafer grid.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Final handling decision for the defect.']
  edge_exclusion_zone_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the defect lies within the defined edge exclusion area.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the defect was detected during inspection.']
  flat_notch_orientation varchar [note: 'Orientation of wafer flat or notch relative to the defect.']
  inspection_recipe varchar [note: 'Name of the inspection recipe or parameter set used.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the defect record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the defect record.']
  repeatability_flag boolean [note: 'True if the defect pattern repeats across multiple wafers.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the identified root cause.']
  x_coordinate double [note: 'Horizontal position of the defect on the wafer map (millimeters).']
  y_coordinate double [note: 'Vertical position of the defect on the wafer map (millimeters).']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing individual defect events, wafer-level defect maps, and spatial bin distributions detected during wafer inspection, die sort, or final test. At the defect event level: stores defect classification code, defect coordinates (X/Y on wafer map), defect size, defect layer (FEOL/MOL/BEOL), detection method (KLA ICOS optical, e-beam, SEM), severity rating, and disposition (scrap, rework, accept). At the wafer map level: captures per-wafer die grid coordinates, per-die pass/fail bin assignment, defect density per zone, edge exclusion zone, flat/notch orientation, and map generation timestamp. Enables yield spatial analysis, systematic defect pattern detection (repeater analysis, cluster detection), and wafer-level bin map visualization. Integrates directly with KLA ICOS inspection system output and ATE wafer probing systems. SSOT for all defect events, spatial defect analysis, and wafer-level quality maps.'
}

Table quality.wafer_map {
  wafer_map_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for wafer_map']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inspection tool that produced the map.']
  final_test_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.final_test_run. Business justification: Final test generates updated wafer maps with bin assignments. Links wafer maps to final test runs for die-level traceability and yield correlation analysis.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Wafer maps represent die-level yield for specific IC products. Essential for product qualification, yield analysis, and known-good-die certification programs.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Wafer maps showing die pass/fail patterns must link to ic_design_project for design-level yield pattern analysis. Semiconductor yield engineers correlate wafer map failure clusters with design blocks ']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: A wafer map is generated as part of an inspection lot — each wafer map captures the spatial defect/bin distribution for a wafer within a specific inspection lot batch. This parent-child relationship i']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: Wafer Map Archive ties each map to the inventory wafer lot for yield analysis and historical compliance audits.']
  process_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.process_node. Business justification: Wafer maps track yield patterns by process node for systematic defect analysis and process capability monitoring. Essential for technology yield ramp and process optimization.']
  process_tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.tool_chamber. Business justification: Wafer maps capture spatial defect patterns tied to specific chambers. Chamber-level bin distribution analysis drives process optimization and chamber matching. Essential for chamber qualification and ']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Wafer maps drive profit center yield metrics (die_yield_percentage), die cost calculations, and fab line performance tracking for P&L reporting in semiconductor manufacturing.']
  tapeout_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.tapeout. Business justification: Wafer maps are produced from wafers fabricated from a specific tapeout run; tapeout-level wafer map aggregation is required for tapeout yield reporting and design-to-silicon correlation analysis in se']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer associated with this map.']
  wafer_probe_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.wafer_probe_run. Business justification: REQUIRED: Test execution (wafer_probe_run) creates the wafer map; traceability report links map to its probe run for root‑cause analysis.']
  bin_count_total varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct bins used in the map.']
  compliance_iatf16949 boolean [note: 'Flag indicating compliance with IATF 16949 for this wafer map.']
  compliance_iso9001 boolean [note: 'Flag indicating compliance with ISO 9001 for this wafer map.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Record creation timestamp in the data lake.']
  defect_density_per_sqmm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average defect density across the wafer measured in defects per square millimeter.']
  defect_type varchar [note: 'Primary defect type identified on the wafer.']
  defect_zone varchar [note: 'Region of the wafer where defects are concentrated.']
  die_grid_columns varchar [note: 'Number of die columns in the wafer grid.']
  die_grid_rows varchar [note: 'Number of die rows in the wafer grid.']
  die_yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Yield percentage calculated from passing dies over total dies.']
  edge_exclusion_zone_mm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Width of the edge exclusion zone in millimeters where dies are not counted.']
  fail_bin_code varchar [note: 'Bin code representing failing (defective) dies.']
  failing_die_count varchar [note: 'Number of dies classified as failing.']
  flat_orientation varchar [note: 'Orientation of the wafer flat/notch relative to the map coordinate system.']
  is_kgd_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wafer passed Known Good Die certification.']
  kgd_certification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when KGD certification was applied.']
  map_checksum varchar [note: 'Checksum (e.g., SHA-256) of the map file for integrity verification.']
  map_file_path varchar [note: 'File system path or URI where the wafer map image/file is stored.']
  map_generation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the wafer map was generated.']
  map_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the wafer map.']
  map_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the map generation algorithm or software.']
  pass_bin_code varchar [note: 'Bin code representing passing (good) dies.']
  passing_die_count varchar [note: 'Number of dies classified as passing.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes about the wafer map.']
  total_die_count varchar [note: 'Total number of dies evaluated on the wafer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Record last update timestamp.']

  Note: 'Spatial defect and bin distribution map for an individual wafer within an inspection lot. Captures wafer ID, lot ID, die grid coordinates, per-die pass/fail bin assignment, defect density per zone, edge exclusion zone, flat/notch orientation, and map generation timestamp. Sourced from KLA ICOS and ATE wafer probing systems. Enables yield spatial analysis and systematic defect pattern detection.'
}

Table quality.yield_record {
  yield_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the yield record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Yield records are generated by cost center operations and tracked for process efficiency, cost-per-die calculations, and manufacturing cost center performance measurement.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment that performed the measurement.']
  final_test_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.final_test_run. Business justification: REQUIRED: Yield records reference the final test run that produced pass/fail counts; needed for yield vs. test performance dashboards.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Yield records track production yield by IC product for cost analysis, manufacturing efficiency monitoring, and product profitability assessment. Core operational metric.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Yield records must link to ic_design_project for yield-by-project NRE ROI analysis and design feedback. Yield records can exist for MPW shuttle runs without a direct tapeout record; direct project lin']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.inspection_lot.inspection_lot_id — Yield measurements are taken at quality gates associated with inspection lots. This link enables yield-to-defect correlation analysis.']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: Yield Reporting aggregates yield percentages per inventory lot for financial reporting and customer yield guarantees.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Yield records are reported per order line; FK supports performance metrics and customer yield reports.']
  lot_process_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.lot_process_run. Business justification: Each yield record captures yield data from a specific lot process run. yield_record already has process_step_id and recipe_id but lacks a direct link to the specific run instance. This enables yield t']
  pdk_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.pdk. Business justification: Yield records must reference PDK version for yield-by-PDK-version analysis, critical for process qualification and PDK release decisions. process_node is a denormalized PDK attribute; the FK replaces ']
  process_tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.tool_chamber. Business justification: Yield loss analysis requires chamber-level granularity. Multiple chambers per tool can have different yield signatures. Chamber-specific yield improvement initiatives and chamber matching programs dep']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Yield performance is reported per profit center to assess profitability of product lines.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Yield analysis per purchase order supports Yield Loss Category reports and supplier yield KPI tracking.']
  recipe_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the process recipe applied to the wafer.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Yield records are produced per SKU for production performance dashboards; FK to sku replaces denormalized product_sku column.']
  step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_step. Business justification: Yield records are aggregated per process step; linking provides step‑level yield analysis for continuous improvement.']
  tapeout_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.tapeout. Business justification: Supports Yield Analysis Dashboard linking yield data to the corresponding tapeout, essential for performance tracking and cost forecasting.']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the individual wafer.']
  wafer_map_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.wafer_map. Business justification: Yield records at the wafer probe stage are directly correlated with wafer maps — the spatial bin distribution on the wafer map is the source data for yield calculations. Linking yield_record to wafer_']
  wafer_probe_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.wafer_probe_run. Business justification: Yield calculated from wafer probe results. Links yield metrics to probe runs for yield analysis and probe program optimization. Critical for fab yield management.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the data acquisition batch.']
  bin_distribution_summary varchar [note: 'Compact representation (e.g., JSON) of die bin counts across test bins.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the measurement equipment was calibrated.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or observations related to the measurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the yield record was first created in the system.']
  defect_count varchar [note: 'Number of defects detected for the specified defect type.']
  defect_density_per_cm2 decimal(8,4) [note: 'Number of defects per square centimeter of wafer area.']
  defect_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the dominant defect observed.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the yield measurement was captured.']
  fab_line varchar [note: 'Production line identifier within the fab.']
  good_die_count varchar [note: 'Number of dies that passed all quality tests.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Overall pass/fail outcome of the inspection.']
  inspection_system varchar [note: 'Name of the inspection/metrology system used (e.g., KLA ICOS).']
  lot_humidity_percent decimal(4,2) [note: 'Relative humidity of the fab environment during measurement.']
  lot_origin varchar [note: 'Fab location code where the wafer lot originated.']
  lot_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the wafer lot.']
  lot_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average temperature of the wafer lot during measurement.']
  measurement_accuracy_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Stated accuracy of the measurement as a percentage.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Physical method used to obtain the yield measurement.']
  measurement_stage varchar [note: 'Quality gate at which the yield was measured.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the primary measurement (e.g., percent, count).']
  measurement_variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical variance observed across repeated measurements.']
  quality_gate varchar [note: 'Specific quality gate where the measurement was recorded.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Work shift during which the measurement was taken.']
  source_file_name varchar [note: 'Name of the source file or data feed that supplied the measurement.']
  test_time_seconds decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total time taken to perform the yield test.']
  tool_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the measurement tool.']
  total_die_count varchar [note: 'Total number of dies on the wafer lot.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the yield record.']
  yield_gap_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Difference between actual yield and target yield.']
  yield_loss_category varchar [note: 'Root cause classification for yield loss.']
  yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Yield expressed as a percentage of good dies over total dies.']
  yield_record_status varchar [note: 'Current validation status of the yield record.']
  yield_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target yield percentage defined for the product/process.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing yield measurement outcomes at each quality gate: wafer probe (die sort yield), final test yield, and packaged unit yield. Stores wafer lot ID, process node, product SKU, total die count, good die count, yield percentage, bin distribution summary, yield loss category (random defect, systematic, parametric, test escape), and measurement timestamp. SSOT for yield tracking across the fab-to-finish flow.'
}

Table quality.spc_chart {
  spc_chart_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SPC chart record. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity does not fit standard role categories and is modeled as a custom data product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: SPC monitoring is a cost center activity with dedicated metrology resources, measurement equipment time, and process control engineering tracked for quality budgets.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: SPC charts monitor process parameters for specific IC products to ensure parametric compliance and detect process drift. Required for ISO9001 and IATF16949 compliance.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.inspection_lot.inspection_lot_id — SPC measurements are taken during inspection activities. Out-of-control SPC signals trigger inspection lot holds. This is the process control to inspection feedback loop.']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: SPC Dashboard monitors process control metrics linked to the specific inventory lot to trigger corrective actions.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: SPC charts monitoring incoming material parameters (resistivity, thickness, particle count) must link to material master for material lot acceptance, supplier process capability (Cpk) tracking, and in']
  opc_rule_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.opc_rule_set. Business justification: OPC rule set defines target CD for the step; SPC chart must reference it for model‑based control limits per JEDEC standards.']
  pdk_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.pdk. Business justification: SPC charts monitor process parameters against PDK-defined design rule limits and process corners. Linking SPC data to PDK version enables correlation of process drift with design rule compliance, requ']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.test_program. Business justification: SPC charts monitor test program stability and process capability. Links parametric measurements to the test program generating them. Required for test program qualification and continuous monitoring p']
  quality_spec_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.spec.spec_id — SPC control limits are derived from product/process specifications. The spec product defines the acceptance criteria that SPC monitors against. This link is essential for automated OOC detection.']
  raw_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.raw_material. Business justification: SPC charts monitor raw material parameter stability (resistivity, purity, thickness). Incoming quality control processes require linking SPC data to raw material lots for supplier qualification, proce']
  step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_step. Business justification: SPC charts are generated per process step; linking enables automated retrieval of step metadata for control‑limit calculations.']
  tool_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: SPC Dashboard links each chart to the Fab Tool that generated the data, enabling tool‑level performance monitoring.']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific wafer within the lot.']
  assignable_cause_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the identified root cause for an out‑of‑control signal.']
  chart_type varchar [note: 'Statistical chart type used for the measurement series.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes or observations related to the measurement.']
  control_limit_center decimal(12,6) [note: 'Center line value (process mean) for the chart.']
  control_limit_lower decimal(12,6) [note: 'Calculated lower control limit for the chart.']
  control_limit_upper decimal(12,6) [note: 'Calculated upper control limit for the chart.']
  corrective_action_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corrective action taken to address the assignable cause.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the SPC chart record was created in the system.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the measurement data.']
  die_position varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual die (chip) measured.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this SPC chart configuration was retired or superseded.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this SPC chart configuration became effective.']
  is_baseline boolean [note: 'True if this chart serves as the baseline for comparative analysis.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Technique by which the measurement was obtained.']
  measurement_subgroup varchar [note: 'Logical grouping identifier for the sample (e.g., lot number, wafer ID).']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the measurement was recorded.']
  measurement_tool varchar [note: 'Equipment or system that performed the measurement.']
  measurement_value decimal(12,6) [note: 'Observed value of the monitored parameter for the sample.']
  out_of_control_flag boolean [note: 'True when the measurement falls outside control limits.']
  parameter_name varchar [note: 'Name of the process parameter being monitored (e.g., CD, Thickness, Resistivity).']
  parameter_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the monitored parameter (e.g., nm, µm, %).']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of units in each sample subgroup.']
  sampling_frequency varchar [note: 'How often samples are taken for this SPC chart.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Work shift during which the measurement was taken.']
  spc_chart_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the SPC chart record.']
  spc_to_metrology varchar [note: 'FK to quality.metrology_measurement.metrology_measurement_id — SPC charts are populated by metrology measurements. This is the fundamental data flow from measurement to statistical control.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the SPC chart record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the control plan applied to this chart.']

  Note: 'Statistical Process Control record encompassing both the control plan definition and ongoing chart measurements for a specific process step and tool combination. Captures control plan parameters (monitored parameters, chart type, sampling frequency, sample size, reaction plan), control chart data (X-bar/R, CUSUM, EWMA values), UCL/LCL/CL values, out-of-control signal flags, assignable cause codes, and corrective action references. Supports IATF 16949 and ISO 9001 SPC requirements for process stability monitoring. SSOT for SPC configuration and measurement tracking.'
}

Table quality.fmea_record {
  fmea_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: FMEA activities are cost center engineering work with labor hours and tool time tracked for process improvement budgets and quality engineering resource allocation.']
  design_ip_core_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.design_ip_core. Business justification: FMEA records are scoped to specific IP cores (memory, PHY, SerDes) for IP-level failure mode analysis. Semiconductor IP qualification programs require IP-core-level FMEA traceability for reuse risk as']
  detection_test_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.test_program. Business justification: FMEA identifies failure modes that test programs must detect. Links detection methods to test programs, enabling detection rating validation and test coverage gap analysis per IATF 16949.']
  equipment_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: FMEA records document failure modes for process steps executed on specific tools. Tool-specific risk assessment drives preventive maintenance planning and equipment qualification requirements in semic']
  equipment_tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.tool_chamber. Business justification: Chamber-level failure modes (gas flow instability, temperature uniformity) require chamber-specific FMEA. Chamber-level risk prioritization drives chamber PM optimization and chamber matching programs']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: FMEA records analyze failure modes at IC product level for design robustness and process risk assessment. Required for automotive qualification (IATF16949) and product safety.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Design FMEA (DFMEA) is performed per design project for IATF 16949 and ISO 26262 compliance. Linking fmea_record to ic_design_project enables design-level failure mode traceability required by automot']
  obligation_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation_register. Business justification: FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) is mandated by automotive (IATF 16949, AIAG FMEA-4) and aerospace regulatory obligations. Real process: obligation register defines which product lines require']
  process_flow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_process_flow. Business justification: FMEA is conducted at both process step level (already linked) and process flow level for system-level risk analysis. Semiconductor process FMEAs cover entire process flows to identify cross-step failu']
  process_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.process_node. Business justification: FMEA analyzes process node failure modes, lithography risks, and process integration challenges. Essential for process risk assessment and technology development.']
  process_step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key hint to the manufacturing process step entity.']
  quality_qualification_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.qualification_program.qualification_program_id — FMEAs feed into qualification programs. IATF 16949 requires FMEA linkage to qualification activities.']
  sales_design_win_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_design_win. Business justification: Design‑win risk management requires associating the FMEA with the winning design to monitor failure modes for the sold product.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key hint to the product master record associated with this FMEA.']
  action_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person accountable for implementing the recommended action.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the FMEA was formally approved by quality management.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual who granted final approval.']
  cause_of_failure varchar [note: 'Identified underlying cause(s) that could lead to the failure mode.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Open text field for any supplemental information or notes.']
  compliance_standards varchar [note: 'Enumerated list of quality or industry standards governing the FMEA.']
  control_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the FMEA to the relevant control plan document.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who entered the FMEA record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the FMEA record was initially entered into the system.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Technique or inspection method employed to discover the failure during manufacturing or testing.']
  detection_rating varchar [note: 'Numerical rating (1‑10) indicating how likely the failure will be detected before it reaches the customer.']
  detection_technique varchar [note: 'Specific detection approach (e.g., visual inspection, automated metrology).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the FMEA record is considered active for risk management.']
  failure_mode_description varchar [note: 'Narrative describing how the failure can occur within the product or process.']
  fmea_code varchar [note: 'Business‑level code used to reference the FMEA in reports and change management systems.']
  fmea_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name that identifies the FMEA, often reflecting the product or process it evaluates.']
  fmea_record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the FMEA record, tracking its progression from creation to retirement.']
  fmea_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative defining the boundaries (product, process, geography) covered by the analysis.']
  fmea_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the record is a Design FMEA (DFMEA) or Process FMEA (PFMEA).']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the failure mode is deemed critical to product safety or performance.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who performed the most recent update.']
  occurrence_rating varchar [note: 'Numerical rating (1‑10) representing the probability that the failure mode will occur.']
  post_action_rpn varchar [note: 'Re‑calculated risk priority number after corrective actions have been applied.']
  potential_effect varchar [note: 'Explanation of the impact on the customer if the failure mode materializes.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Text describing the action(s) proposed to mitigate the identified failure mode.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier tracking changes to the FMEA over time.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the failure mode origin.']
  risk_priority_number varchar [note: 'Calculated risk priority number derived from severity, occurrence, and detection ratings.']
  severity_rating varchar [note: 'Numerical rating (1‑10) of the seriousness of the failure effect on the end customer.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the recommended action should be finished.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the latest modification to the FMEA record.']

  Note: 'Failure Mode and Effects Analysis record documenting potential failure modes for a product design or manufacturing process step. Captures failure mode description, potential effect on end customer, severity rating (S), occurrence probability (O), detection rating (D), RPN (Risk Priority Number = S×O×D), recommended actions, responsible owner, target completion date, and post-action revised RPN. Supports IATF 16949 DFMEA and PFMEA requirements.'
}

  obligation_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation_register.   pdk_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.pdk. Business justification: Reliability test conditions (voltage stress, temperature) are PDK-node-specific. Linking reliability_test to PDK enables node-level reliability qualification tracking required for PDK release certific']

  process_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.process_node. Business justification: Reliability tests validate process node robustness, TDDB, electromigration, and hot carrier injection. Critical for technology qualification and process reliability assessment.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Reliability testing programs are profit center expenses with direct P&L impact.   quality_qualification_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.qualification_program.qualification_program_id — Reliability tests are executed as part of qualification programs. This link is essential for tracking qualification completeness.']
  quality_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_spec. Business justification: Reliability tests verify compliance against quality specifications (JEDEC standards, AEC-Q100 requirements). The reliability_test has applicable_standard and pass_fail_criteria fields that reference q']
  reliability_test_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.reliability_test_run. Business justification: REQUIRED: Reliability test plan must be associated with each execution run to satisfy JEDEC reliability compliance and generate post‑run reports.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Reliability tests qualify specific SKUs (package/speed/voltage variants) for customer release. Essential for JEDEC qualification and automotive-grade certification at orderable-part level.']
  tapeout_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.tapeout. Business justification: Reliability test samples are fabricated from specific tapeout runs; linking enables tapeout-level reliability qualification tracking. Semiconductor qualification engineers must identify which tapeout ']
  test_maintenance_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.maintenance_plan. Business justification: Reliability test equipment requires specific maintenance plans to ensure test validity. Test equipment maintenance plan compliance is a regulatory requirement for JEDEC reliability testing and custome']
  test_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.test_plan. Business justification: Reliability tests are executed according to test plans that define the test methodology, pass/fail criteria, and test conditions. The reliability_test has pass_fail_criteria, test_type, and test_durat']
  test_tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.tool_chamber. Business justification: Reliability stress tests (HTOL, thermal cycling) run in specific chambers with controlled environments. Test validity and repeatability require chamber traceability. Chamber qualification status is ma']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Large reliability programs (automotive qualification, aerospace certification) are managed as projects with WBS tracking for milestone billing, customer invoicing, and R&D capitalization.']
  analysis_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to analyze the failed device.']
  applicable_standard varchar [note: 'Industry standard governing the qualification (e.g., AEC‑Q100, JEDEC JESD47).']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the person/committee approving the qualification.']
  compliance_iatf_16949 boolean [note: 'True if the test complies with automotive quality standard IATF 16949.']
  compliance_iso_9001 boolean [note: 'True if the test complies with ISO 9001 quality management requirements.']
  compliance_jedec boolean [note: 'True if the test follows applicable JEDEC reliability standards.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that supplied the reliability test data (e.g., KLA ICOS).']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Identifier for the device family or SKU under test.']
  failure_mechanism varchar [note: 'Physical mechanism responsible for the failure.']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Observed mode of failure for the device.']
  failure_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the device that failed during testing.']
  failure_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Time elapsed until failure, measured in hours.']
  fit_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Calculated failure rate expressed in FIT (10⁹ hours).']
  fit_rate_confidence decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level for the reported FIT rate.']
  is_kgd_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the device is Known Good Die (KGD) certified.']
  milestone_schedule varchar [note: 'Key milestones and dates for the qualification program.']
  operator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the technician who ran the test.']
  overall_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the qualification program.']
  pass_fail_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rule defining pass or fail for the test (e.g., max failure rate).']
  qualification_plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the qualification plan document.']
  qualification_type varchar [note: 'Type of qualification driving the reliability test.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reliability test record was created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reliability test record.']
  reliability_grade varchar [note: 'Qualitative reliability grade assigned to the device.']
  root_cause_classification varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the root cause.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of devices subjected to the test.']
  test_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total duration of the stress test, in hours.']
  test_execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the test was executed.']
  test_humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity level during the test, expressed as a percentage.']
  test_location varchar [note: 'Facility or fab where the test was performed.']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Overall pass/fail outcome of the test.']
  test_status varchar [note: 'Current execution status of the test.']
  test_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature set point for the stress test, in degrees Celsius.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Stress test methodology applied.']
  test_voltage_v decimal(5,2) [note: 'Voltage applied during the stress test, in volts.']
  weibull_scale_parameter decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scale parameter of the Weibull reliability model.']
  weibull_shape_parameter decimal(6,3) [note: 'Shape parameter of the Weibull reliability model.']

  Note: 'Master record for reliability qualification programs and individual test execution, encompassing program definition, test planning, stress test execution, and failure event tracking. At the program level: captures qualification type (new product, process change, OSAT qualification, PCN-driven), applicable standards (AEC-Q100, JEDEC JESD47, ISO 9001), qualification plan version, milestone schedule, overall qualification status, and approval authority. At the test level: captures test type (HTOL, HAST, TC, ESD, JEDEC stress tests), test conditions (temperature, voltage, humidity, duration), sample size, device type, pass/fail criteria, and individual failure details including failed unit serial number, failure time (hours), failure mode, failure analysis method (SEM, FIB, EMMI, curve trace), root cause classification, and failure mechanism (electromigration, TDDB, HCI, NBTI). SSOT for JEDEC JESD47 and AEC-Q100/Q101 qualification lifecycle from program initiation through test execution to final disposition, FIT rate calculations, and Weibull reliability modeling.'
}

  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the qualification program.']

  program_budget_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Allocated budget for the qualification effort expressed in US dollars.']

  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Master record for a product or process qualification program governing the full set of qualification activities required before production release. Captures qualification type (new product, process change, OSAT qualification, PCN-driven), applicable standards (AEC-Q100, JEDEC JESD47, ISO 9001), qualification plan version, milestone schedule, overall qualification status, approval authority, and final disposition. Links to reliability tests, FMEA records, and PCN events.'
}

  total_units_shipped varchar [note: 'Total number of units shipped to the customer during the shipment period.']

  Note: 'Customer quality performance and communication record encompassing DPPM tracking, formal quality notifications (8D, SCAR, customer complaints), field return management, and customer-facing quality communications. Captures product part number, customer account, shipment period, total units shipped, defective units returned, DPPM value, notification type, failure description, containment actions, root cause, corrective/preventive actions, 8D report reference, response due date, and closure status. SSOT for all customer-facing quality metrics, notifications, and communications per IATF 16949 customer satisfaction monitoring. Integrates with Salesforce CRM for customer communication tracking.'
}

Table quality.capa_record {
  capa_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the CAPA record.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.audit.audit_id — Audit findings (now merged into audit) trigger CAPAs. audit_finding description states \'linked CAPA record\'. This is a mandatory operational link for ISO 9001 clause 10.2.']

  equipment_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: CAPA records for equipment-related issues require tool traceability.   ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Corrective actions often target IC product families for systemic quality issues, design improvements, and process optimization. Essential for product quality management and continuous improvement.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: CAPAs triggered by design-related failures (systematic yield loss, reliability failures traced to design) must reference the originating design project. IATF 16949 CAPA management requires design-proj']
  nonconformance_report_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.nonconformance_report.nonconformance_report_id — CAPAs are triggered by nonconformances. This is a critical traceability link for ISO 9001 clause 10.2 compliance.']
  obligation_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation_register. Business justification: CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) records are generated in response to regulatory obligation violations and audit findings. Real process: obligation register entries trigger CAPA when compliance']
  process_step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_process_step. Business justification: CAPAs address root causes at specific fabrication process steps. Semiconductor quality engineers require direct traceability from CAPA records to the process step being corrected for effectiveness ver']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: CAPA records have financial impact (cost_actual, cost_estimate) tracked by profit center for cost-of-quality metrics, operational efficiency KPIs, and continuous improvement ROI.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.test_program. Business justification: CAPAs often require test program updates as corrective actions. Links quality improvements to test program changes. Essential for CAPA effectiveness verification and change control.']
  quality_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_spec. Business justification: CAPA records address quality issues that typically involve violations of quality specifications.   raw_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.raw_material. Business justification: CAPA records address raw material quality issues (contamination, out-of-spec).   sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product or design associated with the CAPA.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: CAPAs originating from supplier quality issues (material defects, delivery failures) must link to supplier for 8D report tracking, supplier corrective action requests (SCAR), and supplier scorecard im']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the action was actually completed.']
  attachment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference (e.g., URL or file ID) to supporting documents or images.']
  capa_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the CAPA for tracking and reference.']
  capa_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CAPA record.']
  capa_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the action is corrective or preventive.']
  closure_approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the CAPA closure after verification.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the CAPA was formally closed.']
  compliance_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific compliance clause or standard (e.g., ISO 9001, IATF 16949) governing the CAPA.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Specific actions planned or taken to eliminate the identified root cause.']
  cost_actual decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual financial cost incurred after implementation.']
  cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated financial cost to implement the corrective or preventive action.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CAPA record was initially created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for cost fields.']
  department varchar [note: 'Business department owning the CAPA (e.g., Quality, Engineering).']
  detection_date date [note: 'Date when the problem was first detected.']
  detection_phase varchar [note: 'Phase of the product lifecycle where the issue was detected.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'Source of detection (e.g., inspection, customer, audit).']
  effectiveness_verification_criteria varchar [note: 'Criteria and metrics used to verify that the CAPA was effective.']
  impact varchar [note: 'Description of the business or technical impact of the non‑conformance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments related to the CAPA.']
  preventive_action_description varchar [note: 'Actions designed to prevent recurrence of similar issues in the future.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking to schedule the CAPA work.']
  problem_statement varchar [note: 'Clear description of the problem or non‑conformance that triggered the CAPA.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp for when the record was first captured in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp for the most recent modification of the record.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk assessment of the issue before mitigation.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the identified root cause.']
  root_cause_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to determine the root cause (e.g., 5‑Why, Ishikawa, FTA, Pareto).']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the issue addressed by the CAPA.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the corrective or preventive action should be completed.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the effectiveness of the CAPA was verified.']
  verification_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the effectiveness verification.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the CAPA record.']

  Note: 'Corrective and Preventive Action record managing the full lifecycle of a quality improvement action triggered by a defect, customer complaint, audit finding, or reliability failure. Captures problem statement, root cause analysis method (5-Why, Ishikawa, FTA), root cause description, corrective action plan, preventive action plan, implementation owner, target and actual completion dates, effectiveness verification criteria, and closure approval. Supports ISO 9001 clause 10.2 and IATF 16949 CAPA requirements.'
}

Table quality.nonconformance_report {
  nonconformance_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the non‑conformance report.']

  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.audit. Business justification: Nonconformance reports are frequently generated as findings from quality audits. An audit finding that identifies a nonconformance results in an NCR being raised. Linking nonconformance_report to the ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Nonconformance handling expenses are tracked against a cost center for financial impact analysis.']
  defect_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.defect_record. Business justification: Nonconformance reports are triggered by specific defect events. The NCR documents the product or process deviation that was detected, which originates from a defect record. Linking nonconformance_repo']
  die_bank_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.die_bank. Business justification: Nonconformance reports at die level trigger die bank holds and disposition decisions. MRB (Material Review Board) processes require direct linkage to die bank inventory for scrap/rework/use-as-is deci']
  equipment_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: NCRs often cite specific equipment as root cause. Equipment hold decisions and CAPA initiation depend on NCR-equipment linkage. Standard practice in ISO9001 and IATF16949 compliant semiconductor opera']
  equipment_tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.tool_chamber. Business justification: Chamber-specific nonconformances (chamber drift, contamination) require chamber traceability for corrective action. Chamber-level NCR disposition and chamber requalification decisions depend on this l']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Nonconformances affecting export‑controlled items must be reported against the specific export license for regulatory tracking.']
  final_test_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.final_test_run. Business justification: NCRs often originate from final test failures. Links nonconformances to the test run that detected them. Essential for test escape analysis and corrective action tracking per ISO 9001.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.goods_receipt. Enables traceability for MRB disposition, supplier charge-back, and receiving inspection a']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: NCMRs are issued for a specific IC part; linking to ic_catalog supports root‑cause analysis and customer notifications.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. IATF 1694']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.inspection_lot.inspection_lot_id — NCRs are typically discovered during inspection. This link provides traceability from the non-conformance back to the inspection event where it was detected.']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: NCR handling requires linking the report to the inventory lot to enforce holds, quarantine, and corrective action tracking.']

Semiconductor QMS systems require process step traceability on NCRs for root cause analysis, SPC rule violation track']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: NCRs impact profit center yield, scrap costs (impact_amount), and customer quality metrics tracked for P&L accountability and cost-of-quality reporting by business unit.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: NCR‑PO linkage needed for root‑cause analysis and supplier accountability in Nonconformance Reporting.']
  restricted_party_screening_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.restricted_party_screening. Business justification: Nonconformance reports for shipments may trigger restricted party screening when customer or destination is flagged during quality hold review. Real process: quality holds escalate to trade compliance']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: NCRs track defects at SKU level for customer shipments, material review board decisions, and disposition actions. Critical for shipment quality control and customer notification.']
  step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_step. Business justification: NCRs reference the process step where non‑conformance was detected, essential for corrective‑action workflow.']
  tapeout_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.tapeout. Business justification: NCRs scoped to specific tapeout submissions (e.g., GDS submission errors, mask data nonconformances) require direct tapeout traceability. Semiconductor foundry NCR management tracks nonconformances at']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the wafer where the issue was detected.']
  attached_document_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of document identifiers attached to the report.']
  audit_trail_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes capturing audit trail details.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard applicable to the report.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date for completion of corrective actions.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Planned corrective actions to prevent recurrence.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the corrective action implementation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  customer_notification_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the customer must be notified.']
  customer_notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the customer notification was sent.']
  detection_point varchar [note: 'Process step or system where the non‑conformance was first detected.']
  die_range varchar [note: 'Range of die numbers on the wafer affected (e.g., "100-200").']
  disposition_action_required varchar [note: 'Specific actions required to implement the chosen disposition.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision on how the non‑conforming material will be treated.']
  hold_initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the quality hold was placed.']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the lot was placed on hold.']
  hold_release_condition varchar [note: 'Condition(s) that must be satisfied to release the hold.']
  hold_released_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the quality hold was lifted (null if still active).']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Category of quality hold applied to the lot.']
  impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary impact of the non‑conformance.']
  impact_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the financial impact.']
  is_customer_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the non‑conformance impacts a customer.']
  mrb_decision varchar [note: 'Decision made by the MRB regarding the non‑conformance.']
  nonconformance_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the deviation from specification.']
  nonconformance_report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the report.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority assigned for handling the report.']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Business‑visible identifier (NCR number) assigned to the report.']
  report_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the non‑conformance was initially recorded.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Analysis identifying the underlying cause of the non‑conformance.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the non‑conformance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the report (e.g., Camstar MES, KLA ICOS).']
  specification_violated varchar [note: 'Name or code of the specification that was not met.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']

  Note: 'Non-Conformance Report (NCR) and quality hold management record documenting product or process deviations from specification, managing lot hold/release lifecycle, and tracking material review board dispositions. Captures NCR number, affected lot/wafer/unit range, non-conformance description, specification violated, detection point, hold type (process excursion, SPC out-of-control, customer complaint, reliability failure), hold initiation and release timestamps, hold reason, hold release conditions with approver, disposition decision (use-as-is, rework, scrap, return to supplier), material review board (MRB) decision, responsible quality engineer, required disposition actions, and financial impact assessment. SSOT for all in-process quality escapes, quality holds, MRB dispositions, and lot release management. Integrates with Camstar MES and SAP QM for lot status management.'
}

At the finding level: captures individual observations, non-conformances, and opportunities for improvement with finding classification (major NC, minor NC, observation, OFI), finding description, applicable standard clause, corrective action linkage, responsible owner, due date, and closure status. SSOT for all quality audit planning, execution, and finding management per ISO 19011 and IATF 16949 clause 9.2.'
}

Business justification: Quality specifications must comply with legal entity-specific regulatory requirements (ISO 9001, IATF 16949, JEDEC) and are owned by specific legal entities for certification liability and customer co']

Business justification: Quality specs often specify chamber-level requirements (e.g., chamber uniformity specs, chamber matching criteria).   unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the parameter (e.g., mV, nm).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the specification record.']
  upper_limit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value for the parameter.']
  validated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the specification has been validated against actual parts.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the specification.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the quality specification for a product SKU or process step, including all acceptance criteria, parametric limits, visual inspection standards, and test coverage requirements. Captures spec version, applicable product family, process node, specification type (electrical, dimensional, visual, reliability), parameter name, nominal value, upper and lower spec limits, measurement units, and approval status. SSOT for quality acceptance criteria referenced by inspection lots and test programs.'
}

Table quality.failure_analysis_report {
  failure_analysis_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the failure analysis report.']
  analysis_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: FA reports document equipment-related failure mechanisms.   cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Failure analysis activities consume significant cost center resources including lab time, SEM/TEM equipment, FIB, and engineering hours tracked for quality engineering budgets.']
  defect_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.defect_record. Business justification: Failure analysis reports investigate specific defects identified in defect records. The failure_analysis_report has defect_code and failure_site_location fields that correspond to defect_record attrib']
  fabrication_process_recipe_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_process_recipe.
