  oee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated OEE metric for the chamber (percentage).']
  pressure_setpoint_pa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target pressure setting for the chamber during operation.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date when the chamber was last qualified.']
  qualification_result varchar [note: 'Detailed outcome of the qualification test (e.g., pass, fail, notes).']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent qualification run for the chamber.']
  safety_lock_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the chamber\'s safety interlock.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned serial number for the chamber.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version of the control software used by the chamber.']
  temperature_setpoint_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target temperature setting for the chamber during operation.']
  throughput_pph decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum parts‑per‑hour the chamber can process under nominal conditions.']
  tool_chamber_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the chamber\'s purpose, capabilities, or special notes.']
  tool_chamber_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the chamber.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer\'s warranty for the chamber ends.']

  Note: 'Individual process chamber or module within a multi-chamber fab tool (e.g., a CVD cluster tool with 4 deposition chambers, or an etch system with multiple etch modules). Tracks chamber-level qualification status, process recipe assignments, chamber-specific utilization, and maintenance history independently from the parent tool. Essential for chamber-level SPC and yield correlation in multi-chamber tools.'
}

Table equipment.tool_qualification {
  tool_qualification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each tool qualification record.']
  primary_tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific chamber or module within the tool that was qualified.']
  qualified_process_step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_step. Business justification: Tool qualifications are process-step-specific (etch qual, litho qual, implant qual). Production release, capacity planning, and WIP routing require step-level tool qualification status in semiconducto']
  recipe_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the process recipe used during qualification.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the qualification was formally approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the process engineer or manager who approved the qualification.']
  baseline_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Target baseline value that the tool must meet or exceed.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the measurement tool was last calibrated.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current calibration status of the measurement tool.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the change control request linked to this qualification.']
  compliance_approval_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance sign‑off status for the qualification.']
  compliance_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the compliance document associated with the qualification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the qualification record was created in the system.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to detailed qualification documentation or report.']
  equipment_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number of the equipment.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tool is on the critical production path.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent preventive or corrective maintenance on the equipment.']
  maintenance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the equipment maintenance is current.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to capture the qualification metric.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations captured during qualification.']
  oee_impact decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated impact of the qualification on Overall Equipment Effectiveness, expressed as a percentage.']
  process_node varchar [note: 'Technology node (e.g., 7nm, 5nm) for which the tool is qualified.']
  qualification_end_date date [note: 'Date when the qualification activity completed.']
  qualification_location varchar [note: 'Plant or fab location where the qualification was performed.']
  qualification_protocol varchar [note: 'Name or code of the protocol used to qualify the tool.']
  qualification_reason varchar [note: 'Business driver for performing the qualification.']
  qualification_start_date date [note: 'Date when the qualification activity began.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the qualification process.']
  qualification_type varchar [note: 'Category of qualification performed on the tool.']
  qualification_validity_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the qualification remains valid from the start date.']
  qualification_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the qualification record, incremented on re‑qualifications.']
  result varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the qualification.']
  result_metric_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the result metric.']
  result_metric_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Measured value of the primary qualification metric (e.g., overlay error).']
  risk_assessment varchar [note: 'Risk level associated with the qualification outcome.']
  tolerance decimal(12,4) [note: 'Acceptable deviation from the baseline value.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the qualification record.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the qualification expires and must be renewed.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the qualification is considered valid for production.']

  Note: 'Qualification and certification records for fab tools and chambers against specific process nodes, recipes, and product families. Captures qualification type (initial qual, re-qual, process change qual), qualification protocol, pass/fail criteria, baseline metrology results, approving process engineer, and qualification validity period. A tool must be qualified before it can run production wafer lots. Sourced from Oracle Agile PLM and SmartFactory MES.'
}

Ensures preventive maintenance intervals satisfy certificati']

  required_spare_part_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the spare part that must be available for the maintenance.']

Chamber']
  compliance_requirement varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard that the maintenance activity must satisfy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the schedule record was first created in the system.']
  estimated_downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned equipment downtime in minutes for the maintenance activity.']
  interval_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the interval value (day, week, month, wafer, or run).']
  interval_value varchar [note: 'Numeric value defining the interval between maintenance events (e.g., 500 wafers, 1000 runs, 30 days).']

  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the schedule record.']
  last_performed_date date [note: 'Date on which the maintenance activity was last executed.']

  maintenance_window_start timestamp [note: 'Planned start time of the maintenance window.']

  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the maintenance activity.']
  required_technician_skill_level varchar [note: 'Skill level required of the technician performing the maintenance.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to uniquely reference the schedule across systems.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the maintenance schedule.']
  scheduling_constraint varchar [note: 'Business rule or production window constraint that influences when the PM can be run.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the schedule record.']
  work_order_template_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work‑order template used to generate PM work orders.']

  Note: 'Preventive maintenance (PM) schedule master defining planned PM activities for each fab tool or chamber. Drives the PM work order generation cadence in SAP PM module.'
}

Table equipment.maintenance_event {
  maintenance_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the maintenance event record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Maintenance events incur direct labor and parts costs that must be charged to specific cost centers for activity-based costing, variance analysis, and monthly maintenance cost rollups by organizationa']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Major maintenance projects (equipment upgrades, overhauls, relocations) are tracked as internal orders for CAPEX vs OPEX classification, project accounting, and eventual settlement to fixed assets per']
  maintenance_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the scheduled maintenance plan governing this event.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Maintenance consumes materials (lubricants, cleaning chemicals, consumable parts) tracked in material master. Essential for maintenance cost accounting, inventory planning, and MRO material management']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.pm_schedule.pm_schedule_id — Scheduled PM events must link back to the PM schedule that triggered them, enabling PM compliance tracking.']
  primary_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.fab_tool.fab_tool_id — Every maintenance event must reference the tool serviced. Core operational FK for maintenance history and MTTR calculation.']
  primary_spare_part_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.spare_part.spare_part_id — Maintenance events consume spare parts. This link enables parts consumption tracking and inventory depletion.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Maintenance events often procure parts/services via specific POs. Critical for tracking maintenance costs, supplier performance, and linking procurement to equipment uptime/downtime in fab operations.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Equipment upgrades, process modifications, or safety-related maintenance events trigger regulatory filings (EPA notifications, OSHA reports, process change submissions). Links maintenance actions to c']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab floor or site where the equipment resides.']
  tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.tool_chamber. Business justification: maintenance_event has multiple FKs to fab_tool but no FK to tool_chamber. In semiconductor fabs, maintenance events are frequently chamber-specific (e.g., replacing a chamber liner, cleaning a CVD cha']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Major equipment installation, relocation, or upgrade projects are tracked under WBS elements for project cost control, earned value management, and capital project tracking during fab expansions or te']
  baseline_change_flag boolean [note: 'True if the maintenance altered the equipment baseline for qualification.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes captured by technicians or supervisors.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework applicable to the maintenance activity.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  downtime_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total equipment downtime measured in minutes.']
  eco_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ECO that drove the modification, if applicable.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the maintenance work was completed.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier or ticket number assigned to the maintenance event.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance activity.']
  labor_cost_total decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost of labor based on labor hours and rate.']
  labor_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total technician labor time recorded for the event.']
  maintenance_category varchar [note: 'Strategic classification of the maintenance approach.']
  maintenance_event_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the maintenance event.']
  oee_impact_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated impact on Overall Equipment Effectiveness expressed as a percent.']
  parts_cost_total decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate cost of all spare parts and consumables used.']
  performance_improvement_target varchar [note: 'Planned performance gain (e.g., throughput increase) resulting from the event.']
  post_config_version varchar [note: 'Snapshot or version identifier of equipment configuration after maintenance.']
  pre_config_version varchar [note: 'Snapshot or version identifier of equipment configuration before maintenance.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the maintenance event record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the maintenance event record.']
  repair_action_description varchar [note: 'Narrative of the corrective actions performed during the event.']
  requalification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal re‑qualification is needed after the event.']
  requalification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any required re‑qualification process.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the underlying cause of failure.']
  root_cause_detail varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the specific root cause identified.']
  safety_incident_description varchar [note: 'Details of any safety incident associated with the maintenance.']
  safety_incident_flag boolean [note: 'True if the maintenance event involved a safety incident.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the maintenance work actually began.']
  technician_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the technician responsible for the maintenance.']
  total_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Combined cost of parts, labor, and any additional charges.']
  trigger_source varchar [note: 'Origin that caused the maintenance event to be created.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user identifier that last modified the record.']
  upgrade_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the event involved a hardware, software, or firmware upgrade.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System user identifier that created the record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every maintenance activity executed on a fab tool or chamber, including preventive maintenance (PM), corrective maintenance (CM), emergency breakdown repair, engineering-driven modifications, tool upgrades/retrofits, and hardware/software modifications. Captures event type, trigger (scheduled PM, alarm, operator-reported, FDC event, ECO), start/end timestamps, actual downtime duration, root cause classification (5-why, fishbone), repair actions taken, spare parts and consumables consumed (part ID, quantity, unit cost, planned vs unplanned), technician IDs, upgrade/retrofit details (upgrade type, ECO reference, pre/post configuration, performance improvement targets, baseline change flag), and return-to-service sign-off including re-qualification requirements. SSOT for tool downtime attribution, MTTR/MTBF calculation, parts consumption tracking, upgrade history, and total cost of ownership. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA PM and SmartFactory MES.'
}

Table equipment.tool_downtime {
  tool_downtime_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each downtime event record.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_wafer_lot. Business justification: Tool downtime directly impacts specific WIP lots in the fab. Cycle time impact analysis, hot-lot recovery planning, customer notification for affected orders, and OEE-to-yield correlation all require ']
  primary_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the fab tool or chamber that experienced the downtime.']
  primary_tool_maintenance_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work order associated with the downtime, if any.']
  tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.tool_chamber. Business justification: tool_downtime tracks downtime per SEMI E10 for both fab tools and individual chambers. It already has primary_fab_tool_id → fab_tool but lacks a chamber-level FK. Multi-chamber cluster tools (CVD, PVD']
  tool_maintenance_event_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.maintenance_event.maintenance_event_id — Downtime events caused by maintenance must link to the maintenance event for root cause tracing and planned vs unplanned analysis.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or observations related to the downtime event.']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Brief description of the corrective action performed to resolve the issue.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the downtime record was first created in the system.']
  downtime_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the downtime event expressed in whole minutes.']
  downtime_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the tool exited the downtime state.']
  downtime_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the root cause of the downtime.']
  downtime_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of why the tool was down.']
  downtime_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the tool entered the downtime state.']
  downtime_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the downtime according to the SEMI E10 state model.']
  impact_wip_lot_count varchar [note: 'Number of work‑in‑process lots affected by the downtime.']
  oee_impact_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated reduction in Overall Equipment Effectiveness caused by the downtime, expressed as a percentage.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Team or role accountable for handling the downtime event.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category describing the underlying cause of the downtime.']
  scheduled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the downtime was planned (true) or unplanned (false).']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the downtime event based on impact and urgency.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Production shift during which the downtime occurred.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the downtime data.']
  state_after varchar [note: 'Equipment state after the downtime event concluded.']
  state_before varchar [note: 'Equipment state immediately prior to the downtime event.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the downtime record.']

  Note: 'Granular downtime records for each fab tool and chamber classified per SEMI E10 equipment state model (Productive, Standby, Engineering, Scheduled Downtime, Unscheduled Downtime, Non-Scheduled). Captures state transition timestamps, downtime reason code, responsible party (maintenance, engineering, facilities, scheduling), and impact on WIP lots. Feeds OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation and capacity planning models.'
}

The tool_chamber table explicitly tracks calibration_date, calibration_status, and last_calibration_result, confirming th']
  triggering_maintenance_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.maintenance_event. Business justification: In semiconductor fabs, calibration is frequently triggered by maintenance events — after replacing a component, the tool must be recalibrated before returning to production. maintenance_event has requ']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the wafer on which the calibration measurement was taken.']
  calibration_interval_days varchar [note: 'Planned interval in days between successive calibrations for this equipment.']
  calibration_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to perform the calibration.']
  calibration_record_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the calibration record.']
  calibration_report_url varchar [note: 'Link to the detailed calibration report stored in the document repository.']
  calibration_result_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the calibration outcome for downstream analytics.']
  calibration_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated the record (e.g., KLA ICOS, SmartFactory MES).']
  calibration_standard varchar [note: 'Reference standard used for calibration (e.g., NIST traceable, SEMI E30).']
  calibration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the calibration activity was performed.']
  calibration_type varchar [note: 'Method of calibration applied to the equipment.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or observations captured by the technician.']
  compliance_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the compliance clause (e.g., ISO 9001, IATF 16949) satisfied by this calibration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the calibration record was first created in the system.']
  location varchar [note: 'Plant or fab line where the equipment resides (e.g., FAB1, LineA).']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer lot associated with the calibration (if applicable).']
  measured_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Value recorded by the instrument during calibration.']
  measurement_uncertainty decimal(12,4) [note: 'Estimated uncertainty of the measured value.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the measured and nominal values (e.g., nanometers, volts).']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the next calibration must be performed.']
  nominal_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Target or nominal value that the equipment should meet.']
  pass_fail_result varchar [note: 'Pass/Fail outcome of the calibration activity.']
  technician_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the calibration technician.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the calibration record.']

  Note: 'Calibration and metrology verification records for fab tools and measurement instruments. Tracks calibration type (in-situ, offline, periodic), calibration standard used, measured vs. nominal values, calibration pass/fail result, calibration interval, next due date, calibrating technician, and traceability to NIST or SEMI standards. Mandatory for ISO 9001 and IATF 16949 compliance. Sourced from KLA ICOS and SmartFactory MES calibration modules.'
}

Table equipment.equipment_process_recipe {
  equipment_process_recipe_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the equipment process recipe record.']
  change_notification_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the change request that introduced or modified this recipe version.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Process recipes are classified under ECCN codes; linking enables recipe‑level export control compliance checks.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Process recipes consume specific materials (gases, chemicals, photoresists) tracked in material master. Essential for BOM management, material planning, and process cost calculation in semiconductor f']
  pdk_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.pdk. Business justification: Each process recipe is defined for a specific PDK version; needed for the \'Recipe Management\' report linking recipes to PDKs.']
  primary_equipment_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: A recipe is owned by a specific fab tool; adding fab_tool_id enables direct navigation and removes redundant attributes that can be derived from the fab_tool record.']
  step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_step. Business justification: Equipment recipes are step-specific implementations of process intent. Recipe execution control, process integration, and traceability require step-level attribution in semiconductor manufacturing exe']
  technology_control_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.technology_control_plan. Business justification: Recipes containing controlled technology parameters (advanced lithography exposure settings, critical etch chemistries, sub-7nm process steps) require linkage to technology control plans for EAR/ITAR ']
  tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific equipment chamber where the recipe is executed.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the recipe within the change control process.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest audit (passed, failed, pending).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance state of the recipe (e.g., SEMI, ISO).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the recipe record was first created in the system.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to the detailed recipe documentation or PDF.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the recipe version is no longer valid (nullable for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the recipe version is valid for production.']
  exposure_dose_mj_cm2 decimal(6,2) [note: 'Energy dose applied during lithography exposure.']
  focus_offset_nm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Focus adjustment offset for the lithography tool, in nanometers.']
  gas_flow_rate_sccm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Standard cubic centimeters per minute flow rate for process gases.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recipe version is currently active for scheduling.']
  is_deprecated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recipe has been superseded and should no longer be used.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance audit of the recipe.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent execution of this recipe version.']
  oee_actual_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured OEE percentage achieved during the most recent execution.']
  oee_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Overall Equipment Effectiveness percentage for the recipe.']
  parameter_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct process parameters defined in the recipe.']
  parameter_set_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the key process parameters defined in the recipe.']
  pressure_setpoint_pa decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target chamber pressure for the process, in pascals.']
  process_node_target varchar [note: 'Technology node the recipe is intended for (e.g., 7nm, 5nm).']
  process_step_count varchar [note: 'Number of discrete process steps defined in the recipe.']
  recipe_category varchar [note: 'High‑level process domain the recipe belongs to.']
  recipe_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the recipe used in change management and scheduling.']
  recipe_hash varchar [note: 'Checksum or hash value used to verify recipe integrity.']
  recipe_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the process recipe.']
  recipe_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or group responsible for the recipe.']
  rf_power_watts decimal(6,2) [note: 'Radio‑frequency power setting for plasma processes, in watts.']
  safety_status varchar [note: 'Safety classification of the recipe based on hazardous materials or process risk.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the recipe record (e.g., SmartFactory MES).']
  spin_speed_rpm varchar [note: 'Rotational speed for spin‑coat steps, in revolutions per minute.']
  temperature_setpoint_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target temperature setting for the process step, in degrees Celsius.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the recipe record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version string of the recipe (e.g., v1.2, rev3).']
  version_history_note varchar [note: 'Free‑text note describing changes made in this version.']
  yield_actual_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual yield percentage observed in the latest run.']
  yield_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target good‑die yield percentage for the recipe.']

  Note: 'Master and execution records for process recipes on fab tools. Defines complete recipe parameters (temperature, pressure, gas flows, RF power, spin speed, exposure dose, focus offset, etc.) for each process step and tool type, including recipe name, version, process node target, tool compatibility, recipe owner, approval status, and effective date range. Also captures each recipe execution instance linking a specific wafer lot run to the recipe version used, actual as-run vs as-set parameter values, execution start/end timestamps, operator ID, chamber used, and pass/fail outcome. SSOT for recipe configuration, version management, recipe execution history, and recipe-to-yield correlation analysis. Sourced from SmartFactory MES and Camstar recipe management.'
}

Table equipment.recipe_execution {
  recipe_execution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each recipe execution event.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Each recipe execution consumes materials, utilities, and equipment time that must be allocated to cost centers for standard costing, actual vs. standard variance analysis, and production cost accounti']
  equipment_process_recipe_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.equipment_process_recipe.equipment_process_recipe_id — Each recipe execution must reference the recipe version used. Core traceability for yield correlation and process control.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer lot that was processed in this execution.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Recipe execution on a wafer lot must be tied to the design project it produces, used in the \'Production Tracking\' dashboard.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Recipe executions consume materials (process gases, chemicals) in real-time production. Critical for actual material consumption tracking, yield analysis, cost-per-wafer calculation, and supply chain ']
  primary_recipe_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab tool (e.g., lithography scanner, deposition system) where the recipe was executed.']
  recipe_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.fab_tool.fab_tool_id — Recipe executions must reference the tool where processing occurred for tool-specific performance analysis.']
  recipe_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the process recipe definition used for this execution.']
  step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_step. Business justification: Recipe executions occur at specific process steps in the flow. Lot traceability, SPC data collection, and process control decisions require step-level attribution in semiconductor MES systems.']
  tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific chamber or module inside the tool used for the execution.']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the individual wafer within the lot (e.g., Wafer 001).']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch number associated with the lot run.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the execution met all applicable process compliance rules.']
  duration_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total elapsed time of the execution in seconds (derived from start and end timestamps).']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the recipe execution completed or was terminated.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Category of equipment (e.g., \'Lithography\', \'CVD\', \'Etcher\').']
  execution_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the recipe execution: pass, fail, or abort.']
  gas_flow_actual_sccm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Measured gas flow rate during the run.']
  gas_flow_setpoint_sccm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target gas flow rate in standard cubic centimeters per minute.']
  is_calibrated boolean [note: 'True if the tool was calibrated immediately before execution; otherwise false.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the execution is for a critical process step (true) or not (false).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text field for operator comments or observations.']
  oee_availability_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Availability component of Overall Equipment Effectiveness for this execution.']
  oee_performance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Performance component of Overall Equipment Effectiveness for this execution.']
  oee_quality_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quality component of Overall Equipment Effectiveness for this execution.']
  power_actual_w decimal(8,2) [note: 'Measured power delivered during execution.']
  power_setpoint_w decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target power level for plasma or laser processes, in watts.']
  pressure_actual_pa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured chamber pressure during execution.']
  pressure_setpoint_pa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target chamber pressure for the recipe, in pascals.']
  process_step varchar [note: 'Logical name of the process step within the overall flow (e.g., \'Etch\', \'Deposition\').']
  recipe_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the recipe (e.g., v3.2.1) applied during the run.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this execution record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this execution record.']
  result_code varchar [note: 'Machine‑generated code providing detailed reason for pass/fail/abort.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the execution within the batch.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the recipe execution started.']
  temperature_actual_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured temperature achieved during execution.']
  temperature_setpoint_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target temperature for the process step, in degrees Celsius.']
  time_actual_sec decimal(8,2) [note: 'Measured duration the step actually ran.']
  time_setpoint_sec decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target duration for the process step, in seconds.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each process recipe execution on a fab tool or chamber, linking a specific wafer lot run to the recipe version used, actual process parameter values recorded (as-run vs. as-set), execution start/end timestamps, operator ID, and pass/fail outcome. Enables recipe-to-yield correlation analysis and supports SPC control chart population. Sourced from SmartFactory MES and Camstar lot history.'
}

  part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned part number that uniquely identifies the component across suppliers.']

  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the manufacturer\'s warranty for the part expires.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who created the record.']

  Note: 'Master record for spare parts and consumables inventory specific to fab tool maintenance, including critical spare parts (e.g., focus rings, edge rings, quartz liners, lamp assemblies, pump kits), their OEM part numbers, compatible tool types, minimum stock levels, lead times, and storage location in the FAB stockroom. Supports PM planning and emergency repair readiness. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM module.'
}

Business justification: Metrology runs are performed to measure the results of process recipe executions — e.g., measuring film thickness after a CVD recipe execution, or CD measurement after a lithography recipe execution. ']
  recipe_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the measurement recipe used for this run.']
  tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.tool_chamber. Business justification: metrology_run has three FKs to fab_tool but no FK to tool_chamber. Metrology and inspection runs are executed on specific chambers of multi-chamber tools (e.g., a specific etch chamber on a cluster to']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the individual wafer within the lot.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current calibration state of the metrology tool at run time.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the operator or engineer.']

  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure associated with the measurement type.']
  metrology_run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the run.']
  oee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated OEE for the tool during the run, expressed as a percentage.']
  pass_fail varchar [note: 'Overall pass/fail result of the run against spec limits.']
  run_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time of the metrology run in seconds.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the metrology run, e.g., MR000123.']
  run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the metrology run was executed on the tool.']

  spec_limit_low decimal(12,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the acceptable specification window for the measurement.']
  std_dev decimal(12,4) [note: 'Standard deviation of the measured values across all sites.']
  temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature of the tool chamber during the run (°C).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the run record.']

  Note: 'Metrology and inspection run records capturing in-line and off-line measurement results from metrology tools (CD-SEM, OCD, ellipsometer, profilometer) and inspection systems (KLA ICOS, brightfield/darkfield inspection). Records measurement recipe, wafer lot and site map, measured parameter (CD, film thickness, overlay, defect density), measured values per site, mean/3-sigma statistics, and pass/fail disposition against spec limits. SSOT for process control metrology data. Sourced from KLA ICOS and SmartFactory MES.'
}

Maintenance plans in semiconductor fabs are often defined at the chamber level (e.g., a specific maintenance plan for a CVD chamb']
  calibration_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between required calibration activities.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard governing the maintenance activities.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the cost estimate; default is USD.']
  cost_estimate_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected monetary cost to execute the maintenance plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the maintenance plan record was created in the system.']

  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the maintenance plan becomes effective.']
  equipment_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of equipment the plan applies to.']
  frequency_unit varchar [note: 'Time unit for the frequency value.']
  frequency_value varchar [note: 'Numeric interval defining how often the maintenance activity recurs.']

  maintenance_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the maintenance plan.']
  maintenance_task_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the primary maintenance activities covered by the plan.']
  maintenance_window_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Typical duration of a maintenance event expressed in hours.']
  mean_time_between_failures_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Average operating time between equipment failures, used for reliability analysis.']

  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions related to the plan.']

  safety_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed safety risk associated with the maintenance plan.']

  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment warranty expires, influencing maintenance decisions.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the maintenance plan record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for maintenance_plan. Referenced by maintenance_plan_id.'
}

Table inventory.inventory_wafer_lot {
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the wafer lot master record within the inventory domain. Primary key for the inventory_wafer_lot data product. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — this entity represents a discrete WIP business event (a lot moving through FAB) with a clear lifecycle, party references, timestamps, and quantitative results.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer for whom this wafer lot is being fabricated. Applicable for foundry/OSAT business models where lots are customer-specific. Null for internal product lots. This is the PARTY_REFERENCE category field for this TRANSACTION_HEADER entity.']
  compliance_cert_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.compliance_cert. Business justification: Wafer lots may require compliance certifications (ITAR, REACH, RoHS) for export/shipment. Cert status tracking for trade compliance and regulatory reporting requires this link.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for allocating wafer‑lot production costs to a cost center for internal cost‑of‑goods‑sold reporting.']
  customer_design_win_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_design_win. Business justification: Required for Production Tracking Report linking each wafer lot to the originating customer design win for revenue and yield attribution.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: ECCN classification is needed for export control of wafer lots; linking lot to its ECCN record enables automated compliance checks and reporting.']
  errata_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.errata. Business justification: Wafer lots may be affected by errata (silicon bugs). Errata impact on WIP for disposition, customer notification, and workaround implementation requires this link.']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Export License Management requires each wafer lot to be tied to the specific export license authorizing its shipment, a standard practice in semiconductor export compliance.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific equipment unit currently processing or last processing this wafer lot. Enables equipment-to-lot traceability for defect root cause analysis and equipment qualification tracking.']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Wafer lots produce die for specific product families. Revenue forecasting, capacity allocation, and family-level WIP tracking require this link for business planning.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Required for Wafer Lot Production Traceability Report linking each wafer lot to its IC catalog; replaces denormalized product_code with a proper FK.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Wafer‑lot allocation to a specific IC design project is required for production planning and capacity reports.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Wafer lot ownership by legal entity required for transfer pricing, customs valuation, intercompany transactions, and consolidated financial reporting in multi-entity semiconductor operations.']
  ltb_notification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_ltb_notification. Business justification: Wafer lots may be affected by LTB notifications (product discontinuance). LTB impact on WIP for disposition, customer notification, and last-time-buy planning requires this link.']
  mpw_shuttle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MPW shuttle run if this lot is part of a multi-project wafer program. MPW lots share a single wafer with multiple customer designs to reduce NRE costs. Null for dedicated production lots.']
  pcn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.pcn. Business justification: Wafer lots may be affected by PCNs (process changes). PCN impact on WIP for disposition decisions, customer notification, and change management requires this link.']
  process_flow_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the MES process route (flow plan) assigned to this wafer lot, defining the ordered sequence of operations from wafer start through final FAB step. Determines which process steps, equipment, and recipes apply to the lot.']
  process_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.process_node. Business justification: Wafer lots are fabricated on specific process nodes. Yield tracking, technology migration, capacity planning, and fab analytics require direct process node linkage.']
  product_qualification_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_qualification_program. Business justification: Wafer lots may be part of qualification programs (reliability testing, customer qual). Qual program participation tracking for certification and production release requires this link.']
  product_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_spec. Business justification: Wafer lots are fabricated to meet product specs. Yield analysis, process control, and spec compliance tracking for production release require product_spec linkage.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Enables profitability analysis per wafer lot by linking to the profit center that owns the product line.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP SD sales order that triggered this wafer lot start. Links WIP inventory to customer demand for order-to-cash traceability and delivery commitment tracking.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.sku']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: WIP wafer lots are physically stored in specific cleanroom storage locations within the FAB. The existing storage_location_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference that should be replaced by a proper']
  tapeout_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tapeout record (final design submission for manufacturing) associated with this wafer lot. Links WIP inventory to the design domain for design-to-silicon traceability and first silicon yield analysis.']
  wafer_start_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the wafer start authorization record that approved and initiated this lot. Provides traceability from WIP inventory back to the demand signal and capacity authorization event.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Wafer lots produced for customer projects or NRE work track to WBS elements for project costing, margin analysis, and milestone billing.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP work center or MES equipment group where the lot is currently queued or being processed. Enables WIP queue analysis and bottleneck identification across FEOL, MOL, and BEOL process stages.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the wafer lot completed all FAB process steps. Populated upon lot completion. Used for cycle time actuals, on-time delivery KPI calculation, and process improvement analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this wafer lot master record was first created in the inventory data product (Silver layer). Represents the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for this TRANSACTION_HEADER entity. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  current_operation_step varchar [note: 'The current process operation step name or code at which the wafer lot is positioned in the process flow (e.g., \'FEOL_Gate_Oxide\', \'MOL_Contact_Etch\', \'BEOL_Metal1_CVD\'). Sourced from the MES process flow definition. Critical for WIP traceability and cycle time analysis.']
  die_per_wafer varchar [note: 'Theoretical number of die that can be patterned on a single wafer for this product at the given process node and die size. Used as the denominator for yield calculations and finished goods quantity forecasting.']
  good_wafer_count varchar [note: 'Number of wafers in the lot that have passed all in-line inspection and metrology checks to date. Represents the current count of wafers expected to yield good die. Updated after each inspection or disposition event.']
  hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wafer lot is currently placed on hold and cannot proceed to the next process step. A True value means the lot is blocked pending engineering review, quality disposition, or management approval. Sourced from MES hold management.']
  hold_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for the current or most recent hold placed on the wafer lot (e.g., \'PROCESS_EXCURSION\', \'EQUIPMENT_FAILURE\', \'YIELD_LOSS\', \'CUSTOMER_REQUEST\', \'ENGINEERING_REVIEW\'). Populated when hold_flag is True. Used for hold disposition tracking and FMEA analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PROCESS_EXCURSION|EQUIPMENT_FAILURE|YIELD_LOSS|CUSTOMER_REQUEST|ENGINEERING_REVIEW|MATERIAL_SHORTAGE|QUALITY_DISPOSITION — promote to reference product]']
  hold_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the most recent hold was placed on the wafer lot. Null if the lot has never been placed on hold. Used for hold duration tracking and SPC (Statistical Process Control) excursion response time measurement.']
  inventory_valuation_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Current standard cost valuation of the wafer lot in the inventory ledger, expressed in the functional currency. Calculated based on the number of wafers and the standard cost per wafer at the current process step. Used for WIP inventory valuation in SAP FI/CO and financial reporting under SOX.']
  lithography_type varchar [note: 'Type of photolithography exposure technology used for the critical layers of this wafer lot. EUV (Extreme Ultraviolet) is used for leading-edge nodes (sub-7nm); DUV (Deep Ultraviolet) for mature nodes. Determines equipment routing and cost structure.']
  lot_start_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the wafer lot was officially started (wafer start authorization executed) in the FAB. Used for cycle time measurement, WIP aging analysis, and on-time delivery tracking. This is the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP (date precision) category field.']
  lot_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the wafer lot within the FAB WIP flow. \'active\' indicates the lot is progressing through process steps; \'on_hold\' indicates a hold flag is set pending disposition; \'completed\' indicates all process steps are finished; \'scrapped\' indicates the lot has been disposed; \'cancelled\' indicates the lot was terminated before completion. This is the LIFECYCLE_STATUS category field.']
  lot_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the wafer lot by its manufacturing purpose. \'production\' lots are for customer shipment; \'engineering\' lots support process development; \'qualification\' lots validate new processes or products; \'monitor\' lots track process stability; \'reliability\' lots support JEDEC/IATF reliability testing. This is the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category field.']
  mes_lot_reference varchar [note: 'Native lot reference number as recorded in the Applied Materials SmartFactory or Camstar MES system. Enables direct cross-reference to the operational system of record for lot tracking, dispatch, and WIP management.']
  priority_class varchar [note: 'Dispatch priority classification assigned to the wafer lot, used by MES to sequence lot movement through FAB equipment queues. \'hot\' lots receive highest priority for customer-critical or yield-recovery situations; \'expedite\' for accelerated delivery; \'standard\' for normal flow; \'low\' for non-urgent engineering runs.']
  process_stage varchar [note: 'High-level semiconductor fabrication stage classification: FEOL (Front End of Line) covers transistor formation; MOL (Middle of Line) covers local interconnects; BEOL (Back End of Line) covers metal interconnect layers. Used for WIP stage-gate reporting and cycle time decomposition.']
  route_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the process route assigned to this lot. Tracks which revision of the process flow was active at lot start, supporting process change impact analysis and PCN (Product Change Notification) traceability.']
  scrap_wafer_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of wafers scrapped from this lot since lot creation. Includes wafers scrapped due to process excursions, equipment failures, or quality dispositions. Used for yield loss analysis and DPPM (Defective Parts Per Million) reporting.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this wafer lot record was sourced (e.g., CAMSTAR for Camstar MES, SMARTFACTORY for Applied Materials SmartFactory, SAP_PP for SAP Production Planning). Supports data lineage and multi-system reconciliation in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the wafer lot is expected to complete all FAB process steps and be ready for wafer probe/test. Derived from the process flow cycle time and lot start date. Used for delivery commitment and capacity planning.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this wafer lot master record was last modified in the inventory data product. Represents the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category for this TRANSACTION_HEADER entity. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail.']
  valuation_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the inventory valuation amount (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, TWD). Required for multi-currency financial reporting and SOX compliance.']
  wafer_count_current varchar [note: 'Current number of wafers remaining in the lot after accounting for any scrapped or split wafers during processing. Reflects real-time WIP inventory quantity for this lot.']
  wafer_count_start varchar [note: 'Number of wafers in the lot at the time of lot creation (wafer start). Typically 25 wafers per FOUP/cassette for standard lots. Used as the baseline denominator for yield and scrap rate calculations. This is the QUANTITATIVE_RESULT / principal measured quantity for this WIP transaction.']
  wafer_diameter_mm varchar [note: 'Physical diameter of the silicon wafers in this lot, in millimeters. Standard values are 200mm (8-inch) and 300mm (12-inch). Determines equipment compatibility, cassette type, and cost per wafer.']

  Note: 'Master record for a wafer lot (batch of wafers) moving through the FAB as work-in-process inventory. Tracks lot ID, wafer count, process node, product code, lot type (production/engineering/qualification), priority class, current operation step, WIP status (active/hold/complete/scrapped), start date, target completion date, hold flags, cumulative scrap count, good wafer count, and MES lot reference. SSOT for WIP lot identity and traceability across FEOL, MOL, and BEOL process stages within the inventory domain.'
}

Table inventory.raw_material {
  raw_material_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a raw material master record in the semiconductor inventory domain. Primary key for the raw_material product.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Raw materials (chemicals, substrates, gases) require ECCN classification for international procurement, import/export documentation, and supplier compliance verification in semiconductor supply chain ']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Raw material ownership by legal entity required for inventory valuation, tax reporting, intercompany material transfers, and legal entity P&L allocation.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Raw_material (physical inventory) must link to material_master (supply chain master data) to inherit procurement parameters, quality specifications, lead times, compliance attributes, and valuation ru']
  process_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.process_node. Business justification: Raw materials (wafers, substrates) are qualified for specific process nodes. Procurement and supplier qualification depend on process node compatibility for fab operations.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'FK to inventory.storage_location.storage_location_id — Raw materials need a default storage location for receiving and replenishment workflows.']
  substance_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.substance_inventory. Business justification: Raw materials must link to substance inventory for REACH SVHC tracking, RoHS compliance verification, conflict minerals reporting, and hazardous material safety data sheet management required by EU an']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: PROCUREMENT: Supplier of each raw material is required for qualification, lead‑time, and compliance tracking; experts expect a direct supplier_id FK.']
  base_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The primary unit of measure in which the raw material is stocked and consumed (e.g., EA for each wafer, KG for chemicals, L for liquids, M2 for photomasks). Corresponds to SAP MARA.MEINS (base unit of measure). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|L|M2|M3|PC|SHT|BOX|DRUM|CYLINDER — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  batch_managed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the raw material is managed at the batch/lot level in SAP inventory management. Batch management is mandatory for silicon wafers, photomasks, and shelf-life-controlled chemicals to enable full lot traceability from receipt through consumption. Corresponds to SAP MARA.XCHPF.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the raw material was manufactured or substantially transformed. Required for export control compliance (EAR/ITAR), customs declarations, and CHIPS Act domestic content tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the raw material master record was first created in the system of record (SAP S/4HANA MM). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with SOX internal controls over financial reporting.']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'Date on which the raw material is scheduled to be discontinued or reaches end-of-life (EOL). Triggers last-time-buy (LTB) procurement actions and product change notification (PCN) processes. Null if the material is not scheduled for discontinuation.']
  fixed_lot_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fixed order quantity used when lot_size_type is \'fixed\'. Represents the standard purchase order quantity per replenishment cycle, often aligned with supplier minimum order quantities or packaging units. Corresponds to SAP MARC.BSTFE.']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'GHS/UN hazard classification of the raw material for safe storage, handling, and transport. Drives storage location assignment, PPE requirements, and emergency response procedures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: non_hazardous|flammable|corrosive|toxic|oxidizer|pyrophoric|compressed_gas|carcinogen — promote to reference product]']
  inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether incoming quality inspection (IQC) is mandatory upon goods receipt for this raw material. When true, received lots are placed in quality inspection stock in SAP QM before being released to unrestricted use. Corresponds to SAP MARC.QKZUL.']
  itar_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the raw material is subject to ITAR controls under the US Munitions List. ITAR-controlled materials require State Department licensing for export and impose strict access and handling restrictions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the raw material master record. Supports change tracking, delta extraction for the Silver layer, and audit compliance. Corresponds to SAP MARA.LAEDA (last change date).']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned procurement lead time in calendar days from purchase order placement to goods receipt. Used by SAP MRP for replenishment scheduling and safety stock calculations. Corresponds to SAP MARC.PLIFZ (planned delivery time).']
  lot_size_type varchar [note: 'SAP MRP lot-sizing procedure that determines how replenishment quantities are calculated. \'fixed\' orders a fixed quantity, \'lot_for_lot\' orders exact requirement, \'min_max\' uses min/max boundaries, \'periodic\' groups requirements by period. Corresponds to SAP MARC.DISMM.']
  max_stock_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum allowable inventory quantity for the raw material, used to prevent overstocking of hazardous materials, shelf-life-limited chemicals, or high-cost items. Corresponds to SAP MARC.MABST (maximum stock level).']
  min_remaining_shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days of remaining shelf life required at the time of goods receipt for the material to be accepted into inventory. Materials received with less than this remaining shelf life are rejected or placed in restricted use. Corresponds to SAP MARA.MHDLP.']
  moving_avg_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Current moving average price per base unit of measure, recalculated with each goods receipt based on actual purchase prices. Used for inventory valuation under the moving average price control method. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.VERPR.']
  price_control_type varchar [note: 'Determines the inventory valuation method for the material: \'standard\' uses a fixed standard price, \'moving_average\' recalculates price with each goods movement. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.VPRSV (price control indicator: S or V).']
  price_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the standard price and moving average price fields (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, TWD). Required for multi-currency financial reporting and SOX compliance.']
  purity_pct decimal(8,6) [note: 'Chemical purity of the raw material expressed as a percentage (e.g., 99.9999 for 6N-grade specialty gases, 99.99 for ultra-high-purity chemicals). Critical quality specification for process chemicals, specialty gases, and ALD precursors where trace contamination causes yield loss.']
  reach_svhc_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the raw material contains or is classified as a Substance of Very High Concern (SVHC) under the EU REACH Regulation. Triggers mandatory disclosure obligations when SVHC concentration exceeds 0.1% w/w in articles.']
  reorder_point_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Inventory level at which a replenishment purchase requisition is automatically triggered in SAP MRP. When on-hand stock falls to or below this quantity, procurement is initiated to prevent production line stoppages. Corresponds to SAP MARC.MINBE (reorder point).']
  resistivity_ohm_cm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Electrical resistivity of the silicon wafer substrate in Ohm·cm, indicating doping concentration and conductivity type. Critical specification for FEOL process compatibility and device electrical performance. Null for non-wafer materials.']
  rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the raw material complies with the EU RoHS Directive (2011/65/EU) restricting the use of hazardous substances including lead, mercury, cadmium, hexavalent chromium, PBB, and PBDE. Required for materials used in products shipped to EU markets.']
  safety_stock_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum buffer stock quantity maintained to protect against supply disruptions, demand variability, and supplier lead time uncertainty. Represents the floor below which inventory should not fall under normal operating conditions. Corresponds to SAP MARC.EISBE (safety stock).']
  serialized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether individual units of the raw material are tracked by unique serial number. Applicable to high-value items such as photomasks and reticles where individual unit traceability is required for defect root cause analysis and reticle qualification tracking.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days the raw material can be stored from the date of manufacture or receipt before it is considered expired and must be quarantined or disposed of. Critical for photoresists, ALD precursors, and specialty chemicals with time-sensitive stability. Corresponds to SAP MARA.MHDRZ (shelf life in days).']
  standard_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard cost price per base unit of measure used for inventory valuation in SAP CO (Controlling) and FI (Financial Accounting). Used for WIP costing, variance analysis, and financial reporting. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.STPRS (standard price).']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Categorical storage environment required for the raw material. Drives warehouse location assignment and storage area classification. Examples: cleanroom for bare silicon wafers, refrigerated for certain photoresists, nitrogen_purge for moisture-sensitive ALD precursors. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ambient|refrigerated|frozen|cleanroom|nitrogen_purge|dark_storage|fireproof_cabinet — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  storage_humidity_max_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable relative humidity percentage for storage of the raw material. Particularly critical for silicon wafers, photomasks, and hygroscopic chemicals where moisture exposure causes defects or degradation.']
  storage_temp_max_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the raw material. Exceeding this threshold may degrade material quality, trigger safety hazards, or invalidate process qualification. Used for warehouse environmental compliance monitoring.']
  storage_temp_min_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the raw material. Used to configure environmental monitoring alerts in the warehouse management system and ensure compliance with material safety data sheet (SDS) requirements.']
  wafer_diameter_mm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Nominal diameter of the silicon wafer in millimeters (e.g., 200.0 for 8-inch, 300.0 for 12-inch). Applicable only to silicon wafer material class. Critical for process tool compatibility and fab line qualification. Null for non-wafer materials.']
  wafer_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the silicon wafer substrate type: bare_silicon (polished CZ), epitaxial (epi-layer deposited), soi (Silicon-on-Insulator), fz (Float Zone), or cz (Czochralski). Determines process compatibility and electrical characteristics. Null for non-wafer materials.']

  Note: 'Master record for raw materials consumed in semiconductor manufacturing including silicon wafers (bare and epitaxial), process chemicals, specialty gases, photomasks, CMP slurries, and ALD precursors. Captures material code, description, material class, unit of measure, supplier part number, shelf life, storage temperature range, hazard classification (RoHS/REACH), reorder point, and safety stock level. SSOT for raw material identity in the inventory domain.'
}

Table inventory.finished_good {
  finished_good_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the finished good master record in the inventory system. Primary key for the finished_good data product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Needed for Order Fulfillment process to associate shipped finished goods with the purchasing customer account for invoicing and compliance.']
  certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.certification. Business justification: Finished goods must be associated with product certifications to satisfy regulatory audits and customer qualification requirements.']
  compliance_cert_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.compliance_cert. Business justification: Finished goods require compliance certifications for customer shipment. Export control, regulatory compliance, and customer acceptance require cert linkage for trade operations.']
  configuration_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.configuration_rule. Business justification: Finished goods are configured instances (speed grade, voltage, package). Configuration tracking for order fulfillment, pricing, and product variant management requires this link.']
  conflict_minerals_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.conflict_minerals_declaration. Business justification: Finished products require conflict minerals declarations for SEC filing compliance, customer supply chain transparency requirements, and responsible sourcing audits. Mandatory for publicly-traded semi']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Finished ICs require ECCN classification for export license determination, customs declarations, shipping documentation, and customer compliance certificates. Critical for international sales and dist']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Finished goods belong to product families. Family-level finished goods inventory for product line profitability, portfolio management, and business unit reporting requires this link.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Finished goods are packaged/tested instances of IC catalog items. Design-level traceability for reliability analysis, PCN impact assessment, and datasheet compliance requires ic_catalog link.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Linking finished goods to their design project supports warranty tracking and post‑sale support processes.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Finished goods inventory ownership by legal entity mandatory for GAAP/IFRS financial statements, customs declarations, SOX inventory controls, and statutory reporting.']
  pcn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.pcn. Business justification: Finished goods may be affected by PCNs (package/test changes). PCN-affected inventory tracking for customer notification, rework planning, and change management requires this link.']
  process_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.process_node. Business justification: Finished goods are manufactured on specific process nodes. Technology mix analysis, yield correlation, obsolescence planning, and portfolio management require process node tracking.']
  product_qualification_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_qualification_program. Business justification: Finished goods may be qualification samples. Qual program participation tracking for customer acceptance, datasheet validation, and certification requires this link.']
  product_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_spec. Business justification: Finished goods are tested/binned to product specs. Datasheet compliance, customer acceptance, and quality assurance require product_spec linkage for test result validation.']
  reach_svhc_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.reach_svhc_declaration. Business justification: Finished goods require direct REACH SVHC declarations for customer-facing compliance documentation, EU market access authorization, and automotive/industrial qualification requirements. Essential for ']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SKU catalog entry in the product domain that defines the commercial product specification for this finished good.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse or distribution center where this finished good inventory is physically held, including OSAT consignment locations.']
  tapeout_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.tapeout. Business justification: Finished‑good traceability to the originating tapeout is needed for quality audits and regulatory compliance.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Finished goods produced under customer projects link to WBS for project margin analysis, cost recovery, and project profitability reporting.']
  aec_q_qualified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the finished good has been qualified to AEC-Q100 (IC), AEC-Q101 (discrete), or AEC-Q200 (passive) automotive reliability standards. Required for automotive supply chain design-in.']
  bin_code varchar [note: 'Test bin classification code assigned during wafer sort or final test indicating the performance and quality tier of the die/device. Bin codes map to specific product SKUs and determine commercial grade.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the finished good was manufactured or substantially transformed. Required for customs declarations, trade compliance, and preferential tariff determination.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the finished good master record was first created in the inventory system. Audit trail field for data governance and SOX compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the standard cost and inventory valuation of this finished good (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'TWD\').']
  date_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing date code in YYWW format (year + work week) indicating when the device was assembled and packaged. Used for shelf life tracking, lot traceability, and field failure analysis.']
  device_marking varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric marking laser-inscribed or printed on the physical package surface. Includes part number, speed grade, temperature grade, and manufacturer code. Used for traceability and counterfeit detection.']
  dppm_target decimal(10,2) [note: 'Quality target expressed as Defective Parts Per Million (DPPM) for outgoing quality of this finished good. Used in customer quality agreements and SPC monitoring. Automotive-grade typically requires <10 DPPM.']
  eol_date date [note: 'Announced End of Life (EOL) date after which the finished good will no longer be manufactured or sold. Triggers Last Time Buy (LTB) notifications to customers per JEDEC JESD48 standard.']
  hts_code varchar [note: 'Harmonized Tariff Schedule (HTS) classification code used for customs declaration, import/export duty determination, and trade statistics reporting. Follows the 10-digit US HTS format.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current inventory disposition status of the finished good lot. \'available\' indicates unrestricted stock; \'quarantine\' indicates pending quality review; \'reserved\' indicates allocated to an order; \'consignment\' indicates stock held at OSAT or customer site; \'hold\' indicates engineering or compliance hold.']
  inventory_valuation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to value this finished good inventory in the general ledger. \'standard\' = standard cost; \'moving_average\' = moving average price (MAP); \'fifo\' = First In First Out; \'lifo\' = Last In First Out.']
  itar_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the finished good is subject to International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) controls administered by the US State Department DDTC. ITAR-controlled items require specific export licenses and handling procedures.']
  kgd_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the die used in this finished good has been verified as a Known Good Die (KGD) through wafer-level testing prior to packaging. Critical for advanced packaging (CoWoS, InFO, chiplet) and high-reliability applications.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the finished good master record. Used for change data capture (CDC), data lineage tracking, and audit compliance.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Commercial lifecycle stage of the finished good. \'active\' = in production and available; \'nrnd\' = Not Recommended for New Designs; \'eol\' = End of Life (EOL) announced; \'ltb\' = Last Time Buy (LTB) window open; \'discontinued\' = no longer available.']
  lot_traceability_code varchar [note: 'Unique lot or batch identifier linking the finished good back to the wafer fabrication lot, enabling full forward and backward traceability through the manufacturing supply chain. Aligns with Camstar MES lot number.']
  msd_level varchar [note: 'Moisture Sensitivity Level (MSL) classification per IPC/JEDEC J-STD-020 indicating the device\'s sensitivity to ambient moisture during storage and handling. Level 1 = unlimited floor life; Level 6 = must be baked immediately before use. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|2a|3|4|5|5a|6 — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  package_body_size_mm varchar [note: 'Physical body dimensions of the semiconductor package expressed as length x width in millimeters (e.g., \'10x10\', \'5.5x5.5\'). Used for PCB design, handling equipment setup, and storage tray selection.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Physical semiconductor package format of the finished good (e.g., Ball Grid Array (BGA), Quad Flat No-lead (QFN), Wafer-Level Chip Scale Package (WLCSP), Chip on Wafer on Substrate (CoWoS), Integrated Fan-Out (InFO)). Determines handling, PCB footprint, and storage requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BGA|QFN|QFP|WLCSP|CoWoS|InFO|LGA|DIP|SOP|CSP|FCBGA|PGA — promote to reference product]']
  pin_count varchar [note: 'Total number of electrical pins, balls, or leads on the finished good package. Critical for PCB design compatibility, socket selection, and ATE (Automatic Test Equipment) configuration.']
  quantity_on_hand varchar [note: 'Current physical inventory quantity of this finished good in units (individual packaged devices). Reflects unrestricted stock available for order fulfillment as tracked in SAP MM inventory management.']
  reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the finished good complies with EU REACH Regulation (EC No 1907/2006) regarding chemical substance safety, including absence of Substances of Very High Concern (SVHC) above threshold concentrations.']
  rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the finished good complies with the EU RoHS Directive (2011/65/EU) restricting use of hazardous substances including lead, mercury, cadmium, and hexavalent chromium in electronic equipment.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Maximum allowable storage duration in days from the manufacturing date before the finished good must be baked, re-qualified, or scrapped. Particularly relevant for moisture-sensitive devices (MSD) per IPC/JEDEC J-STD-033.']
  shelf_life_expiry_date date [note: 'Calculated date on which the shelf life of this finished good lot expires, derived from the manufacturing date code plus shelf life days. Triggers re-bake or scrap workflow in SAP MM.']
  standard_cost decimal(18,6) [note: 'Standard cost per unit of the finished good in the functional currency as maintained in SAP CO (Controlling) for inventory valuation, cost of goods sold calculation, and variance analysis.']
