  hold_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the hold. Indicates the urgency and business impact of the hold (critical for safety or regulatory issues, high for customer impact, medium for process improvement, low for administrative holds).']
  hold_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the specific reason for the hold. Maps to internal quality management or MES reason code taxonomy (e.g., DEFECT_EXCURSION, SPC_VIOLATION, CUSTOMER_RETURN, ITAR_REVIEW).']
  hold_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text explanation of the hold reason. Provides context and specifics beyond the hold reason code, including root cause details, defect description, or customer complaint reference.']
  hold_release_authority_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person or system that released the hold. Provides human-readable identification of the release authority.']
  hold_release_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hold was released or lifted. Null if the hold is still active. Marks the point when the lot was cleared for continued processing or shipment.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the hold. Indicates whether the hold is currently active (lot is restricted), released (hold lifted and lot available), expired (hold auto-released after time limit), or cancelled (hold invalidated).']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the hold event. Indicates the functional area or business reason category that triggered the hold (engineering investigation, quality issue, customer request, export control compliance, process deviation, equipment failure, or material defect). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: engineering|quality|customer|export_control|process|equipment|material — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_regulatory_hold boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the hold is mandated by regulatory compliance requirements (e.g., export control, environmental, safety). True if the hold is regulatory-driven, false otherwise.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hold record was last updated. Audit trail timestamp for record modification.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the identified root cause of the issue that triggered the hold. Maps to internal root cause taxonomy (e.g., MATERIAL_DEFECT, PROCESS_DRIFT, EQUIPMENT_FAILURE, OPERATOR_ERROR).']
  wafer_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of wafers in the lot subject to this hold. Represents the count of silicon wafers affected by the hold restriction.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing hold events placed on wafer lots or finished goods inventory. Tracks hold ID, lot reference, hold type (engineering/quality/customer/export-control), hold reason code, hold initiator, hold date, release date, release authority, hold disposition (rework/scrap/release/return), and associated quality notification or FMEA reference. Critical for quality management and regulatory compliance workflows.'
}

Table inventory.physical_inventory {
  physical_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the physical inventory count event record.']
  die_bank_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.die_bank. Business justification: Physical inventory counts include counts of die bank inventory (KGD dies in waffle packs) stored in cleanroom environments. Adding die_bank_id FK enables physical inventory records to reference the sp']
  finished_good_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.finished_good. Business justification: Physical inventory counts include annual wall-to-wall counts and cycle counts of finished goods (packaged chips) in finished goods warehouses. Adding finished_good_id FK enables physical inventory rec']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: Physical inventory count events include cycle counts of WIP wafer lots in FAB cleanrooms. Adding inventory_wafer_lot_id FK enables physical inventory records to reference the specific wafer lot being ']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Physical inventory counts must be attributed to legal entity for audit trail, financial reconciliation, and legal entity-level inventory accuracy reporting.']
  primary_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'FK to inventory.storage_location.storage_location_id — Physical counts are always performed at a specific location. Required for SOX audit trail.']
  raw_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.raw_material. Business justification: Physical inventory counts include cycle counts of raw materials (silicon wafers, chemicals, gases) in chemical warehouses and cleanroom storage. Adding raw_material_id FK enables physical inventory re']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Physical inventory counts are performed by SKU for cycle counting, variance analysis, and audit compliance. SOX control requirement for inventory accuracy.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the supervisor approved the physical count results, authorizing variance posting to the system.']
  bin_classification varchar [note: 'Bin classification code for die or chip inventory, categorizing units by electrical test performance (e.g., Bin 1 for prime Known Good Die (KGD), Bin 2-9 for various performance grades or defects).']
  book_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'System-recorded inventory quantity (book quantity) at the time of the physical count, representing the expected quantity per ERP records.']
  consignment_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether this inventory is consignment stock held at an OSAT (Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test) partner or customer location, owned by the company but physically located off-site.']
  consignment_partner_code varchar [note: 'Vendor or partner code identifying the OSAT partner or customer location where consignment inventory is held.']
  count_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the physical count activity was completed and submitted for review.']
  count_date date [note: 'The date on which the physical inventory count was performed or is scheduled to be performed.']
  count_document_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique document number assigned to this physical inventory count event for traceability and audit purposes.']
  count_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments recorded by the counter or supervisor regarding count conditions, discrepancies observed, damaged goods, or other relevant observations.']
  count_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the physical count activity began for this inventory event.']
  count_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the physical inventory count event: planned (scheduled but not started), in-progress (counting underway), completed (count finished awaiting approval), posted (variance posted to system), cancelled, or on-hold.']
  count_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical inventory count event: cycle count (periodic rotation), annual wall-to-wall (full facility), triggered count (post-movement discrepancy or system exception), spot check, variance recount, or regulatory audit.']
  counted_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Physically counted quantity recorded by the counter during the inventory count event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this physical inventory count record was first created in the system.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year when the count was performed, supporting periodic financial close and inventory valuation.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the physical inventory count was performed, supporting annual financial reporting and SOX compliance.']
  freeze_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether inventory movements were frozen (blocked) during the count period to ensure accuracy and prevent discrepancies.']
  inventory_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the inventory being counted: raw material (silicon wafers, chemicals, gases, photomasks), WIP (Work in Process wafer lots), finished goods (packaged chips), die bank, consignment stock, or spare parts.']
  kgd_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the die or chip inventory has been verified as Known Good Die (KGD) through electrical testing, is non-KGD (failed test), untested, or in quarantine pending test results.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this physical inventory count record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot or batch identifier for WIP wafer lots, die banks, or consignment stock being counted, enabling full traceability per IATF 16949 requirements.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the material being counted: raw materials (silicon wafers, chemicals, gases, photomasks), Work in Process (WIP) wafer lots, finished goods (packaged chips), or die bank inventory.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the manufacturing plant or facility where the physical count was performed.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the inventory variance was posted to the financial and inventory management system, adjusting book quantities and valuations.']
  recount_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether this count is a recount of a previously counted item due to variance exceeding tolerance thresholds or supervisor request.']
  recount_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for why a recount was triggered: variance exceeds threshold, supervisor request, system discrepancy, damaged goods, quality hold, or initial count error.']
  shelf_life_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date for materials with limited shelf life (chemicals, photoresists, gases, adhesives), beyond which the material may not meet specification and must be disposed or retested.']
  standard_cost_usd decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard cost per unit of the material at the time of count, used for variance valuation and financial reporting.']
  storage_condition_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating required storage conditions for the material: ambient, controlled temperature, controlled humidity, nitrogen purge (for moisture-sensitive devices), vacuum, or cleanroom environment.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the counted quantity: EA (each for wafers or chips), KG (kilograms for chemicals), L (liters for gases or liquids), or other standard UOM codes.']
  variance_exceeds_tolerance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether the calculated variance exceeds the defined tolerance threshold, requiring investigation or recount per quality management procedures.']
  variance_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Calculated difference between counted quantity and book quantity (counted minus book), representing inventory discrepancy. Positive values indicate surplus, negative values indicate shortage.']
  variance_tolerance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable variance tolerance percentage for this count event, beyond which a recount or investigation is triggered. Typically set per material category and value class.']
  variance_value_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial value of the inventory variance in US dollars, calculated as variance quantity multiplied by standard cost or moving average price. Critical for SOX Section 404 financial controls and inventory valuation accuracy.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of physical inventory count events including cycle counts, annual wall-to-wall counts, and triggered counts (post-movement discrepancy or system exception). Captures count document number, count date, material/lot reference, storage location, system book quantity, physically counted quantity, variance quantity, variance value, count status (planned/in-progress/completed/posted), recount flag, counter ID, supervisor approval, and posting date. Supports SOX Section 404 financial controls, IATF 16949 inventory accuracy requirements, and ISO 9001 documented evidence of stock verification.'
}

Table inventory.consignment_stock {
  consignment_stock_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the consignment stock record. Primary key.']
  compliance_cert_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.compliance_cert. Business justification: Consignment stock may require compliance certifications for cross-border consignment. Cert status tracking for trade compliance and customs clearance requires this link.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Consignment inventory requires ECCN tracking for export control reporting, partner site compliance audits, and liability transfer documentation when goods are located at customer or offshore partner f']
  errata_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.errata. Business justification: Consignment stock could be affected by errata for liability, customer notification, and settlement. Errata impact on consigned inventory tracking requires this link.']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Consignment inventory at customer or partner sites requires export license tracking for ownership transfer compliance, deemed export verification, and liability management when goods cross internation']
  finished_good_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.finished_good. Business justification: Consignment stock includes finished goods (packaged chips) held at customer VMI locations or OSAT partners. The domain description explicitly mentions consignment stock at OSAT partners. Adding finish']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating wafer lot for traceability of consigned die or finished goods.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Consignment inventory ownership tracking by legal entity required for liability recognition, off-balance-sheet treatment, and financial statement disclosure.']
  ltb_notification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_ltb_notification. Business justification: Consignment stock may be affected by LTB notifications for liability, settlement, and customer notification. LTB impact on consigned inventory tracking requires this link.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material or die bank being consigned (finished goods, die banks, or raw materials).']
  pcn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.pcn. Business justification: Consignment stock may be affected by PCNs for liability, settlement, and customer notification. PCN impact on consigned inventory tracking requires this link.']
  primary_consignment_die_bank_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the die bank if the consignment stock consists of Known Good Die (KGD) awaiting packaging.']
  primary_consignment_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'FK to inventory.storage_location.storage_location_id — Consignment stock must reference the OSAT/partner storage location for reconciliation and ownership transfer.']
  raw_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.raw_material. Business justification: Consignment stock includes raw materials (silicon wafers, chemicals) consigned to OSAT assembly/test partners. Adding raw_material_id FK enables consignment stock records to reference the specific raw']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Consignment inventory is managed by SKU for liability tracking, settlement with suppliers/customers, and ownership transfer. Critical for consignment accounting and reconciliation.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: CONSIGNMENT: Consigned inventory is owned by a supplier; the FK identifies the responsible supplier for liability and settlement.']
  available_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of consigned material currently available at the OSAT partner location for consumption.']
  bin_location varchar [note: 'Specific bin or rack location within the OSAT partner warehouse where the consignment stock is stored.']
  consigned_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of material consigned to the OSAT partner or VMI hub under this agreement.']
  consignment_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the consignment agreement expires or when the material must be consumed, returned, or settled.']
  consignment_start_date date [note: 'Date when the material was first consigned to the OSAT partner or VMI hub under this agreement.']
  consignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the consignment stock record.']
  consignment_type varchar [note: 'Type of consignment arrangement: OSAT assembly, OSAT test, vendor-managed inventory hub, or customer consignment.']
  consumed_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of consigned material that has been consumed or used by the OSAT partner, triggering ownership transfer.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating where the consigned material was manufactured or substantially transformed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this consignment stock record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for valuation and settlement amounts.']
  in_transit_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of consigned material currently in transit to or from the OSAT partner or VMI hub.']
  itar_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consigned material is subject to ITAR export control regulations.']
  kgd_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the consigned die or material has been tested and certified as Known Good Die.']
  last_reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent consignment stock reconciliation between the semiconductor company and the OSAT partner.']
  last_settlement_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent financial settlement for consumed consignment stock with the OSAT partner.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this consignment stock record was most recently updated or modified.']
  liability_aging_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the consignment stock has been at the OSAT partner location, used for aging analysis and liability reporting.']
  lot_traceability_code varchar [note: 'Traceability code linking the consigned material to its manufacturing lot, wafer lot, and production history for quality and compliance tracking.']
  msd_level varchar [note: 'JEDEC moisture sensitivity level classification for the consigned material, indicating required storage and handling conditions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|2a|3|4|5|5a|6 — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  next_reconciliation_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next consignment stock reconciliation based on the reconciliation frequency.']
  ownership_transfer_trigger varchar [note: 'Event that triggers ownership transfer from the semiconductor company to the OSAT partner: consumption, shipment, time-based, or withdrawal.']
  reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consigned material complies with EU REACH regulation for chemical substance safety.']
  reconciliation_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which consignment stock levels are reconciled between the semiconductor company and the OSAT partner.']
  returned_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of consigned material returned to the semiconductor company from the OSAT partner or VMI hub.']
  rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consigned material complies with EU RoHS directive restricting hazardous substances in electronic equipment.']
  settlement_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the most recent settlement document or invoice generated for consumed consignment stock.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days the consigned material can be stored before it must be used or returned, applicable to moisture-sensitive devices.']
  shelf_life_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the consigned material reaches the end of its shelf life and must be consumed, returned, or scrapped.']
  standard_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard unit cost of the consigned material used for inventory valuation and settlement calculations.']
  storage_humidity_max_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum relative humidity percentage allowed for storage of the consigned material at the OSAT partner location.']
  storage_temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum storage temperature in degrees Celsius required for the consigned material at the OSAT partner location.']
  total_valuation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the consigned stock at the OSAT partner location, calculated as available quantity times standard cost.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the consigned material quantities (each, piece, kilogram, wafer, die, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|KG|LB|M|FT|L|GAL|WAFER|DIE — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Master record for inventory consigned to OSAT assembly/test partners or customer vendor-managed inventory (VMI) hubs where ownership transfers upon consumption or shipment. Tracks consignment agreement ID, OSAT partner reference, material/die bank reference, consigned quantity, consumed quantity, returned quantity, in-transit quantity, ownership transfer trigger (consumption/shipment/time-based), valuation currency, reconciliation frequency (weekly/monthly), last reconciliation date, settlement document reference, and liability aging. Enables monthly consignment settlement with OSAT partners, VMI replenishment automation, and accurate liability reporting for finance.'
}

Table inventory.stock_valuation {
  stock_valuation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for stock_valuation']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Links each stock valuation record to the cost center that bears the inventory cost for reporting.']
  die_bank_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.die_bank. Business justification: Stock valuation records the financial valuation of die bank inventory (KGD dies). Adding die_bank_id FK enables stock valuation records to reference the specific die bank being valued, supporting die ']
  finished_good_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.finished_good. Business justification: Stock valuation records the financial valuation of finished goods inventory positions. Adding finished_good_id FK enables stock valuation records to reference the specific finished good being valued, ']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Associates valuation entries with a GL account to post inventory value changes to the ledger.']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'FK to inventory.wafer_lot.wafer_lot_id — WIP valuation is performed at the wafer lot level with cost accumulation through process steps. This is the primary valuation entity for in-process inventory.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Stock revaluations post to journal entries for inventory adjustments, variance accounting, and period-end inventory valuation corrections.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Inventory valuation must be tied to legal entity for GAAP/IFRS compliance, tax reporting, and legal entity financial statement preparation.']
  primary_stock_balance_id varchar [note: 'FK to inventory.stock_balance.stock_balance_id — Financial valuation must reference the quantity position it values. Links financial and physical inventory views.']
  primary_stock_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record being valued (raw material, WIP wafer lot, die bank, or finished good).']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Supports profit‑center‑level inventory valuation for margin analysis across product lines.']
  raw_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.raw_material. Business justification: Stock valuation records the financial valuation of raw material inventory positions (silicon wafers, chemicals, gases). Adding raw_material_id FK enables stock valuation records to reference the speci']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Stock valuations are calculated by SKU for COGS, inventory reserves, and financial reporting. GAAP/IFRS requirement for inventory valuation and lower-of-cost-or-market analysis.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: stock_valuation tracks valuation per location using a code; a FK to storage_location provides referential integrity and eliminates duplicate location data.']
  bin_classification varchar [note: 'The test bin classification code indicating the quality grade and performance characteristics of the die or packaged chip.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this inventory valuation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this valuation record.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year for this valuation record.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this valuation record belongs, supporting period-based financial reporting.']
  inventory_category varchar [note: 'The category of inventory being valued: raw material (silicon wafers, chemicals, gases, photomasks), WIP (Work In Process) wafer lots, die bank, finished goods (packaged chips), consignment stock at OSAT partners, or MPW (Multi-Project Wafer) shuttle.']
  is_consignment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory is consignment stock held at an OSAT (Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test) partner or customer location, affecting ownership and valuation treatment.']
  kgd_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the die or wafer lot has been tested and verified as Known Good Die (KGD), critical for die bank and finished goods valuation.']
  labor_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The direct labor cost component allocated to the inventory valuation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this inventory valuation record was last updated or modified.']
  last_valuation_date date [note: 'The date of the previous valuation calculation for this inventory position, enabling period-over-period variance analysis.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'The wafer lot number or batch identifier for WIP and die bank inventory, enabling full traceability.']
  material_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The direct material cost component of the valuation (silicon wafers, chemicals, gases, photomasks, packaging materials).']
  moving_average_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The moving average price per unit, recalculated with each goods receipt when using moving average valuation.']
  nre_cost_allocation decimal(18,2) [note: 'The allocated portion of NRE costs (design, tapeout, mask set, PDK licensing) amortized into the inventory valuation.']
  overhead_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The manufacturing overhead cost component (facility, equipment depreciation, utilities) allocated to the inventory valuation.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The fabrication facility (FAB) or manufacturing plant code where the inventory is located.']
  quantity_on_hand decimal(18,3) [note: 'The physical quantity of inventory on hand at the valuation date.']
  reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inventory item complies with EU REACH regulation for chemical substance safety.']
  rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inventory item complies with EU RoHS directive restricting hazardous substances in electronic equipment.']
  shelf_life_expiry_date date [note: 'The date when the inventory item reaches the end of its shelf life and may require requalification or disposal, critical for chemicals, photomasks, and moisture-sensitive devices.']
  sox_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this valuation record is subject to SOX financial reporting controls and audit requirements.']
  standard_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The standard cost per unit as defined in the material master for standard costing valuation.']
  total_stock_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total financial value of the inventory position (quantity × unit price), representing the balance sheet valuation.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the quantity (e.g., EA for each/die, WF for wafer, KG for kilograms, LT for liters).']
  valuation_class varchar [note: 'The valuation class that groups materials for accounting purposes and determines the general ledger accounts for posting.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'The date on which the inventory valuation was calculated and recorded. Principal business event timestamp for this financial snapshot.']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'The inventory costing method applied: standard cost, moving average price, FIFO (First In First Out), LIFO (Last In First Out), or weighted average.']
  valuation_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the inventory valuation: active (available for use), blocked (restricted), quality hold (pending inspection), obsolete (end of life), or scrapped.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the valuation split (e.g., unrestricted use, quality inspection, blocked stock, consignment).']
  valuation_variance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The variance between current and previous valuation amounts, highlighting inventory value changes for management review.']
  wip_cost_accumulation decimal(18,2) [note: 'The accumulated manufacturing costs for WIP wafer lots, including FEOL (Front End of Line), MOL (Middle of Line), and BEOL (Back End of Line) processing costs.']
  yield_impact_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'A factor representing the yield percentage impact on valuation, used to adjust WIP and die bank valuations based on expected good die yield.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the financial valuation of inventory positions including WIP wafer lots, raw materials, die banks, and finished goods. Tracks valuation date, material or lot reference, valuation class, valuation method (standard cost/moving average/FIFO), standard price, moving average price, total stock value, WIP cost accumulation, NRE cost allocation, currency, and cost center. Supports SAP CO-PC product costing and SOX financial reporting.'
}

Table inventory.reservation {
  reservation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the inventory reservation record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer associated with the reservation, if applicable.']
  consuming_goods_movement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the goods movement that will consume the reservation.']
  die_bank_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.die_bank. Business justification: Reservations earmark KGD die inventory in die banks for assembly orders at OSAT partners. Adding die_bank_id FK enables reservation records to reference the specific die bank being reserved, supportin']
  finished_good_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.finished_good. Business justification: Reservations earmark finished goods inventory for customer orders, sales orders, or transfer orders. Adding finished_good_id FK enables reservation records to reference the specific finished good bein']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: Reservations earmark specific inventory quantities for production orders or customer demand. WIP wafer lots can be reserved for specific downstream operations or customer orders. Adding inventory_wafe']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Material reservations may need legal entity context for intercompany demand planning, transfer pricing forecasts, and legal entity supply chain visibility.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Inventory reservations are created against specific order lines to secure stock for customer commitments. Enables ATP logic, allocation priority enforcement, and reservation expiry management tied to ']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the raw material or finished‑good SKU associated with the reservation.']
  raw_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.raw_material. Business justification: Reservations earmark raw materials (silicon wafers, chemicals) for planned production runs. Adding raw_material_id FK enables reservation records to reference the specific raw material being reserved,']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Reservations allocate inventory by SKU for sales orders, production orders, and customer commitments. ATP logic and order promising depend on SKU-level reservations.']
  split_from_reservation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on reservation (split_from_reservation_id)']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Reservations reference specific storage locations where reserved inventory is physically held. The existing storage_location_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference that should be replaced by a prop']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer or material lot that is being reserved.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Material reservations for customer projects link to WBS for demand planning, cost forecasting, and project resource allocation.']
  bin_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the inventory bin (e.g., A, B, C) used for yield or quality segregation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reservation record was first captured in the lakehouse.']
  demand_source_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating demand record (e.g., sales order, production order).']
  expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reservation automatically expires if not consumed.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the reserved inventory (e.g., available, on_hold).']
  is_kgd boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reservation is for KGD‑qualified inventory.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority used for allocation sequencing.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for why the reservation was created.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Customer‑or‑production requested date for the reserved inventory to be delivered.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible reservation code used in communications and reporting.']
  reservation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reservation.']
  reservation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reservation was created in the source system.']
  reservation_type varchar [note: 'Category of demand source that triggered the reservation.']
  reserved_quantity decimal(20,4) [note: 'Amount of inventory units earmarked by the reservation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating ERP/MES system that generated the reservation record.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the reserved quantity (e.g., pcs, wafers, kg).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the reservation record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for inventory allocation and reservation events that earmark specific quantities of WIP lots, die bank dies, raw materials, or finished goods for a designated demand source (customer order, production order, packaging run, engineering sample request). Captures reservation ID, material/lot reference, reserved quantity, UoM, demand source type and ID, reservation date, requested delivery date, priority, reservation status (active/consumed/cancelled/expired), consuming goods_movement reference, and reserving user. Enables ATP (available-to-promise) calculations, prevents double-allocation of scarce KGD inventory, and supports OSAT packaging run scheduling.'
}

Table supply.supplier {
  supplier_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the supplier record.']

Tax reporting (1099, VAT), intercompany reconciliation, regulatory compliance (FCPA, sanctions screening), and payment processing require linking supplier mast']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the supplier\'s primary address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the supplier\'s primary address (optional).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier was approved for procurement.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the internal user who approved the supplier onboarding.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the supplier\'s primary address.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance standing of the supplier with internal and external regulations.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the supplier\'s primary location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supplier record was first created.']
  credit_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount approved for the supplier.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Default transaction currency used with the supplier.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Dun & Bradstreet unique identifier for the supplier organization.']
  ear_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier is subject to EAR export controls.']
  financial_rating varchar [note: 'Credit rating assigned to the supplier based on financial health.']
  global_footprint varchar [note: 'Scope of the supplier\'s operational presence.']
  industry_classification varchar [note: 'Broad industry segment to which the supplier belongs.']
  is_certified_kga boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier holds a KGA (Known Good Assembly) certification.']
  itar_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier is subject to ITAR export controls.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent supplier quality or compliance audit.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Typical number of days from order placement to delivery.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal registered name of the supplier entity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or comments about the supplier.']
  parent_company_name varchar [note: 'Name of the ultimate parent organization if the supplier is a subsidiary.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms negotiated with the supplier.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the supplier\'s primary address.']
  preferred_logistics_partner varchar [note: 'Logistics provider most frequently used for shipments from this supplier.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the main contact person for the supplier.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary supplier contact.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Telephone number of the primary supplier contact.']
  quality_rating varchar [note: 'Overall quality performance rating based on audits and defect data.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number of the supplier\'s legal entity.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated risk score derived from financial, geopolitical, and operational factors.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the supplier\'s primary address.']
  supplier_group varchar [note: 'Business grouping used for strategic sourcing and spend analysis.']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Primary display name of the supplier used in business transactions.']
  supplier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the supplier record.']
  supplier_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the supplier based on strategic importance and spend volume.']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the supplier\'s environmental and social responsibility performance.']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax registration number for the supplier.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the supplier record.']
  website varchar [note: 'Public website URL of the supplier.']

  Note: 'Master record for all semiconductor supply chain vendors including raw material suppliers, chemical suppliers, gas suppliers, photomask vendors, OSAT partners, and subcontractors. Captures supplier identity, classification (Tier 1/2/3), business registration, DUNS number, geographic footprint, financial health rating, strategic importance, and ITAR/EAR export control classification. SSOT for supplier identity across the supply domain. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master.'
}

  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the supplier organization.']

  Note: 'Approved Vendor List (AVL) records establishing which suppliers are qualified and authorized to supply specific material categories, chemical families, or process inputs to the fab. Tracks qualification status, approval date, expiry date, approved commodity scope, quality tier, and the engineering/procurement authority who granted approval. Supports DFM and process qualification gating. Sourced from Oracle Agile PLM and SAP MM.'
}

Table supply.material_master {
  material_master_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each material master record.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Each material must have an ECCN for export‑control classification; used in licensing and reporting.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Materials map to GL accounts for inventory valuation, COGS posting, and financial statement preparation. Fundamental FI-MM integration for automated journal entries during goods movements, invoice ver']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Material masters represent finished ICs, wafers, or die in semiconductor supply chains. Direct link enables BOM costing, procurement planning, inventory valuation, and MRP explosion for IC products.']
  substance_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.substance_inventory. Business justification: Materials contain substances tracked for REACH/RoHS/conflict minerals compliance. Product compliance declarations, hazardous material handling, and regulatory reporting depend on linking material mast']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved vendor supplying this material.']
  base_uom varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure used for inventory and procurement transactions (e.g., kg, L, pcs).']
  batch_management boolean [note: 'True if the material is managed in batches for traceability.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code indicating where the material originates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the material master record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code of the currency for the standard cost.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the material must not be used.']
  hazardous_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification indicating the material\'s hazardous status under RoHS, REACH, and TSCA.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days from purchase order issuance to receipt of the material.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the material record.']
  lot_size_qty decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard production or procurement lot size for the material.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the material, including its purpose and key characteristics.']
  material_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the material.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the material across the enterprise.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the material by its functional role in semiconductor manufacturing.']
  max_order_qty decimal(12,2) [note: 'Largest quantity that can be ordered in a single purchase order.']
  min_order_qty decimal(12,2) [note: 'Smallest quantity that can be ordered from a supplier.']
  procurement_group varchar [note: 'Organizational group responsible for procuring the material.']
  quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'True if incoming or in‑process inspection is mandatory for the material.']
  reach_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the material meets the Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) requirements.']
  reorder_point_qty decimal(12,2) [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a replenishment order.']
  rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the material complies with the Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) directive.']
  safety_stock_qty decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of material kept on hand to protect against demand or supply variability.']
  serial_management boolean [note: 'True if the material is tracked by serial numbers.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the material can be stored before it expires.']
  standard_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'Default cost used for valuation and planning, expressed in the base currency.']
  storage_humidity_max_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Highest permissible relative humidity percentage for storage.']
  storage_humidity_min_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lowest permissible relative humidity percentage for storage.']
  storage_temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Highest permissible storage temperature in degrees Celsius.']
  storage_temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lowest permissible storage temperature in degrees Celsius.']
  tsca_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the material is compliant with the US Toxic Substances Control Act (TSCA).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the material master record.']
  valuation_class varchar [note: 'Key for grouping materials with similar accounting treatment.']

  Note: 'Authoritative master record for all procured materials used in semiconductor manufacturing: silicon wafer substrates, process chemicals (slurries, etchants, photoresists), specialty gases (silane, nitrogen, argon), photomasks, packaging substrates, lead frames, bonding wire, and consumables. Captures material number, description, material type, base unit of measure, hazardous material classification (RoHS/REACH/TSCA), storage conditions, shelf life, procurement lead time, safety stock levels, reorder points, and lot sizing parameters. SSOT for material identity and procurement parameters within the supply domain.'
}

Table supply.purchase_order {
  purchase_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the purchase order record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Purchase order fulfills a specific customer account order; linking enables order‑to‑cash reconciliation and profitability analysis.']
  approved_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.approved_vendor. Business justification: Purchase orders must be issued only to vendors on the Approved Vendor List (AVL). Linking purchase_order to approved_vendor enforces AVL compliance at the PO level, enables detection of POs issued to ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: PO accounting requires posting expenses to a cost center for internal budgeting and cost‑reporting; the finance cost_center entity holds the approved cost center hierarchy.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Purchase orders for controlled items require ECCN classification for export license determination, customs documentation, and trade compliance screening. PO-level ECCN tracking enables automated compl']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Purchase orders are raised for specific IC catalog items; linking enables order‑to‑item traceability and cost reporting.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: POs are issued by a specific legal entity for intercompany transactions, transfer pricing documentation, VAT/GST compliance, and consolidated financial reporting. Essential for multi-entity semiconduc']
  primary_purchase_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier (vendor) to which the purchase order is issued.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis allocates PO spend to a profit center, enabling product‑line P&L reporting; finance profit_center stores the responsible profit center codes.']
  sourcing_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.sourcing_contract. Business justification: Purchase orders in semiconductor procurement are typically issued against pre-negotiated sourcing contracts that define pricing, lead times, and terms. Linking purchase_order to sourcing_contract enab']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date when goods/services were actually received.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase order was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the purchase order.']
  compliance_flags varchar [note: 'Semicolon‑separated list of compliance indicators (e.g., RoHS, REACH).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this purchase order record was first captured in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts on the PO.']
  delivery_schedule varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of delivery windows or milestones.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the discount applied.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount granted on the gross amount.']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date for goods/services to be delivered.']
  freight_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary cost of freight/shipping.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Responsibility for freight costs.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms defining delivery responsibilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|FOB|CFR|CIF|DAP|DDP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_ear_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the PO is subject to U.S. Export Administration Regulations.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days from PO issuance to expected receipt.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the material or service.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material or service being procured.']
  order_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the purchase order was initially created/submitted.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Agreed payment schedule and conditions for the purchase order.']
  plant varchar [note: 'SAP plant code where the goods are to be received.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the purchase order by the procurement system.']
  purchase_group varchar [note: 'Organizational group responsible for the procurement.']
  purchase_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the purchase order.']
  purchase_order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the PO based on its procurement strategy.']
  purchase_supplier_fk varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — Every PO is issued TO a supplier. This is a day-1 mandatory FK.']
  purchasing_org varchar [note: 'SAP purchasing organization that owns the PO.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units ordered for the material.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase order was released to the supplier.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse or storage bin identifier for receipt.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction and rate applied.']
  total_gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before taxes, discounts, and freight.']
  total_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final payable amount after taxes, discounts, and freight.']
  total_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate tax amount applied to the purchase order.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the ordered quantity (e.g., EA, KG, L).']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed price per unit of the material before taxes and discounts.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the purchase order record.']

  Note: 'Procurement purchase orders issued to qualified suppliers for raw materials, process chemicals, gases, photomasks, equipment spare parts, and subcontracted services (OSAT assembly/test). Captures PO number, supplier, line items, ordered quantities, agreed unit price, currency, delivery schedule, Incoterms, payment terms, and export control classification (EAR/ITAR). Represents the legally binding procurement commitment. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM (EKKO/EKPO).'
}

Table supply.po_line {
  po_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the purchase order line record.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Individual PO line items may have different ECCN classifications. Line-level tracking enables accurate export control assessment, license consumption tracking, and customs valuation for mixed-classifi']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: PO lines post to specific GL accounts for accrual accounting, expense classification, and audit trail. Required for three-way match (PO-GR-Invoice), financial close automation, and SOX compliance in s']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Each PO line references the exact IC part being ordered, supporting receipt matching and inventory control.']
  po_material_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.material_master.material_master_id — Each PO line references a specific material being procured.']
  primary_po_material_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.material_master.material_master_id — Each PO line procures a specific material. Essential for material traceability and MRP consumption.']
  primary_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.purchase_order.purchase_order_id — Header-line pattern: every PO line belongs to exactly one PO. Critical structural relationship.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier providing the material.']
  account_assignment_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific cost center, WBS element, or order linked to this line.']
  account_assignment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost object (cost center, WBS element, or internal order).']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference to a framework contract governing this line, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the purchase order line record was created.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary values on the line.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the supplier should deliver the material.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,4) [note: 'Total discount value applied to the line.']
  goods_receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current status of goods receipt for this line.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms defining delivery responsibilities.']
  is_final_invoice boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line has been invoiced in its final form.']
  is_service_line boolean [note: 'True if the line represents a service rather than a material.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days from order to delivery for this line.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the purchase order.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the purchase order line.']
  line_total_amount decimal(15,4) [note: 'Gross amount before tax and discount (ordered_quantity * net_price).']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the material.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Master data identifier for the material or component being procured.']
  net_amount decimal(15,4) [note: 'Net monetary value of the line after tax and discount.']
  net_price decimal(15,4) [note: 'Net price per unit of the material before taxes and discounts.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of the material requested on this line.']
  plant varchar [note: 'Code of the manufacturing plant or site receiving the material.']
  price_unit varchar [note: 'Number of units that the net price applies to (e.g., price per 1000 pieces).']
  purchase_group varchar [note: 'Organizational group responsible for the procurement.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which the goods were received.']
  receipt_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material that has been received against this line.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse or storage bin where the material will be stocked.']
  supply_risk_score varchar [note: 'Risk rating (0‑100) assessing supplier and material availability risk.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,4) [note: 'Monetary tax amount calculated for the line.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code used to calculate tax for the line.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the ordered quantity is expressed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the line record.']
  vendor_material_number varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s own part number for the material.']

  Note: 'Individual line items within a purchase order, each representing a distinct material or service being procured. Captures line number, material number, ordered quantity, unit of measure, net price, delivery date, plant/storage location destination, account assignment (cost center or WBS element), and goods receipt status. Enables granular tracking of multi-material POs and partial deliveries. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM (EKPO).'
}

Table supply.goods_receipt {
  goods_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the goods receipt record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Goods receipt is allocated to the customer account it fulfills, needed for inventory allocation and billing.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Goods receipts trigger cost allocation to receiving cost centers for inventory valuation, departmental cost tracking, and variance analysis. Standard SAP MM-FI integration pattern for material movemen']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Received goods must be verified against declared ECCN for import compliance and inventory control. Goods receipt ECCN tracking supports customs clearance, deemed export controls, and warehouse segrega']
  export_license_usage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license_usage. Business justification: Goods receipt must reference the export‑license usage that covered the inbound shipment for compliance verification.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Goods receipt ties received material to the IC catalog entry for quality verification and traceability.']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_shipment. Business justification: A goods receipt is the warehouse-side confirmation of an inbound shipment arriving at the fab or warehouse. Linking goods_receipt to inbound_shipment enables end-to-end traceability from logistics tra']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: goods_receipt records the receipt of a specific material but currently has no FK to material_master — only a free-text material_number string. Adding material_master_id provides a normalized reference']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.po_line. Business justification: A goods receipt records the physical receipt of materials against a specific PO line item, not just the PO header. Adding po_line_id normalizes the line-level receipt relationship and removes redundan']
  primary_goods_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the purchase order associated with the receipt.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Goods receipts for finished ICs must capture exact SKU for inventory accuracy, lot control, ATP calculation, and customer order fulfillment. Critical for ERP inventory posting and warehouse management']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier providing the goods.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the production batch or lot of the received material.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material complies with applicable regulations (e.g., RoHS).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Detailed compliance status of the material.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the goods receipt record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter code of the currency used for monetary values.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Any external system reference linked to this receipt (e.g., logistics tracking ID).']
  goods_purchase_order_fk varchar [note: 'FK to supply.purchase_order.purchase_order_id — Goods receipts are posted against a purchase order. Core three-way match requirement.']
  goods_receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the receipt (e.g., posted, reversed, pending).']
  gross_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the receipt before taxes and discounts.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality inspection lot created for this receipt.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days between purchase order issuance and goods receipt.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP movement type code indicating the nature of the receipt (e.g., 101 = goods receipt).']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net monetary value after tax and discounts.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional comments or observations.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or fab where receipt is posted.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the receipt is posted to financial accounting.']
  quality_status varchar [note: 'Result of the incoming quality inspection for the received material.']
  quantity_received decimal(15,3) [note: 'Amount of material physically received, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the goods were physically received.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the receipt document (e.g., GR number).']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the receipt was recorded in the system.']
  received_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the person who logged the receipt.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing supply risk for the received material (higher = higher risk).']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse or fab location where the material is stored after receipt.']
  supplier_batch_reference varchar [note: 'Reference supplied by the vendor for the batch (e.g., vendor lot number).']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the receipt.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the quantity is measured (e.g., each, kilogram, liter, meter).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the goods receipt record.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Valuation category for the material (e.g., S = standard, U = moving average, V = special).']

  Note: 'Records the physical receipt of materials and goods at the fab or warehouse against a purchase order. Captures GR document number, posting date, received quantity, unit of measure, storage location, batch number, quality inspection lot reference, and movement type. Triggers inventory update and initiates incoming quality inspection. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM (MSEG/MKPF — movement type 101).'
}

Table supply.supplier_qualification {
  supplier_qualification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each supplier qualification record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Supplier qualification is performed for a particular customer account\'s requirements, used in account‑specific compliance reporting.']
  compliance_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.certification. Business justification: Qualification programs are linked to a specific compliance certification required for supplier approval.']
  obligation_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation_register. Business justification: Supplier qualification programs verify compliance with specific regulatory obligations (ISO9001, ITAR registration, REACH compliance). Linking qualification records to obligation register enables audi']
  primary_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier being qualified, linking to the supplier master record.']
  tertiary_supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — Qualification records assess a specific supplier. Cannot exist without supplier reference.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the qualification was formally approved.']
  audit_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the audit was performed.']
  audit_team varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of auditors and subject‑matter experts who conducted the audit.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit execution: initial qualification, routine surveillance, or for‑cause audit.']
  compliance_standards varchar [note: 'Semicolon‑separated list of regulatory or industry standards the qualification satisfies.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Date by which all corrective actions must be completed.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Detailed plan describing corrective actions required to resolve audit findings.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the corrective action plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the qualification record was first created in the system.']
  findings_severity varchar [note: 'Highest severity level of audit findings identified during the assessment.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of audit observations, non‑conformances, and strengths.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information captured by the qualification team.']
  overall_rating varchar [note: 'Result of the qualification audit after considering findings and corrective actions.']
  qualification_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the qualification program for tracking and audit purposes.']
  qualification_program_type varchar [note: 'Type of qualification program: initial onboarding, scheduled periodic review, or change‑triggered re‑qualification.']
  qualification_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the qualification assessment covering quality system audit, process capability, material certification, or a combination.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the qualification record.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk score (0‑100) derived from supplier risk assessment models.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the qualification record.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier qualification expires unless re‑qualified.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier qualification becomes effective.']

  Note: 'Formal supplier qualification lifecycle records encompassing the full assessment, audit, and approval process for onboarding new suppliers or re-qualifying existing ones. Captures qualification program type (initial, periodic, change-triggered), assessment scope (quality system audit, process capability, material certification review), on-site and remote audit execution details (audit type — initial qualification, surveillance, for-cause; audit team composition; findings by severity — critical/major/minor; corrective action plans and closure tracking; overall audit rating), qualification criteria and pass/fail results, validity period, and approving engineer. SSOT for all supplier audit and qualification activity within the supply domain. Supports IATF 16949, ISO 9001, and SEMI standards supplier approval requirements.'
}

  audit_report_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored audit report document.']

  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance determination after the audit.']
