  findings_minor_count varchar [note: 'Number of minor severity findings identified.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of audit findings across severity levels.']
  itar_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the audited supplier is subject to ITAR regulations.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical site or facility where the audit was conducted.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the supplier\'s next audit.']
  overall_rating varchar [note: 'Overall rating assigned to the audit based on findings.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk score assigned to the supplier based on audit outcomes.']
  supplier_rating varchar [note: 'Overall rating assigned to the supplier after the audit.']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the supplier\'s environmental and social performance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the audit record.']

  Note: 'Records of on-site and remote quality system audits conducted at supplier facilities. Captures audit type (initial qualification, surveillance, for-cause), audit date, audit team, scope of audit (ISO 9001, IATF 16949, SEMI standards), findings count by severity (critical, major, minor), corrective action plan status, and overall audit rating. Drives supplier performance management and AVL maintenance decisions.'
}

Table supply.risk_assessment {
  risk_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for risk_assessment']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Supply risk assessments (single-source, EOL, geopolitical, capacity constraints) are performed at product level. Risk mitigation planning, business continuity, and alternate sourcing decisions require']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material or component impacted.']
  primary_risk_alternate_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of an approved alternate supplier for the material.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: risk_assessment has an impacted_po_quantity field indicating it can be tied to a specific purchase order disruption event. Linking risk_assessment to purchase_order enables direct traceability from a ']
  sourcing_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.sourcing_contract. Business justification: Supply risk assessments are often scoped to specific sourcing contracts — particularly for single-source or sole-source supply agreements. Linking risk_assessment to sourcing_contract enables contract']
  assessment_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the assessment, used for tracking and reporting.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date when the risk assessment was initially created.']
  assessment_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the risk assessment, typically includes supplier or material reference.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the risk assessment.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who performed the risk assessment.']
  buffer_stock_target_qty varchar [note: 'Target quantity of safety stock to buffer against the risk.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the risk assessment record was first created.']
  disruption_end_date date [note: 'Date when the disruption was resolved or ended.']
  disruption_escalation_level varchar [note: 'Severity level for escalation and response.']
  disruption_event_type varchar [note: 'Category of the disruption that occurred.']
  disruption_report_date date [note: 'Date when the disruption was reported.']
  disruption_reported_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who reported the disruption.']
  disruption_start_date date [note: 'Date when the disruption began.']
  disruption_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the disruption record.']
  estimated_fab_impact_wafer_starts varchar [note: 'Projected loss of wafer starts due to the disruption.']
  impact_estimate_wafer_starts varchar [note: 'Estimated number of wafer starts that could be lost if the risk occurs.']
  impact_severity varchar [note: 'Potential impact level if the risk materializes.']
  impacted_po_quantity varchar [note: 'Total purchase order quantity affected by the disruption.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent risk assessment review.']
  mitigation_actions_taken varchar [note: 'Actions executed to mitigate the disruption impact.']
  mitigation_strategy varchar [note: 'Planned actions to reduce probability or impact of the risk.']
  probability_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Likelihood of the risk event occurring, expressed as a percentage.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the disruption handling process.']
  review_cadence varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the risk assessment is reviewed.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the risk type being evaluated.']
  risk_comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes and observations about the risk.']
  risk_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the risk assessment becomes effective.']
  risk_expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the risk assessment is no longer valid.']
  risk_last_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the risk record.']
  risk_owner_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department of the risk owner.']
  risk_owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person accountable for managing the risk.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite numeric score (0‑100) representing overall risk severity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']

  Note: 'Comprehensive supply risk management combining prospective risk assessments and actual disruption event records. Captures risk evaluations (risk category — single-source, geopolitical, financial, natural disaster, export control; probability, impact severity, risk score, mitigation strategy, buffer stock targets, alternate supplier identification, review cadence) and realized disruption incidents (disruption ID, event type — supplier factory shutdown, natural disaster, logistics delay, customs hold, quality hold, geopolitical trade restriction; affected supplier and materials, disruption start and end dates, impacted PO quantities, estimated fab impact in wafer starts at risk, escalation level, mitigation actions taken, resolution outcome). SSOT for all supply risk evaluation and disruption tracking within the supply domain. Supports supply chain resilience planning, CHIPS Act compliance reporting, and executive supply chain risk dashboards.'
}

Table supply.sourcing_contract {
  sourcing_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the sourcing contract record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Sourcing contracts are negotiated to supply products for a specific customer account, critical for contract management and revenue forecasting.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Long-term supply contracts for controlled technology require ECCN documentation for export license planning and technology transfer agreements. Contract-level ECCN tracking supports deemed export comp']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Contracts for controlled commodities must reference the export license that governs the supply terms.']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Long-term supply agreements for wafers, OSAT services, or materials are negotiated at family level. Enables contract compliance tracking, capacity allocation, and pricing management by product line.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Sourcing contracts are signed by a specific legal entity for contract enforceability, liability tracking, financial consolidation, and intercompany pricing. Required for legal review, signature author']
  primary_sourcing_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier party associated with this contract.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract received final approval.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the contract within internal governance.']
  commodity_code varchar [note: 'Internal or industry code (e.g., part number, SKU) that uniquely identifies the commodity.']
  commodity_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the material, component, or service being sourced.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External contract number assigned by the organization for tracking and reference.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Category of the sourcing contract indicating its business purpose.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in the contract.']
  delivery_terms varchar [note: 'Incoterms governing responsibility and cost transfer between buyer and supplier.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract becomes legally binding.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract terminates unless extended; nullable for open‑ended agreements.']
  force_majeure_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing conditions under which performance may be excused due to extraordinary events.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard supplier lead time in calendar days for the commodity.']
  maximum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Largest quantity the supplier agrees to deliver in a single order.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Smallest quantity the supplier will accept per order under the contract.']
  pcn_notification_obligation varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s obligation to notify the buyer of Product Change Notifications.']
  price_escalation_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing any price adjustment mechanisms (e.g., CPI‑linked escalations).']
  quality_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific quality standards, inspection criteria, and acceptance metrics required by the buyer.']
  rebate_tier varchar [note: 'Rebate level applicable based on purchase volume or other criteria.']
  sourcing_contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract.']
  sourcing_supplier_fk varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — Contracts are negotiated with specific suppliers.']
  supply_continuity_obligation varchar [note: 'Commitments related to uninterrupted supply, safety stock, or backup sources.']
  target_quantity varchar [note: 'Planned total quantity to be procured under the contract.']
  total_contract_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Aggregate monetary value of the contract (unit_price * target_quantity). Stored as raw data; derived metrics are calculated downstream.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the target quantity (e.g., pieces, kilograms).']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed price per unit of the commodity, expressed in the contract currency.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the contract record.']

  Note: 'End-to-end sourcing lifecycle records from initial solicitation through executed supply agreement. Covers RFQ issuance (commodity description, target quantity, technical specifications, evaluation criteria, submission deadline), supplier quotation responses (quoted unit price, currency, delivery lead time, MOQ, technical deviations), comparative evaluation and award decisions, and executed contract terms (contract number, supplier, commodity scope, effective and expiry dates, minimum order quantities, volume rebate tiers, price escalation clauses, force majeure terms, PCN notification obligations, quality requirements, and supply continuity obligations). SSOT for the complete RFQ-to-contract sourcing lifecycle within the supply domain. Distinct from customer-facing sales contracts owned by the sales domain.'
}

Linking material_requirement_plan to sourcing_contract enables the MR']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved supplier that will fulfill the planned order.']

  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the MRP system generated this record.']

  demand_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of the material required by production orders or forecasts.']
  exception_message varchar [note: 'Textual description of any planning exception (e.g., shortfall, excess).']
  is_fixed_lot boolean [note: 'True if the lot size is fixed by engineering, otherwise False.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification (e.g., quantity change, status update).']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days from order placement to receipt of material.']
  lot_sizing_procedure varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to calculate the lot size for the planned order.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Customer‑visible material number (e.g., SKU) used in procurement and production.']

  reorder_point_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a new procurement recommendation.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity kept as buffer to protect against demand or supply variability.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit in which the planned quantity is expressed.']

  Note: 'Material Requirements Planning (MRP) run outputs defining planned procurement quantities and timing for all fab materials based on production schedules, wafer start plans, safety stock levels, and reorder points. Captures planning run date, material, plant, planned order quantity, planned delivery date, MRP type, lot sizing procedure, and exception messages (shortfall, excess, rescheduling). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP (MDKP/MDTB).'
}

  tertiary_supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — Scorecards rate a specific supplier\'s performance.']

  Note: 'Unified supplier performance management, corrective action, and continuous improvement records. SCAR records include SCAR number, triggering event, defect description, 8D problem-solving steps (containment, root cause, corrective action, preventive action), supplier response due dates, verification status, and closure dates. SSOT for all supplier performance measurement and corrective action within the supply domain. Drives AVL status decisions, preferred supplier designations, FMEA-driven quality improvement, DPPM reduction programs, and annual supplier business reviews.'
}

  pcn_change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the change communicated in the PCN.']
  pcn_change_type varchar [note: 'Category of change described in the PCN.']
  pcn_customer_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the PCN must be communicated to customers.']
  pcn_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the PCN change becomes effective.']
  pcn_impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Summary of the impact of the PCN on downstream processes and customers.']
  pcn_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the PCN associated with this material certification, if applicable.']
  pcn_requalification_decision varchar [note: 'Decision on whether the material requires re‑qualification after the PCN.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating associated with the material based on certification and supplier data.']
  supplier_fk varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — Certifications are issued BY a specific supplier for their material lots.']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the compliance test associated with the certification.']
  test_units varchar [note: 'Units of measurement for the test value, such as ppm, mg/kg, or %.']
  test_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Quantitative result of the test (e.g., concentration, impurity level).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the certification record.']

  Note: 'Supplier-originated material compliance documents including quality certifications and product change notifications. Captures Certificate of Conformance (CoC), Certificate of Analysis (CoA), RoHS compliance declarations, REACH SVHC declarations, TSCA certifications, and Product Change Notifications (PCNs). For certifications: certification type, issuing supplier, material lot number, test results, compliance standard, issue date, expiry date, and document reference. For PCNs: PCN number, supplier, affected material or component, change type (process, material, site, EOL), change description, effective date, impact assessment, required customer notification obligation, and re-qualification decision. SSOT for all supplier-originated material compliance and change documentation within the supply domain. Required for regulatory compliance, incoming quality release, process stability, and customer PCN pass-through obligations.'
}

Table supply.supply_forecast {
  supply_forecast_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each supply forecast entry.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Forecasting controlled materials requires ECCN data for export license capacity planning and lead time modeling. Forecast-level ECCN tracking enables proactive license application and allocation plann']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Supply forecasts (wafer starts, OSAT capacity, material availability) are planned against IC products. S&OP processes, capacity allocation, and procurement planning require product-level forecast gran']
  primary_supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material (part number) for which the forecast is generated.']
  primary_supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the strategic supplier receiving the forecast.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Supply forecasts may be converted to or associated with actual purchase orders when the forecast period materializes. Linking supply_forecast to purchase_order enables forecast-to-PO conversion tracki']
  sourcing_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.sourcing_contract. Business justification: Supply forecasts are communicated to strategic suppliers as rolling purchase forecasts under specific sourcing contracts. Linking supply_forecast to sourcing_contract enables contract-level forecast v']
  supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.material_master.material_master_id — Forecasts specify quantities for specific materials.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — Purchase forecasts are communicated TO specific suppliers for capacity planning.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the material is subject to regulatory constraints (e.g., RoHS, REACH) during the forecast period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the forecast record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code representing the currency used for price‑related attributes.']
  forecast_source varchar [note: 'Name of the ERP or planning system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA MM) that produced the forecast record.']
  forecast_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of the forecast (e.g., draft, released to supplier, approved, or rejected).']
  forecast_type varchar [note: 'Specifies if the forecast quantity is a firm commitment (firm) or an indicative estimate (indicative).']
  horizon_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date covered by the forecast horizon.']
  horizon_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date covered by the forecast horizon.']
  is_ltb boolean [note: 'True if the forecast is used for planning a final purchase of an end‑of‑life material.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the forecast was last reviewed by supply‑chain planners.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned supplier lead time in calendar days for the material during the forecast horizon.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks, assumptions, or explanations related to the forecast.']
  period varchar [note: 'Defines whether the forecast is expressed per week or per month.']
  price_per_unit decimal(15,4) [note: 'Estimated cost per unit of material for the forecast horizon.']
  prior_forecast_quantity decimal(20,4) [note: 'Forecast quantity from the immediately preceding version, used for variance calculations.']
  quantity decimal(20,4) [note: 'Projected amount of the material to be supplied during the forecast horizon, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Indicates which regulatory regime applies to the material for the forecasted period.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk rating (0‑100) reflecting supply‑chain uncertainty for the forecasted material.']
  supply_material_fk varchar [note: 'FK to supply.material_master.material_master_id — Forecasts are for specific materials. Required to link forecast quantities to procurable items.']
  supply_supplier_fk varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — Forecasts are communicated to specific suppliers for capacity planning. Required for supplier collaboration.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit (e.g., pcs, kg) used for the forecast quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the forecast record.']
  variance_quantity decimal(20,4) [note: 'Numeric difference between the current forecast quantity and the quantity from the previous forecast version.']
  version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the forecast, incremented on each revision.']

  Note: 'Forward-looking supply demand forecasts communicated to strategic suppliers as rolling purchase forecasts (typically 12-52 week horizon) to enable supplier capacity planning and material pre-positioning. Captures forecast period, material, supplier, forecasted quantity by week/month, firm vs. indicative horizon split, forecast version, and variance from prior forecast. Supports supplier capacity reservation and LTB (Last Time Buy) planning for EOL materials.'
}

Table finance.cost_center {

  cost_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost center.']
  cost_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost center (e.g., fab, R&D, corporate, support).']
  cost_element varchar [note: 'Cost element code associated with the cost center for expense categorization.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the cost center operates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost center record was created in the source system.']
  currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for budgeting and reporting.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code of the department to which the cost center belongs.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center in external systems (e.g., legacy ERP).']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Depth level of the cost center within the organizational hierarchy.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'True if the cost center is currently active; false otherwise.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost center contains confidential financial information.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the cost center was last reviewed for relevance and accuracy.']
  natural_account varchar [note: 'General ledger natural account linked to the cost center.']

  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency of cost center reviews.']
  subdepartment_code varchar [note: 'Code of the sub‑department within the department.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code applicable to the cost center for tax reporting.']
  to_company_code varchar [note: 'FK to finance.company_code.company_code_id — Cost centers belong to a specific company code/controlling area. Required for organizational hierarchy and reporting boundaries.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cost center record.']
  validity_flag boolean [note: 'True if the cost center is within its validity period; false otherwise.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date when the cost center becomes effective.']
  valid_to date [note: 'Date when the cost center ceases to be effective (null if open‑ended).']

  Note: 'Authoritative master record for all SAP CO cost centers used in semiconductor operations. Captures fab cost centers (FEOL, BEOL, CMP, lithography), R&D cost centers, engineering support centers, and corporate overhead pools. Includes cost center hierarchy, responsible manager, controlling area, currency, validity periods, and budget allocation type. SSOT for cost center identity used across all financial postings.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry {
  journal_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the journal entry.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer involved in the transaction.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Journal entries are posted against cost center, profit center, internal order and WBS element; FK replaces ambiguous code fields.']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Export‑controlled sales require each journal entry to reference the export license authorizing the shipment for compliance reporting.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_wafer_lot. Business justification: WIP inventory valuation journal entries in semiconductor fabs reference specific wafer lots for lot-level cost traceability. Required for WIP accounting, lot cost variance analysis, scrap write-off jo']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Link journal entry to internal order for cost allocation and tracking.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Every SAP FI journal entry is posted within a company code that maps directly to a legal entity. For SOX-compliant financial reporting and intercompany reconciliation in semiconductor enterprises, jou']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Journal entries need a reliable link to profit center for reporting.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order. Business justification: Revenue journal entries must reference the originating sales order for audit trails and financial reporting.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor involved in the transaction.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Journal entries should reference the WBS element they belong to.']
  amount_base decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of the entry converted to the corporate group currency.']
  amount_local decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of the entry in the document\'s currency.']
  asset_number varchar [note: 'Fixed‑asset identifier for depreciation or acquisition postings.']
  business_area varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of profit centers for reporting.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Organizational unit identifier for legal entity reporting.']
  consolidation_group varchar [note: 'Group identifier for consolidation adjustments.']
  consolidation_method varchar [note: 'Method used for the consolidation entry.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the journal entry record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the document.']
  debit_credit_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the line is a debit or credit.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the journal entry document.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Category of the financial document (e.g., vendor invoice, intercompany).']
  exchange_rate decimal(15,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert document currency to group currency.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Two‑digit period within the fiscal year (01‑12).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year to which the entry belongs.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'Functional area code for internal reporting.']
  intercompany_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the entry is part of an intercompany transaction.']
  journal_entry_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the journal entry purpose.']
  ledger_account varchar [note: 'General Ledger account number associated with the entry.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after tax and adjustments.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the accounting period for the entry.']
  posting_key varchar [note: 'SAP posting key that determines debit/credit logic.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the journal entry.']
  posting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the entry was posted to the ledger.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the original entry being referenced.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'Date on which the reversal was posted.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the entry reverses a previous posting.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Market or product segment for analytical reporting.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component of the entry.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code determining tax calculation rules.']
  to_company_code varchar [note: 'FK to finance.company_code.company_code_id — Every journal entry is posted within a specific legal entity (company code). Required for legal entity reporting and consolidation.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the journal entry record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the journal entry record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the journal entry record.']

  Note: 'Core SAP FI general ledger journal entry capturing all financial postings across the semiconductor enterprise including consolidation adjustments. Document types include vendor invoice, customer payment, asset depreciation, accrual, intercompany, and consolidation entries (intercompany elimination, minority interest adjustment, currency translation adjustment, goodwill amortization). Captures posting date, fiscal period, company code, currency, exchange rate, consolidation unit/group (for group entries), consolidation method, and SOX-relevant reversal indicator. Covers fab cost postings, NRE revenue recognition, R&D capitalization entries, period-end accruals, and group-level consolidation adjustments for SOX-compliant financial reporting under US GAAP or IFRS. Primary transactional record and audit trail for all financial activity.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry_line {
  journal_entry_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the journal entry line record.']
  booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.booking. Business justification: Revenue recognition report requires each journal entry line to map to its originating sales booking for reconciliation.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center.cost_center_id — Cost center assignment on journal entry lines is required for management accounting, cost allocation, and P&L reporting by organizational unit.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.gl_account.gl_account_id — Every journal entry line posts to a GL account. This is the core of double-entry bookkeeping and is required for trial balance, financial statements, and all reporting.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Line‑level journal entries need the order line ID to allocate revenue and cost precisely per SKU.']
  maintenance_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.maintenance_event. Business justification: Financial postings for maintenance costs must reference the originating maintenance event to provide audit trail, enable cost reconciliation between finance and operations systems, and support varianc']
  primary_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent journal entry header to which this line belongs.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.profit_center.profit_center_id — Profit center assignment enables profitability analysis by business segment, technology node, and product line - core semiconductor finance requirement.']
  to_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.journal_entry.journal_entry_id — Fundamental header-line relationship. Every journal entry line must reference its parent journal entry document. This is the most critical FK in the entire domain - without it, line items are orphaned']
  wafer_start_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.wafer_start_authorization. Business justification: WSA-related costs (wafer starts, NRE charges) are posted to GL via journal entries; linking enables cost reconciliation and audit trail for capitalized vs. expensed wafer starts. Controllers reconcile']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.wbs_element']
  amount_document_currency decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the line expressed in the document\'s currency.']
  amount_local_currency decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the line converted to the company\'s local currency.']
  assignment_field varchar [note: 'User‑defined field for additional cost allocation or tagging.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the line record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the document currency.']
  debit_credit_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the line is a debit or credit.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the line amount becomes effective for reporting.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'Functional area classification for reporting (e.g., R&D, Manufacturing).']
  line_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the line item.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the line.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the line is posted to the ledger.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity associated with the line (e.g., number of units, hours).']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this line reverses a previous entry.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system that generated the journal entry line.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code determining tax treatment for the line.']
  to_journal_entry varchar [note: 'FK to finance.journal_entry.journal_entry_id — Header-line relationship is the most fundamental FK in any GL system. Every line item must reference its parent journal entry document. Without this, you cannot reconstruct a balanced journal entry.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the line record.']

  Note: 'Individual debit/credit line items within a SAP FI journal entry document. Each line captures GL account, cost center, profit center, WBS element, functional area, amount in document and local currency, tax code, and assignment field. Supports granular cost allocation to fab process steps, equipment depreciation lines, and NRE milestone revenue lines. Essential for drill-down financial analysis and SOX audit trails.'
}

Required for node-level capital inv']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: In SAP CO, capital expenditure requests are frequently linked to internal orders used to collect and track costs prior to capitalization as fixed assets. For semiconductor fab equipment purchases, an ']
  primary_capex_fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.fixed_asset.fixed_asset_id — Approved capex requests result in fixed asset creation. Links investment approval to the resulting asset for lifecycle tracking.']

Table finance.fixed_asset {

  fabrication_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_technology_node. Required for technology-node-level asset depreciati']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Semiconductor fab equipment (fixed assets) is capitalized from WBS elements representing capital projects.   work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.work_center. Business justification: Fixed assets are physically assigned to work centers in semiconductor fabs. Required for work-center-level asset tracking, maintenance scheduling integration, and depreciation allocation to the correc']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total depreciation recorded to date for the asset.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred to acquire the asset, including purchase price, freight, installation, and commissioning.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the asset was purchased or otherwise acquired.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level classification (e.g., Equipment, Facility, IT Infrastructure).']
  asset_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the asset (e.g., EUV Lithography Scanner 1).']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric tag or barcode assigned to the asset for physical identification.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Category of the asset such as Lithography Scanner, CVD Tool, CMP Equipment, Cleanroom Infrastructure, or IT Asset.']
  capitalized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset has been capitalized on the balance sheet (True) or remains expense (False).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the asset record.']

  depreciation_end_date date [note: 'Projected date when the asset will be fully depreciated or disposed.']

  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to calculate periodic depreciation.']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation calculations begin for the asset.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date the asset was removed from service and disposed of, sold, or transferred.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the asset was disposed.']
  disposal_proceeds decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary proceeds received from the disposal of the asset.']
  fixed_asset_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the asset.']
  grant_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the grant allocated to the asset.']
  grant_recognition_date date [note: 'Date on which the grant amount is recognized in financial statements.']
  grant_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for CHIPS Act or other government grant associated with the asset.']
  impairment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount by which the asset\'s carrying value is reduced due to impairment.']
  impairment_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether an impairment test has been performed and resulted in a loss.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent maintenance activity performed on the asset.']
  location_bay varchar [note: 'Specific bay or cleanroom area within the building housing the asset.']
  location_building varchar [note: 'Name or code of the building where the asset is installed.']
  maintenance_contract_number varchar [note: 'Contract identifier for the service agreement covering preventive maintenance.']
  net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Asset\'s carrying amount after subtracting accumulated depreciation.']
  next_maintenance_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled maintenance.']
  remaining_useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated remaining economic life of the asset, expressed in years.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department that owns or manages the asset.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the asset record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fixed asset record.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Planned economic life of the asset expressed in years for depreciation purposes.']
  utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average operational utilization of the asset expressed as a percentage of its capacity.']

  Note: 'SAP FI-AA fixed asset master and full depreciation lifecycle for semiconductor manufacturing equipment and infrastructure. Covers EUV/DUV lithography scanners, deposition systems (CVD/PVD/ALD), etch tools, CMP equipment, ATE platforms, cleanroom infrastructure, and IT assets. Master attributes include asset class, acquisition cost, capitalization date, useful life, depreciation key, location (fab building/bay), technology node assignment, and CHIPS Act grant tracking. Depreciation records capture each periodic posting: run date, fiscal period, depreciation area (book/tax/IFRS), depreciation amount, accumulated depreciation, net book value, and remaining useful life. Supports fab utilization-based depreciation adjustments, technology node retirement accounting, asset impairment testing, and disposal/transfer processing. SSOT for the complete asset register from acquisition through disposal including all depreciation schedules. Generated by SAP FI-AA depreciation run (AFAB).'
}

Supports fab utilization-based depreciation adjustments and technology node retirement accounting. Generated by SAP FI-AA depreciation run (AFAB).'
}

  Note: 'Work Breakdown Structure element master from SAP PS for semiconductor R&D and capital projects.   profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Cost allocation cycles in semiconductor fabs distribute overhead costs between profit centers as well as cost centers. The existing FKs cover sender and receiver cost centers, but profit center assign']
  tertiary_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center.cost_center_id — Cost allocation cycles have sender cost centers (overhead pools) that distribute costs. Without this FK, allocation cycles cannot identify their source.']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount allocated to the receiver cost center.']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the allocation record.']

  Note: 'Periodic cost allocation, assessment, and distribution cycle records for semiconductor fab overhead and shared service costs. Captures allocation cycle ID, sender cost center (fab overhead pool, shared services), receiver cost centers or orders, allocation method (activity-based using machine hours or wafer passes, statistical key figure, fixed percentage), allocation base quantity, allocated amount, fiscal period, and reversal indicator. Supports wafer cost modeling by distributing equipment depreciation, utilities, cleanroom maintenance, and indirect labor to process steps based on fab utilization rates and activity drivers. Enables accurate product costing and profitability analysis.'
}

Table finance.wafer_cost_model {
  wafer_cost_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each wafer cost model record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Wafer cost models are allocated to cost centers; replace code with FK.']
  fab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fab_facility. Business justification: Wafer cost models are specific to fab locations due to labor rates, overhead, and equipment differences. The existing fab_location plain attribute is a denormalization of fab_facility. Required for lo']
  fabrication_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_technology_node. Business justification: Wafer cost models are defined per technology node. The existing technology_node plain attribute is a denormalization. Required for node-specific cost modeling, technology node economics analysis, and ']
  process_flow_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing process flow associated with this cost model.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Wafer cost models are scoped to a profit center; the FK provides proper ownership and reporting.']
  chemicals_gases_cost_usd_per_wafer decimal(12,2) [note: 'Direct material cost for chemicals and gases consumed per wafer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost model record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values in the model.']
  die_size_um2 decimal(8,2) [note: 'Area of a single die on the wafer in square micrometres.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the cost model is retired or superseded; null if still active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the cost model becomes valid for planning and reporting.']
  equipment_depreciation_usd_per_wafer decimal(12,2) [note: 'Allocated equipment depreciation cost for each wafer pass.']
  fab_overhead_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Factory overhead rate applied to the wafer cost, expressed as a percent.']
  labor_hours_per_wafer decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total labor hours required to process a single wafer.']
  labor_rate_usd_per_hour decimal(8,2) [note: 'Hourly labor rate applied to wafer processing activities.']
  mask_set_amortization_wafers decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of wafers over which the mask set cost is amortized.']
  mask_set_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost of the photomask set required for the process flow, expressed in US dollars.']
  model_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used to reference the cost model in finance and planning systems.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the cost model (e.g., "7nm High‑Yield Model").']
  silicon_wafer_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cost of the raw silicon wafer per unit, in US dollars.']
  target_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned percentage of good dies per wafer used for cost allocation.']
  total_cost_per_wafer_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fully‑loaded cost of a wafer after aggregating all direct and indirect cost components.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cost model record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier for the cost model, incremented on each change.']
  wafer_cost_model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost model.']
  wafer_diameter_mm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Diameter of the silicon wafer in millimetres.']

  Note: 'Semiconductor-specific wafer cost model master defining the standard cost build-up for a wafer lot at a given technology node and process flow. Captures process node, wafer diameter, die size, target yield, mask set cost amortization, direct material cost (silicon wafer, chemicals, gases), direct labor rate, equipment depreciation per wafer pass, fab overhead rate, and fully-loaded cost per wafer out. Used for product pricing, NRE recovery planning, and fab utilization-based cost allocation. Unique to semiconductor manufacturing finance.'
}

Table finance.finance_nre_agreement {
  finance_nre_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the NRE agreement record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer party to whom the NRE agreement applies.']
  chips_act_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.chips_act_obligation. Business justification: NRE agreements can trigger CHIPS Act obligations; linking enables mandatory regulatory‑financial reporting of those obligations.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: NRE agreements are associated with a cost center; FK enforces this relationship.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IC design project associated with the NRE agreement.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: NRE agreements in semiconductor operations use SAP CO internal orders to collect and track the actual engineering costs incurred against the NRE contract. The internal order serves as the cost collect']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.legal_entity']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: NRE agreements generate revenue and costs that must be attributed to a profit center for internal profitability reporting. In semiconductor enterprises, NRE revenue from fabless customers is assigned ']
  sales_nre_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_nre_agreement. Business justification: Financial NRE agreement must reference the matching sales NRE agreement to align revenue recognition with the sales contract.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: NRE (Non-Recurring Engineering) cost recovery agreements in semiconductor are tracked against WBS elements for R&D capitalization and project cost management. The WBS element represents the specific d']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External contract number assigned to the NRE agreement.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Category of NRE work covered by the agreement.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the agreement was approved.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes or remarks about the NRE agreement.']
  contract_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the NRE contract document.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agreement record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the NRE amount.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the NRE agreement becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the NRE agreement ends, if applicable.']
  finance_nre_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the NRE agreement.']
  payment_milestone_schedule varchar [note: 'Serialized representation of payment milestones and amounts.']
  recovery_method varchar [note: 'Method used to recover NRE costs from the customer.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code representing the geographic region of the agreement.']
  revenue_recognition_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method for recognizing revenue from the NRE agreement.']
  tax_regime varchar [note: 'Tax treatment applicable to the NRE agreement.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for termination if the agreement status is terminated.']
  total_nre_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the NRE agreement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the agreement record.']

  Note: 'Non-Recurring Engineering cost recovery agreement master tracking NRE contracts with fabless customers for ASIC/SoC design services, mask set costs, and process qualification. Captures NRE type (mask NRE, design NRE, qualification NRE), total NRE amount, payment milestone schedule, recovery method (upfront, volume-based royalty, hybrid), customer, product design reference, and revenue recognition method per ASC 606/IFRS 15. SSOT for NRE financial obligations distinct from recurring product sales.'
}

Table finance.budget_plan {
  budget_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the budget plan record.']
  chips_act_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.chips_act_obligation. Business justification: Budget plans for CHIPS Act funded projects must allocate resources to meet specific compliance obligations (workforce targets, domestic content requirements, guardrail restrictions). Finance planning ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Budget plans are defined per cost center, profit center, and WBS element. Adding explicit FK columns replaces the string codes and enables proper joins for reporting and allocation.']
  fab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fab_facility. Business justification: Budget plans in semiconductor fabs are structured by facility for capacity and operational cost planning. Required for facility-level budget vs. actual reporting, fab capacity investment planning, and']
  fabrication_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_technology_node. Business justification: Budget plans are structured by technology node for R&D and production investment planning in semiconductor companies. Required for node-level budget tracking, technology roadmap financial planning, an']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Budget plans in SAP CO/FM are created at the GL account level to enable budget vs. actual variance analysis by account. In semiconductor financial planning, budget line items are mapped to specific GL']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Budget plans in SAP CO can be created at the internal order level for specific projects and cost collection objects. In semiconductor operations, budget plans for R&D projects, NRE activities, and fab']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Budget plans in semiconductor enterprises are prepared at the legal entity level for statutory reporting and intercompany planning. Each budget plan must be anchored to a specific legal entity to supp']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Budget plans belong to a profit center; the FK enables drill‑down of profit‑center level budgeting.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Budget plans are linked to a WBS element for project‑level budgeting; the FK replaces the free‑text WBS code.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the budget plan within the governance workflow.']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the budget (e.g., operating expense, capital expenditure, R&D investment, headcount).']
  chips_act_funding_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the budget includes funding that is aligned with the US CHIPS Act grant requirements.']
  compliance_regulation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the budget plan complies with applicable regulatory requirements (e.g., SOX, CHIPS Act).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the budget plan record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the local amount.']
  financial_reporting_standard varchar [note: 'Accounting framework used for the budget plan (e.g., IFRS, US‑GAAP).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit calendar year to which the budget plan applies.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the budget plan record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or explanations about the budget plan.']
  organization_unit_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the cost or profit center, division, or legal entity responsible for the budget.']
  plan_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the budget plan received final approval.']
  plan_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the budget period ends.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the budget plan, used for tracking and reporting.']
  plan_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the budget period begins.']
  planned_amount_group decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budgeted monetary amount expressed in the corporate group reporting currency.']
  planned_amount_local decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budgeted monetary amount expressed in the local operating currency.']
  planned_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Planned count or volume associated with the budget line (e.g., wafer starts, FTEs, machine hours).']
  planning_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to develop the budget (e.g., top‑down, bottom‑up, rolling forecast, zero‑based).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the planned quantity.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last updated the budget plan.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the budget plan record.']
  variance_to_prior_year_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between the planned amount and the actual amount of the prior fiscal year, expressed in currency.']
  variance_to_prior_year_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage difference between the planned amount and the prior year actual amount.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the plan to support revisions and audit trails.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who initially created the budget plan.']

  Note: 'Annual and multi-year financial budget plan with granular line-item detail for the semiconductor enterprise. Header captures planning version, fiscal year, budget type (operating expense, capital expenditure, R&D investment, headcount), organizational unit (cost center, profit center, company code), planning method, and approval status. Line items (owned within this product) specify GL account/cost element, cost center or WBS element, fiscal period distribution, planned quantity (wafer starts, headcount FTEs, machine hours), planned amount in local and group currency, and variance to prior year. Supports fab capacity-based opex planning, technology node R&D investment roadmaps, CHIPS Act grant-aligned capex budgets, rolling forecasts, and period-level budget vs. actual variance analysis. Enables drill-down from plan header to individual cost element allocations. Sourced from SAP BPC/CO planning cycles.'
}

Table finance.standard_cost {
  standard_cost_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each standard cost record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center.cost_center_id — Standard costs reference production cost centers for labor and overhead rate determination. Links cost model to organizational cost structure.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Cost models must incorporate export classification (ECCN) to allocate compliance‑related costs accurately.']
  fabrication_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_technology_node. Business justification: Standard costs are defined per technology node in semiconductor fabs. The existing technology_node plain attribute is a denormalization of fabrication_technology_node. Required for node-level standard']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Standard cost models are applied to order lines for margin calculation, variance analysis, and pricing decisions. Order management systems pull standard costs to compute gross margin at quote and orde']
  process_flow_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab process flow associated with this cost.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Standard cost records are defined per profit center; the FK enables cost‑to‑profit‑center mapping.']
  wafer_cost_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wafer_cost_model. Business justification: Standard cost for semiconductor products is derived from wafer cost models that define the cost build-up for a specific technology node and process flow. The standard_cost record represents the pre-de']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Rule used to allocate shared costs to the product.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost record received formal approval.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual who approved the cost record.']
  cost_basis varchar [note: 'Methodology used to value inventory for cost calculation.']
  cost_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Approach used to compute the standard cost (bottom‑up or top‑down).']
  cost_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the cost component.']
  cost_model_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the cost model methodology.']
  cost_per_good_die decimal(18,4) [note: 'Yield‑adjusted cost allocated to each functional die.']
  cost_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost record.']
  cost_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the cost is a standard, actual, or forecast figure.']
  cost_version varchar [note: 'Version label of the cost record, typically reflecting fiscal period or revision cycle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost record was initially created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency identifier for all monetary amounts.']
  die_size_um2 decimal(10,2) [note: 'Area of a single die on the wafer in square micrometers.']
  dies_per_wafer varchar [note: 'Number of dies that can be placed on a wafer before yield adjustment.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the standard cost becomes effective for accounting.']
  equipment_depreciation_per_wafer decimal(18,4) [note: 'Depreciation expense of fab equipment allocated to each wafer pass.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the standard cost is superseded or retired.']
  fab_overhead_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Rate for indirect fab costs (utilities, cleanroom, indirect labor) applied per wafer.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost record is currently active for costing.']
  labor_rate_per_hour decimal(12,4) [note: 'Hourly labor cost applied to wafer processing activities.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent cost review cycle.']
  mask_set_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Amortized cost of photomasks for the product run.']
  material_cost_per_wafer decimal(18,4) [note: 'Direct material expense (silicon, chemicals, gases) allocated per wafer.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or comments about the cost record.']
  packaging_cost_per_die decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost of packaging (die‑attach, encapsulation, testing) allocated per finished die.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'External part number or SKU used to identify the semiconductor product.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'High‑level family grouping of the semiconductor product.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'Specific product line within the family.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number for change tracking.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating ERP or PLM system that supplied the cost data (e.g., SAP CO‑PC).']
  target_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned percentage of good dies per wafer after processing.']
  total_wafer_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Sum of all cost components for a single wafer before yield adjustment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the cost record.']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any deviation from prior cost version.']
  wafer_diameter_mm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Diameter of the silicon wafer in millimeters.']

  Note: 'Standard cost master for semiconductor products defining the pre-determined cost build-up for wafers, dies, and packaged chips at each technology node and process flow. Encompasses full wafer cost modeling: process node, wafer diameter, die size, target yield, mask set cost amortization, direct materials (silicon wafers, chemicals, gases, photomasks), direct labor rate, machine cost (equipment depreciation per wafer pass, maintenance allocation), fab overhead rate (utilities, cleanroom, indirect labor), and fully-loaded cost per wafer out. Calculates yield-adjusted cost per good die and packaging cost for finished product costing. Used as the basis for inventory valuation (ASC 330), COGS determination, manufacturing variance analysis (price/usage/efficiency/yield), product pricing decisions, NRE recovery planning, and fab utilization-based cost allocation. Updated each fiscal year during standard cost roll with quarterly variance reviews. Sourced from SAP CO-PC product costing.'
}

Table finance.transfer_price {
  transfer_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the transfer price master record.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Transfer price agreements are typically managed at the profit‑center level; linking to profit_center provides proper ownership and reporting.']
  source_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Transfer pricing records govern intercompany pricing between legal entities in semiconductor enterprises (e.g., wafer pricing between fab subsidiary and design entity, IP royalty pricing between IP ho']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or code assigned to the intercompany pricing agreement.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pricing agreement method (e.g., cost-plus, comparable uncontrolled price, resale, transactional net margin method).']
  allocation_basis varchar [note: 'Basis on which the transfer price is allocated (e.g., volume, value).']
  approval_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or approver who authorized the agreement.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the transfer pricing agreement.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the agreement received its latest approval decision.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement complies with BEPS Action 13 and local tax regulations.']
  cost_component varchar [note: 'Cost element used in the price calculation (e.g., material, labor, overhead).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the transfer price record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the price amount.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the transfer pricing agreement becomes binding.']
  effective_price_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Calculated price per unit of product or service after applying the method and margin.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the transfer pricing agreement expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transfer price is subject to tax in the jurisdiction.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the agreement was last reviewed for compliance or market changes.']
  margin_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target profit margin percentage applied to the cost base, expressed as a percent.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next compliance or market review of the agreement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Supplementary remarks or comments related to the agreement.']
  oecd_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the OECD documentation supporting the transfer pricing methodology.']
  price_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary amount agreed for the transfer of the product or service.']
  price_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the transfer price for the agreement.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'Category of the transferred product (e.g., wafer, die, IP core, shared service).']
  service_category varchar [note: 'Category of the transferred service when applicable.']
  source_entity_code varchar [note: 'Internal code of the legal entity that provides the product or service for the transfer price.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system where the transfer price record is maintained.']
  target_entity_code varchar [note: 'Internal code of the legal entity that receives the product or service under the transfer price.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code indicating the tax jurisdiction governing the transaction.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statutory tax rate applied to the transfer price, expressed as a percent.']
  transfer_price_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose, scope, and key terms of the transfer pricing agreement.']
  transfer_price_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the transfer pricing agreement.']
  transfer_price_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the price applies to a product, service, or royalty arrangement.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the effective price is expressed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the transfer price record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier for change‑tracking of the agreement.']

  Note: 'Transfer pricing master records governing intercompany pricing of wafers, dies, IP royalties, and shared services between semiconductor group entities. Captures transfer pricing method (cost-plus, comparable uncontrolled price, resale price, TNMM), applicable entity pair, product or service category, price or margin rate, validity period, and OECD documentation reference. Supports tax-compliant intercompany transactions and country-by-country reporting under BEPS Action 13.'
}

  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the tax provision record for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Tax provision and deferred tax asset/liability tracking for the semiconductor enterprise. Captures current tax expense, deferred tax positions arising from temporary differences (accelerated depreciation on fab equipment, R&D credit carryforwards, CHIPS Act incentive tax treatment, unrealized intercompany profits), effective tax rate reconciliation, uncertain tax positions (ASC 740/IAS 12), and country-by-country tax allocation. Essential for semiconductor companies with global operations, significant R&D tax credits, and complex transfer pricing structures.'
}
